18.title: THE STRANGER IN THE BARN
prose: *South Road — Night*

The barn south of the ridge — the one the mess-hall rider mentioned, the one the refugees have been avoiding. You remember his words: someone sheltering there, civilian clothes, moves like military. You almost walked past it. You did not.

The barn was supposed to be empty. Night patrol sweep, routine, you have done a hundred of these. Your lantern finds them behind the hay bales: dark clothes, a wound managed for at least a day without proper materials, eyes that come up to meet yours with the calm of someone who has set fear aside for later as a matter of function. The clothes are civilian. The cuts where insignia were removed are still visible in the fabric.

boxes.text:
**COMPANION JOINS: COMMANDER SERATH (CONDITIONAL)**
Dragon Army defector, possible love interest. Joins ONLY if you hide them.
Role: Intelligence on Dragon Army movements and patrol schedules.

**ROLL REQUIRED:** Insight (+1) | WIS | DC 14
**SUCCESS (roll 14+):** They are telling the truth. The fear points south, not at you. Choose with full knowledge.
**FAILURE (roll 1-13):** You cannot read them. You decide blind.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

Your Divine Sense stirs unbidden. Serath is not fiend, undead, or celestial — they register as a person in genuine distress. The reading is clean. This is a human who is afraid. Mishakal, the god of mercy toward the wounded, acknowledges the moment. Mark 1 tally under MISHAKAL.

choices.text: Hide them. Treat the wound. Sort the implications later.
Ask them to prove the defection first.
Tell Aldric — this is a two-person decision.
Report them immediately.

26.title: THE SOUTH ROAD AMBUSH
prose: *South Road — Day 1 of Exercise*

The south road is pine-scented and cold and then the treeline erupts. Three miles from Brightholm — close enough that you could still, if you strained, hear the morning bell — four Baaz Draconians come out of the trees with the businesslike certainty of a unit that has done this before and expects it to work. They cut off the road. They advance. No parley. Aldric's hand is on his sword hilt. So is yours, you notice, before you consciously decided.

The lead draconian hisses something to the others. [LANG:Draconic|*The creature speaks in a hissing tongue you do not understand.*]'Baaz-thral vex. Solam-grish — ssilith-naar.' Kill the Solamnic dogs. Leave no witnesses.[/LANG]

boxes.text: In the half-second before the draconians move, you hear something — a voice, barely there, like a command shouted across a great distance. It is one word. You cannot quite catch it. But your hand is already on your sword and you feel no fear. Kiri-Jolith, the god who steadies soldiers before battle, has spoken. Mark 1 tally under KIRI-JOLITH.

**COMBAT ENCOUNTER — DRAGON ARMY SCOUT PATROL**
Setup: Four Baaz Draconians block the road. Aldric fights beside you.
**ENEMIES:**
• Baaz Draconian x4: AC 13, HP 18 each, Speed 30 ft.
• Shortsword +3, 1d6+1 piercing. Or Javelin +3, range 30/120 ft., 1d6+1.
• Death Throes: Turns to stone at 0 HP. DC 10 DEX save or restrained 1 min.
**YOUR STATS THIS FIGHT:** Fighter: HP 12, AC 16, Longsword +5 1d8+3. Second Wind available. Aldric: AC 15, HP 14, +1 to your rolls. Paladin: HP 10, AC 16, Longsword +5 1d8+3. Lay on Hands pool available. Divine Smite: expend a spell slot (at Level 2) for +2d8 radiant.
Enemy Tactics: Two draconians rush the closest target. Two hang back with javelins. Killing two causes the remaining two to spread rather than retreat.
Fleeing: Disengage + Dash action. DC 12 Athletics or one free attack as you run.
Rewards: 60 XP. Sealed dispatch pouch (inventory item).
Victory: Road clear. Aldric claps your shoulder. 'I did not expect that.' Proceed to Scene 27.
Fallen (0 HP): 0 HP. Aldric drags you clear and stabilises you. Proceed to Scene 27 with 1 HP, one exhaustion.

**BAAZ DRACONIAN (CR 1/2)** AC 13 | HP 18 | Speed 30 ft.
Attacks: Shortsword +3, 1d6+1 piercing. Javelin +3, range 30/120 ft., 1d6+1 piercing.
Special: Death Throes: Stone at 0 HP. Creature within 5 ft. DC 10 DEX save or restrained 1 min.

choices.text: The draconians are stone. Continue to Scene 27.

27.title: AFTER THE AMBUSH
prose: *South Road*

Four Baaz, stone now. The dispatch pouch from the patrol leader bears a Black Wing seal. This is an advance element. They were mapping the road to the garrison. Aldric looks at you with a new calculation in his eyes.

boxes.text:
**TRACK YOUR HP**
After the ambush: update your current HP on your sheet. Native PC starting HP: Fighter 12, Paladin 10. Each Baaz hit deals 1d6+1 (average 4). CPC: record any HP lost. Rest opportunity before Scene 27 if you need it.

**LEVEL UP — YOU ARE NOW LEVEL 2**
New Features: Fighter: Action Surge (extra action, 1/short rest). Choose Archetype: Champion (Improved Critical 19-20), Battle Master (4 Superiority Dice d8, 3 manoeuvres), or Eldritch Knight (2 cantrips, 2 1st-level spells, 2 spell slots). Paladin: Fighting Style (Dueling, Great Weapon, or Protection). Spellcasting: 2 first-level slots. Choose 2 spells from: Bless, Command, Cure Wounds, Detect Evil and Good, Divine Favour, Protection from Evil and Good. Divine Smite now available: on a hit, expend a spell slot to add +2d8 radiant damage.
Spell Slots / HP: Native PC — Fighter: HP +7 (total 19). Paladin: HP +6 (total 16). CPC: add your Level 2 HP roll (1d10+CON for Fighter, 1d8+CON for Paladin) to your sheet.
Record this on your character sheet before continuing.

**PALADIN — DIVINE TRACK**
Before making your choice below, Paladins turn to Scene P1 (The First Sign) now. It occurs this same night — you are on watch and something happens. After P1 and DG1, return here and choose your path forward.

**OPTIONAL SCENE — THE WAYPOINT FIRE**
The night after the ambush, on the road back: there is someone at the waypoint mile-marker who should not be there. If you want to investigate, turn to Scene 27B now. Scene 27B introduces Lysander Graves. It leads to Scene 36.

**DARK SCENE — SCENE 32B**
Scene 32B (What the Army Left Behind) is available now. It depicts what the Dragon Army did in the village of Thresh — you will find it before Scene 31. It leads to Scene 36. NOTE: Vael Ashmore appears in 32B.

choices.text: Turn to Scene 32B now — what the army left in Thresh.
Turn to Scene P1 now .
Take the dispatch immediately to Lord Caeron.
Read it yourself first.
Hide it and find the nearest refugee camp.
Investigate the stranger at the waypoint fire (Scene 27B)

28.title: WHAT CAERON LOOKS FOR
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Armoury*
'Someone who can hold two truths at once — that the law matters and that people matter more. Most can only hold one.'

He says it the way someone says a thing they have thought about very carefully and arrived at through experience rather than reading. You have the sense that this answer cost him something — that there was a version of Lord Caeron Ashvale who could only hold one truth at a time, and the version in front of you is the result of learning, painfully, to hold two.

boxes.text:
**ROMANCE MOMENT**
He meets your eyes with unusual directness. 'Ask me something harder.'

choices.text: 'What do you do when your orders conflict with your conscience?'
'What made you a knight rather than a lord?'
Thank him and leave.

31.title: THE HIGH JUSTICE ARRIVES
prose: *South Road Camp — Day 2*

He rides in with six escort soldiers and the expression of a man carrying something heavy that he did not choose to carry. Behind the escort, riding alone on a civilian horse with no insignia, there is a woman who is clearly not part of the party but has arrived with it — watching the camp with the unhurried assessment of someone cataloguing everything she sees. You file that away. The decree is formal — sealed, High Justice mark, legally binding and structurally unambiguous. He reads it out in the field camp: The refugee group is to be turned back south. Roads must remain clear for military movement. Crown law overrides field commander discretion. Thirty people. Families. Two wounded from yesterday. The Dragon Army, on a map, is approximately one day south of where they will be sent.

boxes.text:
**DRAGONLANCE CAMEO — LORD GUNTHAR UTH WISTAN**
The High Justice who arrives with the decree is Lord Gunthar uth Wistan, Grand Master of the Knights of Solamnia. He is a man of genuine honour caught between the law he serves and the world that law was written for, and they are no longer the same world.

**ROLL REQUIRED:** Persuasion (+0) | CHA | DC 15
**SUCCESS (roll 15+):** You find wording in the decree permitting a 24-hour field assessment delay. Caeron approves. The refugees have time..
**FAILURE (roll 1-14):** The decree is airtight. You must choose: comply or refuse.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

**OPTIONAL SCENE — THE OBSERVER**
A woman arrived with Lord Gunthar's escort who is not part of it. If you want to meet her, turn to Scene 31B (The Observer) now. Scene 31B introduces Tariel Duskmantle.

**PALADIN — DARK TEMPTATION TRACK**
Tonight, Takhisis speaks again. Before making your choice below, Paladins with the DG track active turn to Scene DG2 (The Offer) now.

choices.text: Comply. The decree is the law.
Ask Caeron privately if he believes it is right.
Refuse outright.
Use the dispatch pouch from Scene 27 as leverage.
Meet the diplomatic observer who arrived with the escort (Scene 31B)
Paladin: Takhisis speaks again (Scene DG2 — The Offer)

36.title: WOUND AND TRUST
prose: *South Road — Abandoned Barn, Night*

The barn smells of old hay and damp and the copper-salt note of a wound managed without proper materials. You work by lantern light. Serath watches you work with the careful attention of someone building a file on you in real time. They do not speak while you work. When it is done: 'You did not have to do that.' Not gratitude. Not manipulation. An honest observation.

boxes.text:
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Medicine (+1) | WIS | DC 12
**SUCCESS (roll 12+):** Clean technique. Serath watches your hands the whole time — not checking your work, just watching. 'You have done this before,' they say. It is not quite a question. When you finish, they flex the joint experimentally and nod once. By morning they will be mobile.
**FAILURE (roll 1-11):** Adequate work. The bandage holds, but Serath will move carefully for a day or two. They don't complain. They wouldn't.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

**ROMANCE MOMENT**
'You could still report me,' Serath says. 'I want you to know I understand that.' Not manipulation. Just honesty.

choices.text: 'I know. I am not going to.'
'I have not decided yet. But I treated the wound first.'
'Tell me something the Dragon Army is planning. For the people in its path.'

37.title: PROOF OF DEFECTION
prose: *Abandoned Barn*

Serath gives you the patrol schedules by drawing them — a grid in the dirt with a stick, the specific rotations that tell you where the Dragon Army pickets are and more importantly where the gaps are.

boxes.text:
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Investigation (INT, proficiency if trained) (+3) | INT | DC 12
**SUCCESS (roll 12+):** The patrol schedules are genuine. But there is something else: the description of Commander Hestrix's seal matches what is on the dispatch pouch from Scene 27 exactly. Serath knew about that pouch before you mentioned it.
**FAILURE (roll 1-11):** The information checks out.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

choices.text: Enough. Treat the wound and hide them.
Use the information but turn them in.

38.title: ALDRIC AND THE QUESTION
prose: *South Road*
He has been quiet since the barn. Now he asks: 'What do you want to do?' Not: what does protocol say. Not: what would Caeron decide. What do you want.

choices.text: 'Hide them.'
'Report them.'
'I honestly do not know.'

39.title: THE REPORT
prose: *Garrison*
You report Serath. They are detained. Lord Caeron commends your adherence to protocol. The paperwork is filed. The garrison moves on.

You think about the village they described. You think about the way they said it — flat precision, no performance of grief, worse than if they had been crying. You hear later they survived captivity and escaped three months after.

choices.text: You made the right call by the law.
It sits poorly.
A storm breaks on the road (Scene 40B)

44.title: THE REFUGEE COLUMN
prose: *South Road — Day of Exercise*

The waystation is the skeleton of a building — three walls standing, the fourth a pile of useful stone. Thirty-one people in a space meant for one night's passing have been here for three days. Three children. Eight elderly. A man with a barrel-stave splint.
When they see you, the able-bodied step forward and the children are moved to the back. Then the man with the splint tries to stand at attention when he sees the Solamnic tabard.

An elven woman near the back speaks quietly to her companion. [LANG:Elvish|*She speaks in flowing syllables you cannot follow.*]'Karas'thel anarani. The dragon shadow falls here too. These humans — will they help us, or turn us back?'[/LANG] Her companion shakes their head, watching you.

**IF VAEL IS IN YOUR PARTY:** Vael is already here. She scouted ahead and is standing very still at the edge of the column. 'I counted. Thirty-one. Three children. We have to do something.' Not a suggestion. A fact about who she is.

**IF SERA IS IN YOUR PARTY:** Sera has been scouting ahead. She gives you the tactical picture: patrol distances, terrain cover, the window you have. 'You have about four hours,' she says. 'Use them.' +1 to the Perception roll.

boxes.text:
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Perception (+2) | WIS | DC 13
**SUCCESS (roll 13+):** Scout still half a mile back. All options available without time pressure.
**FAILURE (roll 1-12):** Patrol is closer than you thought. Options B and C cost one extra round.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

**OPTIONAL SCENE — THE WAYSTATION**
There is someone inside who should not be here. Turn to Scene 18 (The Stranger in the Barn).

**OPTIONAL NIGHT SCENE — SCENE 34B**
If you met Sera in Scene 3B: turn to Scene 34B now.

choices.text: Report and wait for garrison orders.
Move them north yourself, now.
Set a defensive position. Hold scouts while they move.
Find Caeron first.
What the scouts found before the march (Scene 34B)
Explore the old ruins nearby (Scene 44B)
A boy in a blue coat by the road (Scene 45B — optional)
The Waystation — someone is inside who should not be here (Scene 18)

46.title: THE DECREE STANDS
prose: *South Road Camp*
The refugees are escorted south. The man with the splinted leg is helped by one of the escort soldiers who does not look at him while doing it. The children do not fully understand what is happening.

boxes.text: **Consequence:** If Mira is in your party, she leaves quietly that evening.

**FIGHTER — MEASURE SUBPLOT**
Fighters turn to Scene M2 (The Wrong Order) now.

choices.text: You obeyed the law. You will live with that.
You will never make that choice again.
Fighter: The Measure subplot continues (Scene M2 — The Wrong Order)

47.title: CAERON'S PRIVATE ANSWER
prose: *South Road Camp*

'No. I do not believe it is right.' He does not look at you when he says this. 'It is, however, the law. And I am bound by the law I swore to serve.' A pause. 'I am telling you this because I think you should know where I actually stand.'

boxes.text:
**ROMANCE MOMENT**
His eyes hold yours one second longer than the conversation requires.

choices.text: 'Then find a way to do both.'
'I respect the honesty.'
'What would you do if you were a squire right now?'

48.title: REFUSAL
prose: *South Road Camp*
You say the word. In the careful language of the Measure, clearly, to Lord Caeron's face: No. The refugees are not turned back.

boxes.text: **Consequence:** . Formal reprimand. The refugees live. Aldric looks at you differently.

choices.text: Accept the reprimand without argument.
Tell Caeron privately you would do it again.

49.title: THE 24-HOUR WINDOW
prose: *South Road Camp*
The delay buys a day. By the time the argument is over, the refugees have been given directions north. They move through the night.

choices.text: File the assessment and say nothing more.
Make sure Caeron knows exactly what you did and why.

50.title: SERATH IN THE MORNING
prose: *South Road Camp*
Just before dawn, Serath tells you three things about the Blue Wing's plans — specific things that can only come from someone recently inside the command structure.

boxes.text: **Intelligence Gained:** Dragon Army timeline on the north road is two days earlier than garrison estimates. The Blue Wing has orders to take Vogler before moving to Kalaman.

choices.text: Thank them formally.
Ask what they want in return. 'Nothing,' they say.

51.title: MOVING THE COLUMN
prose: *South Road*
You get everyone moving. Aldric takes the rear. You take point. Three miles north you hear the patrol behind you.

boxes.text:
**COMBAT ENCOUNTER — REARGUARD ACTION**
Hold 3 Baaz Draconians and 1 Human Corporal for 3 rounds while refugees move ahead.
**ENEMIES:**
• Baaz Draconian x3: AC 13, HP 18 each.
• Human Soldier (Corporal): AC 14, HP 16. Longsword +4 1d8+2. Tactical Command: one draconian gets +2 to its next attack.
Enemy Tactics: Corporal hangs back with crossbow. After 3 rounds a recall horn sounds.
Victory: The draconians fall back as the recall horn sounds. The column is clear. Twenty refugees owe their lives to the three rounds you held this line.
Rewards: 20 XP.
Fallen (0 HP): Column is safe. Aldric stabilised you. 1 HP, one exhaustion.

choices.text: Proceed to Scene 53.

52.title: THE DEFENSIVE POSITION
prose: *Waystation*
You organise the waystation into a defensible position. The patrol probes twice and pulls back.
*End of Act Two.*

boxes.text: **Outcome:** Refugees safe. You take 1d6 damage (roll or take 3)..

choices.text: Proceed to Scene 53.
Paladin: before continuing, turn to Scene P2.

53.title: THE NEWS ARRIVES
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Great Hall, Night*

Three bells at midnight — the signal everyone knows and hopes never to hear at this hour. The rider is at the back being given water. Lord Caeron is at the front with the dispatch. He reads it through twice. In the moment before he turns, before he chooses his expression, you see what is underneath it. Then he turns. 'The Dragon Army's Blue Wing has been confirmed south of Lake Akh-Tiel. Approximately two thousand. Moving north. Three days from Vogler. Possibly two.'

boxes.text:
**OPTIONAL ROLL:** Composure (WIS Save) (+1) | WIS | DC 11
**SUCCESS (roll 11+):** Your face stays neutral. Caeron holds your gaze a half-second longer than necessary — measuring, perhaps. Or acknowledging. With him, it is hard to tell the difference.
**FAILURE (roll 1-10):** Something crosses your expression. Caeron's eyes flick to it and away. He has filed it somewhere. Everything gets filed with him.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

choices.text: Go to your companions.
Stay for the full briefing.
Find Thessaly.
Find Aldric.
Something is wrong about the timing. Find out who knew first.
Find Tariel, if present.
Find Lysander, if present.
Mira tends to the wounded Commander (Scene 54B)

54.title: TENDING THE COMPANIONS
prose: *Castle Brightholm*
Aldric is in the armoury, re-checking equipment. Mira — if present — is doubling her medical supplies. Serath — if present — is watching the south road.

choices.text: Spend the time with Aldric.
Spend the time with Mira if present, otherwise train.
Sit with Serath if present.

55.title: THE EMPTY HALL
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Great Hall*

Everyone left. The hall emptied in the efficient way of a military institution responding to something real. You stayed. You are not entirely sure why — something in how he stood after everyone else left made going feel like the wrong thing to do. He is at the table now with the dispatch open again, reading it a third time or using it as somewhere to look. He hears you. Looks up. For one moment, before the composure reassembles, you see what is underneath the officer. He is thirty years old. He is afraid. He is very good at his job and that has not made this less frightening. 'You stayed,' he says. The two words carry at least three others.

You stay for a while. Not long — there is work to do and both of you know it. But a while. The dispatch is still open on the table. He does not close it. You do not ask him to. Some things you can only do if you do not make them into an act — if you just stand in the same room with someone who is carrying something heavy until they are ready to carry it into the next room.

boxes.text:
**ROMANCE MOMENT**
'I intend to conduct your Trials evaluation before we march — whatever comes after, you should have the thing you came here for.' He says this to his papers. You have never heard Caeron Ashvale ask for anything before.

choices.text: 'I do. Thank you, my lord.'
'What about you? What do you want?'
Stand beside him at the table. Look at the dispatch together. Say nothing.

56.title: THESSALY'S INFORMATION
prose: *Castle Brightholm*

Thessaly finds you in the map room an hour after the briefing. She has been busy since — ink on her hands, the look of someone who has been writing fast and stopping to think. She lays it out: the Dragon Army has a specific objective in Vogler. Not only the supply route. Something in the village — or someone. She has narrowed it to two possibilities and cannot narrow it further. 'I wanted you to know before we march,' she says. 'Because you are riding toward it whether you know what it is or not.'

boxes.text:
**OPTIONAL ROLL:** History (INT, proficiency if trained) (+3) | INT | DC 13
**SUCCESS (roll 13+):** Vogler is on Lake Akh-Tiel — the only navigable route between the southern Solamnic coast and the northern fortresses that does not require mountain passes. Whoever controls Vogler controls that supply line for the whole northern campaign. The Dragon Army does not need something in the village. They need the village itself.
**FAILURE (roll 1-12):** The strategic value is military. You cannot narrow it further.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

choices.text: Take this to Caeron immediately.
Keep it between you for now.
Stay. There is something else between you tonight.

57.title: ALDRIC IN THE ARMOURY
prose: *Castle Brightholm*

He is sharpening his sword for the third time tonight. You can tell it is the third time because the blade has the particular high shine of something worked past the point of usefulness by someone who needed something to do with their hands. He looks up when he hears you.

The armoury is quiet. Tallow candles, low. Somewhere down the corridor someone is walking fast and then stopping, then fast again — the sound of a garrison trying to be ready for something it cannot fully prepare for. After a while Aldric sets the whetstone down. 'Whatever Order claims you,' he says, 'I am glad you were here this year.'

boxes.text:
**ROMANCE MOMENT**
He hands you a leather wrist guard — his, slightly worn. 'Fits better than yours,' he says. He is not looking at you.

**CONDITIONAL — IF YOU READ SCENE 53B CHOICE B**
If in Scene 53B you chose option B (implicating Aldric): before your choice below, turn to Scene 58B (What Aldric Did) now.

choices.text: Take it. 'Thank you.'
'You will want it back when this is over.' 'Maybe,' he says.
If you implicated Aldric in Scene 53B: see what happened (Scene 58B)

59.title: WHAT CAERON CANNOT SAY
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Great Hall*

'I became a knight because the world requires people willing to stand in front of the bad things. I have been wondering for some time whether I am still that person.' A pause. 'You make me think that I might be.'

boxes.text:
**OPTIONAL ROLL:** Insight (WIS, proficiency if trained) (+1) | WIS | DC 15
**SUCCESS (roll 15+):** He said 'still' — present tense. This is not a past doubt: it is active. Lord Caeron Ashvale does not know if he has what this moment requires. He told you because he trusts you with it. ROMANCE FLAME Caeron activates immediately if you respond without trying to reassure him.
**FAILURE (roll 1-14):** He is speaking honestly but opaquely. The layer beneath the words is not clear.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

choices.text: 'You are.'
'Show me at Vogler.'
Paladin: something pulls you to the south field tonight. Turn to Scene P3 before Scene 60.

60.title: THE ACCELERATED TRIALS
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Great Hall, Dawn*
The day of the Trials begins before dawn. No ceremony — there is no time for it. But someone has lit the chapel candles anyway, all of them, and left them burning without explanation.

Stripped of the usual ritual, the day has a different weight — denser, more honest. Physical evaluation. Combat assessment, Aldric as your opponent. Written Measure examination. An oral panel: Caeron, Aldous, and two senior knights whose names you have never learned because they have been here for thirty years and no longer need names so much as presence.

**IF SERA IS IN YOUR PARTY:** Sera is not in the hall for the Trials — she is on the armoury roof again, where she was the first time you saw her. She watches the sparring bout from above. After, when you pass the armoury, there is a folded note on the step: 'Your footwork gap is fixed. Your guard on the right needs work. I will show you on the road. — S.' This is the most she has ever written to you.
**Final Oral Question:** 'Name the highest principle of the Solamnic Knighthood.'

boxes.text:
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Athletics (Sparring Bout) (+3) | STR | DC 15
**SUCCESS (roll 15+):** You disarm Aldric on the third pass. His blade clatters across the stones and for a moment there is silence. Then Aldric laughs — genuine, surprised — and the panel leans forward.
**FAILURE (roll 1-14):** Aldric takes the bout. His blade touches your chest a half-second before yours touches his. He nods once — respect for the attempt. The panel makes notes.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

**ROLL REQUIRED:** History / Religion (Written Measure Exam) (+0) | INT | DC 14
**SUCCESS (roll 14+):** Your responses are precise and occasionally surprising. Aldous underlines one answer twice.. (Paladins: your answer on divine duty is unexpectedly sophisticated.)
**FAILURE (roll 1-13):** Solid, correct, conventional..
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

**TONIGHT — BEFORE SCENE 61**
The Oath is tomorrow. Tonight is the last night before everything changes. • PALADINS on the DG track: turn to Scene DG3 (The Crown) now. • FIGHTERS on the Measure track: turn to Scene M3 (Two Oaths) now. M3 leads to M4, then return to Scene 62.

choices.text: 'Est Sularus oth Mithas. My honour is my life.'
'The protection of the innocent, from which all other laws derive.'
'Courage in the face of darkness, maintained even when the outcome is uncertain.'
Fighter: The Measure — Two Oaths (Scene M3)
Paladin on DG track: The Crown (Scene DG3)
(Paladin) ‘For the innocent. For those who cannot protect themselves.’
(Paladin) ‘For those who fight beside me. For the line that holds.’
(Paladin) ‘For the living things that have no voice in the decisions made about them.’
(Paladin) ‘For the possibility that the world can be better than it is.’
(Paladin) ‘I do not know yet. I am still becoming the person who will know.’

61.title: THE OATH OF SQUIREHOOD
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Chapel, Afternoon*

Caeron's sword is cold on your shoulder. The chapel smells of tallow and old stone and something you will later identify as the specific smell of commitment taking a permanent form. Aldric in the front row, jaw set. Thessaly with her notebook closed. Mira at the back if she is still here. Ser Aldous in the corner.
Caeron speaks the words of the Oath of Squirehood. When you rise, quiet enough for only you: 'Whatever Order claims you — you have earned this.'
*End of Act Three.*
You march south toward Vogler. Two more combat encounters await. Romance arcs reach their Oath moments. Paladins: Paladin Scene P4 — the Choosing — is in Act Four.

boxes.text:
**You Are Now a Squire**
Total your Order points. The highest score determines which Order claims your oath.

**LEVEL UP — YOU ARE NOW LEVEL 3**
Fighter Level 3: Full Archetype activates. Paladin Level 3: Sacred Oath (Devotion, Ancients, or Vengeance). Channel Divinity: 2 uses/long rest. Spell slots increase to 3 first-level.
Native PC — Fighter: HP +7 (total 26). Paladin: HP +6 (total 22).

**ITEM GIVEN — ALDOUS'S COMPASS**
A small brass compass, old, with a chip on the case. 'It has been wrong since 862,' he says. 'Points about twelve degrees west of north. I have used it my entire career and navigated correctly every time, because I knew to account for it.'

choices.text: (Fighter) Rest tonight. March at dawn.
(Fighter, Measure track) Seek out Aldous tonight — he said one more conversation.
(Paladin, DG track) Tonight, before rest, the voice comes again.
(Paladin) Rest tonight. March at dawn.
(Paladin) The Choosing — your god speaks.

62.title: THE MARCH
prose: *South Road — Day 1*

Brightholm disappears behind the first hill at the third mile. You watch the towers go until the hill takes them. Aldric rides beside you in the grey morning and says nothing, which is something — Aldric Voss does not ride in silence unless he is thinking something he has not yet decided whether to say.
After a long while: 'Whatever Order you got — I am not competing with you anymore.' He means: I will now be competing to be worthy of riding beside you, which is a different thing.

boxes.text:
**OPTIONAL ROLL:** Perception (+2) | WIS | DC 12
**SUCCESS (roll 12+):** Boot prints south of the verge — someone left the road in a hurry recently. Dragon Army standard issue, but the stride is wrong. Too short for a soldier in formation. Someone running. Someone alone.
**FAILURE (roll 1-11):** The road looks clear. Whatever passed this way left no trace you can read.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

choices.text: Laugh. 'That is the nicest thing you have said to me.'
'Understood. Same.' Comfortable silence.
'Race you to the ridge.'
Make camp on the hilltop. Tomorrow you will see Vogler.

27B.title: THE WAYPOINT FIRE
prose: *South Road — Night After the Ambush*
The dead draconians at the waypoints. The garrison rider's story at the mess table: someone is out there hunting them alone. You find out who at the mile-marker.

The waypoint mile-marker, which the garrison uses for overnight stops on extended patrols. A fire, built well — the economical kind that throws heat in one direction and minimal light in every other, the fire of someone who knows what sees fire in the dark. A man sitting against the marker stone, cleaning a blade. He looks up when you approach without alarm, which means he heard you a hundred yards ago and decided you were not a threat.

The blade is not regulation. Solamnic steel, yes — you recognise the fuller line — but the grip has been rewrapped in dark leather, and the crossguard has been modified. The armour, too: Solamnic issue, Knight of the Sword markings half-filed and then left, as if the filing was an impulse he reconsidered or an argument he abandoned.
There are two dead Baaz Draconians at the tree line. They are very recently dead. He did this alone.

'You are from Brightholm,' he says. Not a question. He has seen your tabard. 'Squires.' He looks at Aldric, then at you, the assessment of a professional cataloguing what he is working with. 'How many did you fight today?' You tell him. Four. He nods. 'I had two. They are getting sloppy or I am getting faster. Both are bad signs, for different reasons.'

'Sit,' he says. 'The road is clear for two miles and I have more food than one person needs. That is not generosity — it is logistical efficiency. I stole it from a Dragon Army supply drop an hour ago and it is going to spoil.'

He eats the way soldiers eat — fast, without tasting it, the habit of someone who has been eating between things for long enough that meals have become fuel rather than events. Between bites he tells you, without being asked, that he has been on the south road for three weeks. Alone. He has killed more draconians in that time than most patrols. He has reported none of it because there is no one to report it to, or rather there is, but the report would involve explaining why he is here and who sent him and the answer to both is: no one.
**CONVICTION CHOICE (Lysander Corruption Track — Experience 1):**
**ROMANCE CHOICE (optional — after conviction choice):**

boxes.text:
**COMPANION JOINS: LYSANDER GRAVES**
Knight of the Sword — technically. Currently operating as an independent. He is brilliant, charismatic, and carrying something he has not set down in years. Possible love interest — the dangerous kind.
**Role:** Combat instructor: once per Act, Lysander teaches you a technique. +1 to your next attack roll after receiving instruction.

**OPTIONAL ROLL:** Insight (WIS, proficiency if trained) (+1) | WIS | DC 12
**SUCCESS (roll 12+):** The half-filed insignia. He started removing his Order markings and stopped. Not because he changed his mind about leaving — his body language is clear: he has left. He stopped filing because erasing it felt dishonest. He earned it. He does not want to serve under it. He does not want to pretend he never wore it. This is a man for whom honesty is the last principle standing, and he is holding onto it with both hands.
**FAILURE (roll 1-11):** He is a Knight, or was. He fights well. That is all you can read in the firelight.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

choices.text: 'You fight like a Knight of the Sword.' 'I fight like a man who was one.'
'The half-filed insignia. You stopped.' 'I stopped.'
'You should report to the garrison. There is a chain of command.' He laughs — genuinely.
Say nothing. Share the fire.
There is something about the firelight and the competence and the half-filed insignia. You notice him differently than you notice other people.
You share the fire. It is what it is.

P1.title: THE FIRST SIGN
prose: *South Road — Night After the Ambush*

You are keeping watch after the ambush. The fire is low. Aldric is asleep. The night is ordinary in every way except one: the wound on your arm — a graze from a javelin you barely noticed during the fight — has stopped bleeding. You look at it in the firelight. The skin is closed. Not healed over days. Closed, as if it were a week old.
You did not do this. You do not have magic like this. Not yet. There is no spell, no prayer, nothing you can point to as a cause. Just: you were hurt, and now you are less hurt than you should be.
You sit with this for a while. Then you decide what it means:

boxes.text:
**PALADIN ONLY**
Fighters: skip to Scene 27 or wherever the story sends you next. Paladins: read this scene before continuing.

**DIVINE SIGN**
The wound is closed. The night is still. Somewhere very far away, something is paying attention to you. It does not feel like a threat. It does not feel like a gift either. It feels like: a gaze. Patient. Vast. Waiting to see what you do next.

choices.text: A sign of protection from a god of good and law — silver light, a guardian's gaze.** Mark 1 tally: PALADINE.
A sign from a god of battle — the warrior's body sustained so it can fight again.** Mark 1 tally: KIRI-JOLITH.
A sign from a god of healing — the body made whole by something that values wholeness.** Mark 1 tally: MISHAKAL.
A sign from a god of nature — living things mend; this is just that, applied.** Mark 1 tally: HABBAKUK.
You are not sure what it means. Mark nothing. Carry the question.

DG1.title: THE WHISPER IN THE WOUND
prose: *South Road — Night After the Ambush (Paladin Only)*

The wound is closed. That is what you know. What you do not know is what closed it. You are sitting alone at the edge of the firelight when you first hear it — not a voice exactly, more like the shape a voice leaves behind. Something that knows your name and the weight of your ambitions and has been waiting for a moment when you were alone and questioning.

boxes.text:
**PALADIN ONLY**
This scene occurs the same night as P1. Read P1 first. Then read this. Fighters: skip to Scene 28.

**DARK TEMPTATION — TAKHISIS WHISPERS**
You have been chosen. Not by your little gods of law and healing — by something older. Something that does not ask you to serve. That wound? I closed it. Not them. I saw what you could become and I preserved it. Your gods of good ask you to give. I offer you to take. The Dragon Armies will reshape this world. You could reshape them. One word is all it needs. One small yes.

choices.text: Refuse. Directly, clearly, with the Oath. You know what this is.
Refuse but you are shaken. The voice knew things.
Ask who is speaking. Not agreement — but you engage with it. (-1 Honour)
The wound is real. The help is real. You do not say yes — but you do not say no. (-1 Honour)

DG1_held.title: THE REFUSAL
prose: *South Road — The Same Night*

The voice does not argue. It simply goes. That is what tells you it was real — a voice inside your head would have argued. This one left clean, the way things leave when they have been told no by someone who means it. You sit with the silence for a while. It smells of cold pine and the road and something faintly metallic that might be the aftermath of a divine voice or might just be the night air near a weapon.

boxes.text:
**DG TRACK: HELD**
You have refused the first temptation cleanly. Mark HELD on your God Sign notes. This choice compounds — each refusal makes the next easier.

choices.text: Continue to Scene 28.

DG1_shaken.title: THE SHAKEN REFUSAL
prose: *South Road — The Same Night*

You said no. That matters. But it knew your name. It knew about the ambition you have not told anyone — the one that lives just under the dedication, the one that might be what actually drives you. You cannot sleep. Somewhere between watch changes you make yourself say the Oath aloud, quietly, to no one. Just to hear it in your own voice. Just to check it is still yours.

boxes.text:
**DG TRACK: HELD (SHAKEN)**
You refused, but the temptation landed. Reduce your highest god tally by 1 — the doubt is real. The Dark Queen will find the crack next time.

choices.text: Continue to Scene 28.

DG1_listen.title: THE FIRST STEP
prose: *South Road — The Same Night*

You asked. The voice told you: Someone who sees what you are capable of. Then it went quiet, not because you refused it but because it had planted what it wanted to plant. You will think about this in the morning. You will tell yourself you were just gathering information. You will almost believe it.

boxes.text:
**DG TRACK: LISTENING ■**
You engaged. This is not a fall — yet. But Takhisis does not need agreement. She needs attention. You have given her yours. Mark LISTENING. If you reach FALLEN by DG3 without recovery, your divine abilities are affected.

choices.text: Continue to Scene 28.

DG1_silent.title: THE SILENT COMPROMISE
prose: *South Road — The Same Night*

You did not say yes. But you let the silence be the answer, and the voice heard that silence the way it was listening for — not as refusal. In the morning the wound is still closed and you are still yourself and the world is still the same world. Almost.
*End of Act One.*

The field exercise has become something far more serious. The 'bandits' are refugees. The Dragon Army has a scout presence within two hours. Scenes 28-52 cover the refugee crisis, a major command decision, two more combat encounters, and the aftermath that defines your Order path. Paladins: Paladin Scene P2 is in Act Two.

boxes.text:
**DG TRACK: COMPROMISED ■■**
Silence was acceptance enough. Mark COMPROMISED. Your Lay on Hands pool is reduced by 2 HP until you find recovery. The connection to your true god has been partially muffled.

choices.text: Continue to Scene 28.

31B.title: THE OBSERVER
prose: *South Road Camp — Day of the High Justice's Arrival*

She arrives with Lord Gunthar's escort, though she is clearly not part of it — positioned at the rear, riding a civilian horse, wearing no insignia. Her credential, when Caeron examines it with the unhurried scepticism he brings to all documents, identifies her as a diplomatic observer from the eastern provinces, authorised by the Council of Whitestone to monitor Solamnic field operations during the current crisis. The credential is genuine. Caeron's expression when he hands it back suggests he wishes it were not.

Tariel Duskmantle dismounts with the easy competence of someone who has been in considerably more militarised situations than this and adjusted to each of them within the hour. She surveys the camp — not the defences, which she clearly assessed on approach, but the people. How they stand. How they respond to Gunthar's presence. How the squires look at the knights and the knights look at the decree and the decree looks at the refugees and no one is looking at the same thing.

Then she looks at you. Not past you to Aldric, or through you to Caeron. At you. With the particular attention of someone who has identified the interesting person in the room and intends to have a conversation with them whether they like it or not.
'The decree,' she says, when she finds you after the reading. No preamble. 'Thirty people sent south into the path of the army that displaced them. The Measure permits this?'
**CONVICTION CHOICE (Tariel Corruption Track — Debate 1):**
**ROMANCE CHOICE (optional — after conviction choice, if you chose A or C):**

boxes.text:
**COMPANION JOINS: TARIEL DUSKMANTLE**
Diplomatic observer. She is watching everything. She will share her observations if you engage with them — and her observations will make you uncomfortable.
**Role:** Political intelligence: +1 to any Persuasion or Insight check involving authority figures if you consult her first.

**OPTIONAL ROLL:** Persuasion (CHA) (+0) | CHA | DC 14
**SUCCESS (roll 14+):** Your response accounts for the tension between law and protection — not resolving it, but naming it honestly. Tariel tilts her head slightly. 'You actually thought about that,' she says. It sounds like the beginning of something.
**FAILURE (roll 1-13):** 'The standard answer,' she says, without contempt. 'It is not wrong. Just... well-rehearsed.'
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

choices.text: 'The Measure does not permit it. The decree does. There is a difference.'
'The Measure is imperfect. All law is imperfect. That does not make it worthless.'
'You are right. The Measure failed those people. I do not know how to reconcile that.'
'You are an observer. Observe.'
Hold her gaze a moment longer than the argument requires.
Do not.

DG2.title: THE OFFER
prose: *Field Camp — Night of the Refugee Decree (Paladin Only)*

Thirty people. The decree in one hand. The Measure in the other. Tomorrow you will have to choose — follow the law that is supposed to protect people but tonight is asking you to destroy them, or break it. You are still awake at the second watch when the voice returns. It sounds different this time. Patient. Almost kind.

boxes.text:
**PALADIN ONLY**
This scene triggers after Scene 31 (The High Justice Arrives), on the night before you must act on the decree. Fighters: skip to Scene 36.

**DARK TEMPTATION — TAKHISIS WHISPERS — THE SWEET OFFER**
You see the problem with your little code? The law is wrong and you know it and you have to obey anyway or break it and be punished. I am offering you a third path. Serve me — just this once — and those thirty people live. I will arrange it. No one will be punished. The decree will be satisfied. The refugees will disappear from the road in a way no one questions. All it costs is this: you carry my mark. Just a small one. No one will know but you.

choices.text: No. You will find your own way to protect them, inside the Measure or outside it. The answer is no.
No — but you ask how she would arrange it. Just information. (-1 Honour)
You ask for a day to think. (-1 Honour)
You accept. The refugees will be safe. That is what matters. (-3 Honour)

DG2_held.title: THE REFUSAL — SECOND TIME
prose: *Field Camp — The Same Night*

The voice goes. You are alone with the decree and thirty people and no clean answer. This is what the Measure does not account for — the moments when the law fails the people it was written to protect and you are the only person standing between them. You will figure it out yourself. That is the point of the oath. Not to be given the answer. To find it.

boxes.text:
**DG TRACK: HELD FIRM ✓**
Second refusal. Your connection to your god strengthens. +1 to your highest god tally (faith proved under pressure).

choices.text: Continue to Scene 36.

DG2_listen.title: THE QUESTION
prose: *Field Camp — The Same Night*

She tells you. Casually, as if the answer costs her nothing. The refugees would simply... find another road. One that leads somewhere safe. The decree would be satisfied by their absence from this road specifically. It is, technically, not wrong. You catch yourself thinking: that is actually clever. Then you catch yourself having thought that. You say no. But later.

boxes.text:
**DG TRACK: LISTENING DEEPENS ■**
You are reasoning with a dark god. This is dangerous. If already LISTENING: mark COMPROMISED. If first LISTENING: stay at LISTENING but -1 to any one god tally.

choices.text: Continue to Scene 36.

DG2_delay.title: THE DELAY
prose: *Field Camp — The Same Night*

She takes your pause as what it was — consideration. By morning the refugees are gone from the road. Quietly, without your involvement. The decree is technically satisfied. You did not agree. You just did not say no in time. That distinction matters. It also does not matter at all. Your Lay on Hands heals. The healing feels the same. You test it three times to be sure.

boxes.text:
**DG TRACK: COMPROMISED ■■**
She acted on your silence. The debt is recorded even if you didn't sign it. Your Lay on Hands pool is reduced by 3 HP. You are aware of a faint wrongness when you use it.

choices.text: Continue to Scene 36.

DG2_fall.title: THE FIRST FALL
prose: *Field Camp — The Same Night*

The refugees are safe. You told yourself that is what matters. You still believe this. But there is something different about the divine warmth in your hands now — not absent, not cold, but filtered. As if it is coming through something. The healing works. The prayers feel answered. But there is a quality to the answer that is new and you cannot quite name what the quality is.

boxes.text:
**DG TRACK: FALLEN ■■■**
You have accepted the Dark Queen's help with full awareness. This is recoverable — but only through Scene DG4 (The Reckoning). Until recovery: Lay on Hands heals half normal HP (round up). Divine Smite does full damage but leaves a faint shadow-bruise on targets. Your god tallies are frozen until you recover.

choices.text: Continue to Scene 36.

32B.title: WHAT THE ARMY LEFT BEHIND
prose: *South Road — Abandoned Village of Thresh, Day 2*

You smell it before you see it. Woodsmoke that has been burning too long. The village of Thresh is eight buildings around a well and it is mostly still standing, which is worse than if it had burned. What the Dragon Army did here did not require fire.

There are bodies. The Dragon Army did not take prisoners here — or they took some and left the rest as a message. The message is: this is what happens when you do not comply quickly enough. The village elder is still at his table. The door is still open. Someone's dog is still waiting outside a house whose occupants are not coming back. The well has been fouled. This was not a battle. This was an afternoon's work.

Someone has painted words on the well-house wall in black tar. [LANG:Nerakan|*The words are in a language you do not recognize — Common letters, but wrong.*]'Thax-gloreth. Draak-wing marches. Submit or burn.' The signature below is the five-headed dragon of Takhisis.[/LANG]
There is one survivor. An old woman sitting behind the mill, uninjured, who watched from there and who has not spoken since.

boxes.text:
**VAEL ASHMORE**
Vael Ashmore is already here. If you have not read Scene 5B, this is where you meet her. She was scouting ahead. She found this first. She has been standing here for a while.
**DARK SCENE — This scene contains depictions of war atrocities.**

**ROMANCE MOMENT**
Vael does not cry in front of you. Later — when you make camp and she thinks you are asleep — you hear her. It is brief and very quiet and when it stops she is silent for a long time. You do not tell her you heard. But in the morning you make sure you are the one who brings her water, and she takes it without looking at you, and that is also a kind of closeness.

**OPTIONAL ROLL:** Investigation (INT, proficiency if trained) (+3) | INT | DC 12
**SUCCESS (roll 12+):** Four days ago — but the supplies show three days of consumption after the army passed. Some people survived the initial visit and stayed or left afterward. The casualty count is lower than it first appears.
**FAILURE (roll 1-11):** The village shows you what happened. You cannot read more than the obvious.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

choices.text: Bury the dead before you move on.
Get the survivor moving north.
Find something to mark the place.
Report the coordinates to Caeron. Document it precisely. This is evidence.
Lysander wants to talk philosophy (Scene 36B)

34B.title: WHAT SCOUTS DO BEFORE A MARCH
prose: *Field Camp — Night Before Moving the Column*

The watch at midnight has its own texture: coals where the fire was, stars very bright, the camp breathing in the slow rhythm of people genuinely asleep. Sera finds you here not because she was assigned this post but because she walked the perimeter and here is where she ended up. She sits a foot away and does not speak for a while.

She has been doing reconnaissance on the south road and what she found she has already reported. What she has not reported is what it did to her. She has the flat stillness of someone who has been holding something at arm's length all day and is now, in the dark, letting themselves feel the weight.

boxes.text:
**ROMANCE MOMENT**
She does not ask for anything. She is simply — present. Near you. After a while her shoulder finds yours and stays there. That is all. It is not nothing.

choices.text: Stay still. Let her have the silence she came here for.
Ask quietly: 'What did you find out there?'
Cover her hand with yours for a moment. No words.

36B.title: LYSANDER'S PHILOSOPHY
prose: *South Road — Camp, Night (Conditional: Lysander in party)*

He finds you at the edge of the firelight. He is sitting on a rock with his modified sword across his knees and the stillness of someone who has been sitting like this for years and has stopped noticing the cold.
'Let me ask you something,' he says. 'The man with the barrel-stave splint. At the refugee column. You saw him try to stand at attention when he saw the tabard.' You nod. 'Why did he do that?'

'No,' he says. 'He did it because three hundred years of Solamnic presence taught civilians that the tabard means safety. And it did, once. Before the Cataclysm, before the Order turned inward, before the Measure became a text people quote instead of a thing people do.' He is not angry. That is the dangerous part. He is calm. 'The man stood at attention for a symbol. You — specifically you — chose to live up to it. The institution chose to send him south.'

'I am not saying the Oath is worthless,' he says. 'I am saying it is insufficient. I am saying a good person does not need it to do the right thing, and a bad person will not be stopped by it. I am saying the Measure is beautiful and I loved it and it told me to let people drown. I did not let them drown. The Measure told me I was wrong. Which of us do you believe?'
**CONVICTION CHOICE (Lysander Corruption Track — Experience 2):**
**ROMANCE CHOICE (optional — if at SPARK or higher):**

boxes.text:
**CONDITIONAL SCENE**
This scene occurs only if Lysander Graves is in your party.

choices.text: 'I believe you. And I believe the Measure. Both at the same time.'
'The Measure was wrong. You were right. But walking away means no one fixes it from inside.'
'You asked me which I believe — you or the Measure. I believe you.'
'The Oath exists because individual judgment is not enough.' He walks away.
'I do not know. I am a squire who has been at this for three weeks. But I am listening.'
The dark and the fire and the honesty have created something. Lean into it.
Keep the distance.

40B.title: THE STORM
prose: *South Road — Field Exercise, Day 2*

The storm comes from the west with the particular speed that storms have when they are not interested in your schedule. One hour of grey sky, then thirty minutes of wind that turns the treeline into a sound you have never heard trees make, then rain that is not rain so much as a quantity of water that has decided to be somewhere lower and does not care what is in the way.

Aldric has his back against a pine and his arms folded with the focused patience of someone who has decided that being wet is a temporary condition he refuses to acknowledge. Mira, if present, is already working — redistributing supplies with calm efficiency. Caeron stands in the rain without appearing to notice it.
**IF TARIEL IS IN YOUR PARTY:** 'The rain is useful — it reveals who adapts and who endures. Both are valuable. The Knights of Solamnia, in my experience, default to endure.'

**IF LYSANDER IS IN YOUR PARTY:** He appears from the treeline with dry deadwood gathered before the rain started. He builds a fire with offhand competence. 'The garrison teaches you to endure discomfort. I have found it more useful to eliminate discomfort and save the enduring for things that actually require it.'

boxes.text:
**OPTIONAL ROLL:** Survival (WIS) (+1) | WIS | DC 12
**SUCCESS (roll 12+):** This will pass in ninety minutes. The treeline ridge path will dry faster than the road.
**FAILURE (roll 1-11):** The storm is a storm. You endure it.

choices.text: Endure. Stand in the rain with Caeron.
Adapt. Help Mira redistribute supplies.
Build shelter.
Join Lysander at the fire.

44B.title: THE OLD RUINS
prose: *South Road — Between the Column and the March, Day 2*

There is a ruin on the ridge that the garrison maps mark as 'Solamnic watchtower (inactive).' The walls are chest-high. The archway is intact. Someone carved Est Sularus oth Mithas into the keystone at a date you can identify by the letter forms as pre-Cataclysmic.

Inside: grass growing through flagstones. A fire pit long cold. And carved into the inner wall at about knee height, a child's handprint in mud, dried hard. Someone sheltered here on the way north. Someone very young left their mark because that is what very young people do when they are in a place they will not come back to.

boxes.text: The Est Sularus oth Mithas carved in the keystone warms when you pass under the archway. Not the stone — the words themselves. The oath lives here, in cut stone and old intent. Mark 1 tally under PALADINE or KIRI-JOLITH.

**OPTIONAL ROLL:** History (INT) (+0) | INT | DC 13
**SUCCESS (roll 13+):** This was a Solamnic chapter house. Before the Cataclysm, one of hundreds. Now it is grass and stone and the handprint of a child.
**FAILURE (roll 1-12):** Old ruin. Old words.

choices.text: Touch the handprint.
Read the keystone aloud.
Make camp here for the night.
Explore the old ruins before returning.

M2.title: THE WRONG ORDER
prose: *Field Camp — During the Refugee Crisis (Fighter Only)*

You have seen the decree enforced. You have your choice on record. Now you have six hours before the column moves and Ser Aldous has found you at the camp perimeter. He is not here to debrief you. He looks, if anything, like he has been thinking very hard about something he should have said years ago.

'I want to ask you something,' Aldous says. 'Not as a senior officer. As a man who got this wrong once. What do you do when the Measure is clear and the order contradicts it?' He waits. He wants your answer, not the textbook one.

boxes.text:
**FIGHTER ONLY**
This scene occurs alongside Scene 46 (The Decree Stands). Paladins: you have the divine scenes instead — skip to Scene 47.

**THE MEASURE: THE MEASURE ON UNJUST ORDERS**
Est Sularus oth Mithas. My honour is my life. The Measure is explicit: a decree that requires acting against innocents is an improper decree and a Knight is not bound by it — but must be prepared to defend that judgment at tribunal. This is not the easy clause. It puts the burden of judgment on the individual Knight in the exact moment when the individual Knight is most afraid.

choices.text: 'You follow the Measure. That is the point of it. The order is wrong and the Measure says so.'
'You follow the order and argue at tribunal. The moment of conflict is not the time to make policy.'
'You try to find a path that satisfies both. The 24-hour delay, the technical argument. You use the law against itself.'

M2_measure.title: THE MEASURE IS CLEAR
prose: *Field Camp*

Aldous is quiet for a long time. Then: 'I got it wrong the first time. I followed the order. The village is not there anymore.' He looks at you. 'The Measure gave me the answer. I chose the safer path anyway. I have had a long time to understand why that was wrong. You worked it out in one conversation.' He stands. 'You will be a good knight. What matters now is making sure the good is worth the trouble it causes.'

boxes.text: **MEASURE TRACK: +3**

choices.text: Continue to Scene 47.

M2_order.title: DEFENSIBLE
prose: *Field Camp*

'Defensible,' Aldous says. 'Not right. Defensible.' He does not push further. He does not need to. You know the difference and he knows you know. The conversation ends there and you carry the word defensible with you for the rest of the exercise, testing things against it, checking whether what you are about to do is merely defensible or actually correct.

boxes.text: **MEASURE TRACK: +1**

choices.text: Continue to Scene 47.

M2_clever.title: USING THE LAW
prose: *Field Camp*

'Sometimes correct,' Aldous says. 'Know when it isn't.' He lets that sit. Then: 'The people who use the law against itself are necessary. They are also the ones who eventually find a case where the clever solution does not exist and they have to make the same choice you avoided making. Know which kind of case that was. Know it before you need to.'

boxes.text: **MEASURE TRACK: +2**

choices.text: Continue to Scene 47.

P2.title: THE CHAPEL VISION
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Old Chapel, Night*

You cannot sleep. The old chapel of the garrison is not the official chapel — the official one is clean and regularly used. This one is behind the armoury, half the roof gone, grass growing through the floor stones. The garrison uses it for storage now. But the altar is still there, uncovered, and the stone that was once the offering bowl is still the shape of a bowl.
You did not mean to come here. You were walking and ended up here. That is different from choosing.

You sit in front of the altar for a while. Nothing happens. Then you put your hand in the offering bowl — an absurd thing to do, and you do not know why you do it — and the stone is warm. Not the warmth of stone that has been sitting in an enclosed space. Warm like something living.

You speak aloud. You have not decided to do this — it simply happens. You say: 'Who are you?' No answer in words. But the warmth in your chest becomes, briefly, the specific warmth of something smiling. Then it fades to ordinary stone temperature and you are alone again.
You think about what kind of warmth that was:

The Dragon Army is confirmed within twenty miles. The formal Trials are accelerated. Scenes 53-70 cover the crisis, the condensed Trials, and the road south to Vogler. You gain Level 3 at Scene 61. Paladins: Paladin Scene P3 is in Act Three.

boxes.text:
**PALADIN ONLY**
This scene occurs the night before the formal Trials begin. Fighters: proceed directly to Scene 53 after Scene 51 or 52.

**DIVINE SIGN**
The warmth travels up your arm and settles behind your sternum. Not painful. Not frightening. It feels like: the moment before you speak the correct answer to something important. Certainty that has not yet found words. The stone is warm and the grass is still growing through the floor and the stars are visible through the broken roof and you understand, in the part of you below language, that you are not alone in this room.

choices.text: The warmth of law and order — a gentle judge's certainty, high and patient.** Mark 1 tally: PALADINE.
The warmth of a forge before battle — practical, purposeful, directed.** Mark 1 tally: KIRI-JOLITH.
The warmth of a healer's hands — soft, focused on what hurts.** Mark 1 tally: MISHAKAL.
The warmth of sunlight through trees — wide, un-directed, alive.** Mark 1 tally: HABBAKUK.
The warmth of music heard from another room — joyful, reaching, optimistic.** Mark 1 tally: BRANCHALA.
You cannot read it. The warmth was just warmth. Mark nothing.

53B.title: THE SOURCE
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Records Room, Night After the News*
Thessaly finds you an hour after Caeron's briefing. She has been in the records room and her face has the specific quality it gets when she has found something she did not want to find.

The Dragon Army knew the refugees were on the south road before the patrol found them. The dispatch pouch from the patrol — if you kept it — contains a reference code that matches a garrison communication cipher. The Blue Wing had intelligence on the garrison's movements. Current intelligence. Not intercepted — given.

Thessaly lays it out carefully: the timing, the cipher reference, the two officers who had access to that level of communication. One is dead — killed in the ambush on the south road. The other is Ser Aldous.

There is another possibility Thessaly offers, quietly: the cipher reference could have been planted to implicate Aldous. By the Dragon Army, if they knew his history with that village. If someone wanted a trusted garrison mentor removed. She does not know which is true. She has given you everything she has.

boxes.text:
**DARK SCENE — BETRAYAL**
Ser Aldous, who told you about the village he could not save. Who mentored you on the Measure. Thessaly's evidence is circumstantial but strong. What you do with this is yours to decide. Caeron does not know yet.

**ROLL REQUIRED:** Investigation (INT, proficiency if trained) (+3) | INT | DC 14
**SUCCESS (roll 14+):** The cipher reference uses a secondary field code, not the primary garrison cipher. A traitor using the primary code would be caught by standard security sweeps. Using the secondary requires counter-intelligence knowledge — this narrows the suspect list significantly and rules out the obvious names.
**FAILURE (roll 1-13):** The evidence is what Thessaly described. You cannot evaluate it further without more information.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

choices.text: Take the evidence to Lord Caeron immediately. Let the command structure decide.
Confront Aldous directly, alone, before going to Caeron. Give him the chance to explain.
Tell no one yet. Keep it between you and Thessaly and watch Aldous closely.
Destroy the evidence. Aldous has given you too much for this to be what it looks like.

53C.title: ALDOUS ALONE
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Chapel Steps, Night*

He is exactly where he always is late at night: the chapel steps, watching whatever hawk or star he watches when he cannot sleep. He sees you coming and he looks at what you are carrying — the papers — and something in his face shifts. Not guilt. Something more complicated.

He is quiet for a long time after you show him. Then: 'I did not give them information about the refugees. I would tell you if I had. I have been honest with you about worse things than this.' He looks at the cipher reference. 'I know who used this code. And I know who had reason to make it look like me.'

He gives you a name. A garrison officer you know slightly — someone whose brother is a Dragon Army officer. Someone who has been sending private correspondence south. Someone Aldous has been watching for three weeks and has not yet had enough evidence to bring to Caeron.

boxes.text:
**The Real Traitor**
Aldous was right. The officer is the source. If you went to Aldous first, you now have the actual name. You can bring this to Caeron with full context — but it means admitting you sat on Thessaly's evidence for a night before reporting it. Caeron will know. He will not say anything. He will remember.

choices.text: Take everything to Caeron immediately — Thessaly's evidence, Aldous's name, all of it.
Deal with the officer yourself, tonight, before Caeron is involved.

53D.title: TARIEL AFTER THE NEWS
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Map Room, Night (Conditional: Tariel in party)*

'Two thousand soldiers,' she says. 'Moving north. The garrison has — what — eighty? The decree sent thirty refugees south into their path. And the Order's response is to accelerate your Trials so you can ride to Vogler as a sworn squire rather than an unsworn one.' She looks at you. 'Does the title make your sword sharper?'
Then she opens the locket. A portrait. A young man in Dragon Army colours. Dark hair like hers. Her eyes.

'My brother,' she says. 'Aldren. He joined the Dragon Army because the Knights would not have him — wrong province, wrong politics, wrong family name. He tried to leave after Thresh. They do not let people leave. He is dead. Both institutions failed him. He was twenty-three.'
**CONVICTION CHOICE (Tariel Corruption Track — Debate 2):**
**ROMANCE CHOICE (optional — if at SPARK or higher):**

boxes.text:
**OPTIONAL ROLL:** Insight (+1) | WIS | DC 13
**SUCCESS (roll 13+):** The locket portrait is recent — within the year. The grief is not old. The diplomatic observer role is not a career choice. It is a response. She came here looking for someone to blame, and she has not found them yet. That makes her dangerous in ways that have nothing to do with politics.
**FAILURE (roll 1-12):** She is grieving. That much is clear. What she intends to do with that grief is not.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

choices.text: 'Neither system killed your brother. People did. The Measure exists to hold those people accountable.'
'I am sorry about Aldren. The Order should have been better. I intend to make it better.'
'You are right. Both systems failed. I do not know what replaces them but I know they are not enough.'
'The Order can be reformed. Your brother's death is an argument for reform, not abandonment.'
'I am sorry about Aldren.' Name him. Touch her hand.
Maintain professional distance.

53E.title: LYSANDER'S TRUTH
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Garrison Wall, Night (Conditional: Lysander in party)*
He tells you. The bridge. The refugees. The court-martial. The Knight who filed the court-martial was promoted to Commander.
He tells this story the way Aldous told his — flat, worn smooth. But where Aldous carries the weight as penance, Lysander carries it as evidence.
**CONVICTION CHOICE (Lysander Corruption Track — Experience 3):**
**ROMANCE CHOICE (optional — if at SPARK or higher):**

choices.text: 'File the appeal. I will speak on your behalf.'
'The system that punished you is broken. It needs people like you inside it.'
'They promoted the man who let people drown. I do not know how to defend that.'
'You did the right thing. The institution did the wrong thing. Both are true and I can hold both.'
'I want to believe the Order would have done differently if it knew.'
Put your hand on his arm.
Carry it with him through witness.

54B.title: MIRA TENDS SERATH
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Infirmary, Night (NPC Cross-Talk — Conditional: both Mira and Serath present)*
You pass the infirmary and the door is ajar. Inside: Mira is re-dressing Serath's wound. Her hands move with certainty. She does not look at Serath's face while she works.
Serath is watching Mira's hands. Then the assessment becomes something else. They are watching someone who is kind to them without asking for anything.
Mira: 'What you did — leaving the army. That took a different kind of courage.' Serath: 'You are the first person who called it courage.'

choices.text: Watch. Learn.
Enter. 'Is there anything you need?'

58B.title: WHAT ALDRIC DID
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Storeroom, Night Before Trials*
Aldric finds you before you find him. He looks like someone who has done something and cannot undo it and has not decided yet whether he should try.

He found the officer. The one Aldous named. He followed him to the storeroom where the private correspondence was hidden. And then — Aldric does not describe this part clearly, and you do not push him — something happened. The officer is alive. He is going to stay alive. He is also, currently, unconscious, bound, and in the storeroom, and Aldric hit him harder than he meant to and then harder than that and then stopped himself and he does not know what to do with what stopping himself felt like.
He looks at you. He is not asking for absolution. He is asking whether he is still the person he thought he was. He does not know yet. He wants you to help him figure it out.

boxes.text:
**CONDITIONAL DARK SCENE**
This scene occurs if Aldric is in your party AND you chose Option B in Scene 53B (watched the suspected traitor rather than reporting).

**MORAL WEIGHT**
Aldric did not kill him. He stopped. That matters. But he also crossed a line the Measure is very clear about. The officer is a traitor. Aldric's anger was not wrong. His method was. Both of these are true at the same time.

**OPTIONAL ROLL:** Medicine (WIS, proficiency if trained) (+1) | WIS | DC 12
**SUCCESS (roll 12+):** His knuckles are split and his breathing is slightly elevated — not from exertion but from the controlled effort of not shaking. He stopped himself mid-action. That kind of stop leaves a specific mark: not guilt about what he did but the physical memory of choosing not to continue. He is more in control of himself than he believes. Tell him this.
**FAILURE (roll 1-11):** He is in distress. The specific detail of his emotional state is not readable.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

choices.text: Report everything to Caeron — the officer, Aldric's actions, all of it. Aldric faces a formal reprimand. The traitor is imprisoned.
Report the officer. Say Aldric found him and subdued him in the line of duty. The rest is not said.
Tell Aldric: 'You stopped. That is the part that matters.' Then go wake Caeron together.

P3.title: THE TEST
prose: *Castle Brightholm — South Field, Night Before Trials*

The south field is empty and dark. You are here because something pulled you — the same wordless pull that brought you to the old chapel. You are getting used to following it. You are not sure this is wise.

There is a figure in the field. Not human — or not entirely. It has the general shape of a person standing very still in the darkness, but the details do not resolve cleanly. It does not threaten. It waits.
It speaks. One question. Simple. Unanswerable by anyone who has not thought about it very carefully:
The figure waits. It is genuinely asking. This is not a test with a wrong answer — it is a test to see if you have an answer at all, and whether it is yours.

boxes.text:
**PALADIN ONLY**
Fighters: proceed directly to Scene 60.

**DIVINE SIGN**
'You have power that most people in this age do not possess. It came to you through something vast and old that has been watching you since before you knew it was there. The question is not whether you will use it. You will. The question is: for whom?'

choices.text: 'For the innocent. For those who cannot protect themselves.'** Mark 1 tally: PALADINE or MISHAKAL .
'For those who fight beside me. For the line that holds.'** Mark 1 tally: KIRI-JOLITH.
'For the living things that have no voice in the decisions made about them.'** Mark 1 tally: HABBAKUK.
'For the possibility that the world can be better than it is.'** Mark 1 tally: BRANCHALA or PALADINE .
'I do not know yet. I am still becoming the person who will know.'** Mark nothing.

P3a.title: THE TEST — RESPONSE
prose: The figure is still for a long time. Then: 'Good. That answer holds.' It does not say whose answer it is. It does not need to. It was already there when you started speaking.
Award:. Proceed to Scene 60.

choices.text: Continue to the Trials.

P3b.title: THE TEST — RESPONSE
prose: The figure's stillness changes quality — something in it settles, like a weapon lowered rather than sheathed. 'That is the answer of a soldier. There is no higher one.' It steps back and is not there anymore.
Award:. Proceed to Scene 60.

choices.text: Continue to the Trials.

P3c.title: THE TEST — RESPONSE
prose: The figure turns its head as if listening to something you cannot hear. Then: 'There are more things alive in this world than have names. It is good to remember them.' Warmth. Brief. Gone.
Award: +1 ROSE . Proceed to Scene 60.

choices.text: Continue to the Trials.

P3d.title: THE TEST — RESPONSE
prose: The figure makes a sound that might be music if it were louder. 'That is the most difficult answer,' it says. 'It requires you to keep hoping.' A pause. 'Do not stop.'
Award:. Proceed to Scene 60.

choices.text: Continue to the Trials.

P3e.title: THE TEST — RESPONSE
prose: The figure is still for a long time. Then, quietly: 'Most people never ask the question. You asked it. That is enough for now.' It is gone when you blink.
Award: +1 ROSE . Proceed to Scene 60.

choices.text: Continue to the Trials.

DG3.title: THE CROWN
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Night Before the Oath (Paladin Only)*

You are alone in the old chapel. The official ceremony is tomorrow. You have been sitting here for an hour with your hands on your knees trying to remember why you started. The voice does not whisper tonight. It speaks clearly, quietly, with the tone of someone who believes they are offering something genuinely good.

This is the most sophisticated temptation yet. It is not asking you to be evil. It is asking you to believe the ends justify the means and that you are wise enough to navigate the result. It might even be true. That is what makes it the worst one.

boxes.text:
**PALADIN ONLY**
Only triggers if you are LISTENING, COMPROMISED, or FALLEN on the DG track. If you are HELD FIRM throughout: skip to Scene 61 — you will receive your gift there instead.

**DARK TEMPTATION — TAKHISIS SPEAKS PLAINLY**
Tomorrow they give you an Order. Rose, Sword, Crown — their little hierarchy of virtue. I offer you something they cannot. Not a rank. A purpose. The Dragon Armies will reshape this world. Every Solamnic Knight who rides against them will die for a structure that cannot hold. But a Knight who rides with me — a Knight who carries both edges — could change everything. Not a traitor. A bridge. You know how to hold two truths. I am asking you to hold this one: the light and the dark, working through the same hands.

choices.text: No. The oath is not a strategy. It is what you are.
You are tempted — but you pull back. You spend the night in prayer.
You accept. The bridge metaphor is genuinely compelling. (-3 Honour)

DG3_purified.title: THE PURIFICATION
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Old Chapel, Night Before the Oath*

You say no so clearly that the voice does not just leave — something cleanses. Whatever had been accumulating — the listening, the delay, the compromise — burns off in a single moment of absolute certainty. You know who you are. You know whose you are. Tomorrow the Oath will be the truest thing you have ever said.

boxes.text:
**DG TRACK: PURIFIED ✓✓**
All previous compromises cleared. Restore any reduced Lay on Hands pool. Restore frozen god tallies — add +1 to highest for this proof of faith. Proceed to DG_GIFT_PURE for your reward.

choices.text: Receive your gift.

DG3_resist.title: THE HARD RETREAT
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Old Chapel, Night Before the Oath*

You pull back. It costs you — not just sleep. The cost is the admission that you were closer than you want to admit. You spend the night in prayer with the specific discomfort of someone who has seen themselves clearly and does not entirely like the view. By morning you are steady. Not clean — steady. That distinction matters. Steady is enough.

boxes.text:
**DG TRACK: PULLED BACK ✓**
Improved state. Lay on Hands partially restored (+2 to pool). God tallies partially unfrozen. Proceed to DG_GIFT_SCARRED for your reward.

choices.text: Receive your gift.

DG3_fall.title: THE BRIDGE ACCEPTED
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Old Chapel, Night Before the Oath*

You have convinced yourself. That is the part that will haunt you later — not that you were deceived but that you did the work yourself. The reasoning was so good. In the morning you take the Oath with the others. The words come out. They feel true. They feel true through something. Through something that is not quite you anymore.

boxes.text:
**DG TRACK: DEEPLY FALLEN ■■■■**
The Oath is taken. Its power continues to function. The source is no longer what you believe it is. • Lay on Hands heals 1 HP per use until DG4 is resolved. • Divine Smite functions. The radiance is violet-tinged. • Proceed to DG4 — The Reckoning is your next scene on the road to Vogler.

choices.text: Continue to Scene 61.

DG_GIFT_PURE.title: THE GIFT OF STEADFAST FAITH
prose: *Castle Brightholm — After the Oath Ceremony*

After the ceremony, after everyone else has gone, you find something on the chapel step. It was not there when you arrived. There is no note. You do not need one. The warmth in it is unmistakeable — the same warmth from P1, the same quality, made solid and given form. Your god has been watching. Your god is pleased.

boxes.text:
**✦ MAGIC ITEM GAINED: THE UNBROKEN VOW**
*Wondrous Item (Requires Attunement by a Paladin), Uncommon*
A silver disc engraved with the words EST SULARUS OTH MITHAS on one side and a five-pointed star on the other. It is warm to the touch and has been since you found it.
**Mechanics:** While attuned: your Lay on Hands pool increases by 5 HP. Once per long rest: when you or an ally within 10 ft. drops to 0 HP, you may use your reaction to immediately stabilise them and grant them 1 HP. Additionally, you have advantage on saving throws against fear effects.
**Lore:** Given to a paladin who was tempted and did not bend.

**✦ MAGIC ITEM GAINED: OATH-KEEPER**
*Weapon (Longsword), Requires Attunement by a Paladin, Rare*
A longsword with a blade of white steel and a crossguard shaped like two wings folded in rest. The grip is wrapped in white leather that does not soil. The blade catches light and holds it a moment longer than it should.
**Mechanics:** +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls. When you hit a fiend or undead, the target takes an extra 1d6 radiant damage. Once per long rest: on a hit, you may choose to maximise the damage rather than rolling. The sword does not glow on command — it glows when it judges the moment worthy.
**Lore:** Forged in spirit and given form by an act of unbroken faith. In the main campaign, it will glow in the presence of Dragon Army officers who are genuinely conflicted about their service.

**✦ MAGIC ITEM GAINED: THE VIGIL CLOAK**
*Wondrous Item (Cloak), Requires Attunement by a Paladin or Knight, Rare*
A deep blue-grey cloak with a silver clasp shaped like the Solamnic sun. It is heavier than it looks and does not blow in wind.
**Mechanics:** +1 to AC. Advantage on saving throws against being magically moved or knocked prone.
