Once per long rest: when an ally within 30 ft. is targeted by an attack, you may use your reaction to impose disadvantage on that attack roll. Cannot be charmed or frightened while in direct sunlight.
**Lore:** Given to a paladin who stood where they were supposed to stand and did not move.

choices.text: Continue to Scene 62.

DG_GIFT_SCARRED.title: THE GIFT OF THE SCARRED FLAME
prose: *Castle Brightholm or Vogler Road — After Pulling Back*
You do not find it placed with ceremony. It is simply in your hand at some point after — after the hard night of prayer, after the slow return. You are not entirely sure when it arrived.

boxes.text:
**✦ MAGIC ITEM GAINED: THE SCARRED FLAME**
*Wondrous Item (Requires Attunement by a Paladin), Uncommon*
A pendant shaped like a flame with a crack through it, sealed with silver. The gold and violet in the metalwork are both deliberately visible — not hidden, acknowledged.
**Mechanics:** Once per long rest: when you fail a saving throw against a charm or compulsion effect, you may reroll it with advantage. Proficiency in Insight checks when determining if a creature is deceiving you or acting under magical influence. Your Lay on Hands pool is fully restored.
**Lore:** Given to a paladin who was tempted, bent toward the fall, and pulled back. The scar is not hidden because the gods of good do not hide their histories.

choices.text: Proceed to Scene 62.

M3.title: TWO OATHS
prose: *Castle Brightholm — Night of the Oath Ceremony (Fighter Only)*

After the ceremony, after Caeron's sword and the words and Aldous in the corner, you find yourself standing outside the chapel with a very specific question you did not have before you took the oath. The oath says: honour the innocent, obey your commanding officer, serve the Order. But you have now seen, in three weeks of field exercise, two cases where those clauses are in direct conflict. Aldous finds you. Of course he does. 'You have questions,' he says. Not a question.

boxes.text:
**FIGHTER ONLY**
This scene occurs on the night of Scene 61, after the ceremony. Paladins skip to Scene 62.

**THE MEASURE: THE MEASURE ON CONFLICTING OBLIGATIONS**
The Oath has three pillars: Rose (justice, compassion, wisdom), Sword (courage, battle, honour), Crown (law, loyalty, duty). The Measure does not rank them. This is intentional. It places the burden of adjudication on the Knight who must live with both the choice and its consequences.

choices.text: 'When the three conflict, Rose takes precedence. A law that creates injustice has already broken the Oath.'
'When the three conflict, Sword takes precedence — the person who holds the line protects the others.'
'When the three conflict, Crown takes precedence — the structure must hold or nothing else can.'
'I do not know yet. I think the answer is situational.'

M3_rose.title: THE ROSE PATH
prose: *Chapel Yard*

Aldous looks at you steadily. 'The Rose Knights have always said that. Most of them are right. Some of them have used it to excuse breaking every rule they found inconvenient. The difference is whether you actually know what justice requires or whether you just think you do.' A pause. 'You do. So far.'

boxes.text: **MEASURE TRACK: ROSE orientation.**

choices.text: Continue to Scene M4.

M3_sword.title: THE SWORD PATH
prose: *Chapel Yard*

Aldous considers this. 'The Sword Knights have always said that too. And they are right: the person who does not hold their post creates the hole that everything else falls through. The question is what you do when holding your post requires injustice.' He looks at you. 'You have an answer. I can tell.'

boxes.text: **MEASURE TRACK: SWORD orientation.**

choices.text: Continue to Scene M4.

M3_crown.title: THE CROWN PATH
prose: *Chapel Yard*

'The Crown Knights,' Aldous says. 'The institution has to survive for the ideal to survive — that argument has defended both the best decisions and the worst ones in Solamnic history. The difference is who is doing the reforming and from what motive.' He is quiet. 'You have the motive right. Keep it.'

boxes.text: **MEASURE TRACK: CROWN orientation.**

choices.text: Continue to Scene M4.

M3_balance.title: THE BALANCE PATH
prose: *Chapel Yard*

Aldous is quiet for a long time. Then: 'That is the answer the Measure itself gives, if you read it carefully. The lack of ranking is intentional. It is asking every Knight to do the hardest thing: think. Every time. Even when you are tired.' He nods. 'Harder path. But a real one.'

boxes.text: **MEASURE TRACK: BALANCE orientation.**

choices.text: Continue to Scene M4.

P4.title: THE CHOOSING
prose: *Forest Road — Dusk, Night Before Vogler*

You are on watch. The camp is quiet behind you. The road south is dark. Tomorrow you reach Vogler. The presence is here tonight. Not behind you — not anywhere you can point at. It is simply present, the way a colour is present when you open your eyes. You have felt it before. It has been patient. It is still patient. But tonight you understand, in the way you understand things below language, that it has brought you this far and will not push you further. Whatever comes next is your decision.
Count your tallies:
Now choose:

boxes.text:
**PALADIN ONLY — THE MOST IMPORTANT SCENE**
Count your God Sign tallies now. The god with the most tallies has been reaching for you throughout this journey. Fighters: skip ahead to Scene 70.

**DIVINE SIGN**
The forest is still. The stars are out — Solinari rising, Lunitari somewhere behind the clouds, and in the space between them, if you look in the right way, a darkness that is not empty: Nuitari, the Black Moon. All three moons moving through their courses. And below them, somewhere very far down, the presence. Patient. Waiting. Still not asking.

choices.text: Speak to Paladine. 'I am yours, if you will have me. I will guard what is good. I will not look away.'
Speak to Kiri-Jolith. 'I will fight. I will not waste what you give me. The line will hold while I stand.'
Speak to Mishakal. 'I will heal. I will not choose who deserves it. I will be where the hurt is.'
Speak to Habbakuk. 'I will protect what cannot protect itself. I will not ask permission first.'
Speak to Branchala. 'I will hold onto the possibility of beauty in the middle of this. I will not let the war take everything.'
Remain silent. 'I am not ready to name you. But I will not pretend you are not here either.'
The tallies do not match how you feel. Choose the god who calls to you most regardless of the count.

P4_Paladine.title: PALADINE RESPONDS
prose: *Platinum Dragon, The Valiant Warrior, Father of Good*

Something vast and silver acknowledges you. Not with words — with the feeling of a great weight settling, not on you but around you, as if the space you occupy has been noted and found to matter. The silence after is profound.
**Mechanics:** Devotion Oath (recommended). Sacred Weapon grants your longsword +CHA to attacks. Channel Divinity: Turn the Unholy.. Advantage on CHA saves for the remainder of this adventure.

**In the Main Campaign:** Paladine was worshipped as the highest of the gods of good. His chosen were judges and guardians. His divine spark in you is a candle against a coming darkness — and candles matter.
Proceed to Scene 70.

boxes.text:
**DRAGONLANCE CAMEO — AN OLD MAN WITH A POINTY HAT**
There is an old man sitting on a tree root nearby. He was not there before. He has a long beard, a battered pointy hat, and the general air of someone who wandered in by mistake and is enjoying the view. He looks at you with kind, gold-flecked eyes. 'Ah,' he says. 'Good. Very good.' He sounds pleased in the way someone sounds pleased when a thing they have been hoping for finally happens. When you look back he is gone. Fizban is, depending on who you ask, a befuddled old wizard, a Hero of the Lance, or Paladine himself walking the world in mortal guise. These three things are not mutually exclusive.

choices.text: Continue to Vogler.

P4_KiriJolith.title: KIRI-JOLITH RESPONDS
prose: *The Bison of Heaven, God of Just War*

Something strikes. That is the only word for it — not painful but percussive, like a hammer on an anvil very close to you. A sound and a sensation at once. Then silence. Then the smell of steel and something that might be blood but might be rain.

**Mechanics:** Vengeance Oath (recommended) or Devotion. Vow of Enmity: choose one enemy per fight to gain Advantage against for 1 minute. Abjure Enemy: one creature within 60 ft. must make WIS DC 13 save or be frightened and halved in speed for 1 minute..

**In the Main Campaign:** Kiri-Jolith has always chosen those who fight not for glory but for the person beside them. His paladins will find that fighting to give someone else a chance to live is its own kind of prayer.
Proceed to Scene 70.

choices.text: Continue to Vogler.

P4_Mishakal.title: MISHAKAL RESPONDS
prose: *The Blue Lady, Goddess of Healing and Compassion*

Warmth. Simple and enormous. Your hands feel it first — both palms, spreading up your arms and settling in your chest. It is the warmth of something that is pleased with you. Not proud. Pleased. Like coming home.

**Mechanics:** Devotion Oath. Sacred Weapon. Channel Divinity: Turn the Unholy. Bonus: Lay on Hands pool increases to 15 HP per long rest. Cure Wounds spell added to your spells known (no slot required, 1/day)..

**In the Main Campaign:** Mishakal's return is the most significant spiritual event in Krynn's history since the Cataclysm. Her chosen carry actual divine healing in a world that has forgotten what that feels like. The healing you provide will be understood by NPCs as a miracle. Because it is.
Proceed to Scene 70.

choices.text: Continue to Vogler.

P4_Habbakuk.title: HABBAKUK RESPONDS
prose: *The Fisher King, God of Nature, Sea, and Animal Life*

The forest moves. Not like wind — like attention. The trees around you shift their weight fractionally. Somewhere close, an owl lands and watches you with complete stillness. A fox crosses the road twenty feet ahead and sits down and looks at you and then moves on.

**Mechanics:** Ancients Oath (recommended). Nature's Wrath: restrain one creature with spectral vines (STR/DEX DC 13, concentration 1 min). Turn the Faithless: fey and fiends within 30 ft. must make WIS DC 13 save or be turned for 1 min.. Animals in the main campaign will not flee from you.

**In the Main Campaign:** Habbakuk is the god of living things that do not have a vote in what happens to their world. His paladins will find that Krynn's natural world responds to them — animals bring warnings, weather shifts, and occasionally something very old in a forest recognises you.
Proceed to Scene 70.

choices.text: Continue to Vogler.

P4_Branchala.title: BRANCHALA RESPONDS
prose: *The Song of Life, God of Beauty, Art, and Inspiration*

You hear music. Not in your ears — in the space behind your eyes. Something that is not quite a song and not quite a feeling and is exactly what you would call the reason not to give up. It lasts three seconds. It is enough.

**Mechanics:** Devotion or Ancients Oath. Channel Divinity: Sacred Weapon. Bonus: once per long rest, you may use your Channel Divinity to grant an ally advantage on their next ability check or attack roll..

**In the Main Campaign:** Branchala is the god of the unreasonable belief that beauty and hope survive. His paladins carry something the Dragon Army cannot easily destroy: the ability to make people feel, briefly, that it will be all right. This turns out to matter enormously.
Proceed to Scene 70.

choices.text: Continue to Vogler.

P4_Unnamed.title: THE UNNAMED PRESENCE
prose: *The God Who Did Not Name Themselves*

The presence acknowledges your silence the way a patient teacher acknowledges a student who says: I do not understand yet. Not disappointment. Something that is almost approval. The between-spaces are where most thinking actually happens.
**Mechanics:** Choose any Sacred Oath at Level 3. Bonus: once per long rest, you may roll twice on any Religion or History check and take the better result..

**In the Main Campaign:** The unnamed presence has been patient for three hundred years. Your god's identity will become clearer through your actions rather than through declaration. What you do in Vogler will matter more than what you said tonight.
Proceed to Scene 70.

choices.text: Continue to Vogler.

M4.title: THE VOGLER QUESTION
prose: *Vogler Road — Day Before the City (Fighter Only)*

Ser Aldous finds you at the edge of the last camp. He has the old book again — the cracked binding one, not the official Measure. He sets it down beside you. 'One more question,' he says. 'Tomorrow you ride into Vogler. You will be a newly-oathed squire of the Knights of Solamnia carrying the Measure you have been learning for three weeks. Someone in that village will need you to know what it means. What do you know?'

boxes.text:
**FIGHTER ONLY — MEASURE SUBPLOT CONCLUSION**
Count your Measure Track score: 3-4 = Scholar, 5-7 = Practitioner, 8+ = Keeper. This determines your gift.

choices.text: 'I know the Measure is a tool for protecting people. Not a shield for the person holding it. If I have to choose between the law and the person, the law is there to serve the person.'
'I know the Measure requires me to make judgment calls I cannot undo. And that the right call is not always the comfortable one.'
'I know I do not know everything yet. But I know enough to hold the line until I do.'

M4_keeper.title: THE KEEPER'S GIFT
prose: *The Last Camp*

He nods slowly. Not just at the answer — at the person saying it. 'That is what the Measure says,' he tells you. 'Not in those words. But that is what it says.' He hands you the old book. 'Not a copy. The original. My copy, the one I have carried for forty years. I do not need it anymore. I have it memorised.' He looks at you. 'You have what matters. Go use it.'

boxes.text:
**✦ MAGIC ITEM GAINED: ALDOUS'S MEASURE**
*Wondrous Item (Requires Attunement by a Solamnic Knight), Uncommon*
A small, ancient book with a cracked leather binding and forty years of margin notes in Ser Aldous's precise hand. The pages are worn thin where he returned to them most. It smells of tallow and old stone — the chapel library at Brightholm.
**Mechanics:** While attuned: advantage on History checks related to law, justice, or the Knights of Solamnia. Once per long rest: you may consult the book to automatically know the Measure's position on any legal or ethical question (the DM will provide the answer). Additionally, Insight checks against NPCs who are acting under false pretence of law are made with advantage.
**Lore:** Ser Aldous of Brightholm carried this book through forty years of knighthood and gave it to the squire who understood it fastest. In the main campaign, senior Solamnic Knights will recognise it and understand what it means that you are carrying it.

choices.text: Proceed to Scene 70.

M4_practitioner.title: THE PRACTITIONER'S GIFT
prose: *The Last Camp*

He is quiet for a moment. Then: 'That is honest. That is the honest answer and it is the right answer.' He reaches into his robe and takes out something he has been carrying. 'I was not sure I would give you this,' he says. 'But you have earned it. Not because you were perfect. Because you understood what you were not perfect at and kept going anyway.'

boxes.text:
**✦ MAGIC ITEM GAINED: THE CHAPLAIN'S SEAL**
*Wondrous Item (Requires Attunement), Uncommon*
A brass stamp in the shape of the Solamnic sun, worn smooth with use. Aldous has used it to seal documents for thirty years. When you press it to paper, the impression is clean and permanent.
**Mechanics:** While attuned: once per long rest you may invoke the Measure's authority — any Intimidation or Persuasion check made to enforce law or protect innocents is made with advantage. Additionally, documents bearing this seal are treated as legally binding by any Solamnic NPC of Knight rank or below.
**Lore:** A working seal, not ceremonial. Aldous of Brightholm's seal carries forty years of genuine decisions. In the main campaign, NPCs who recognise it will understand that its bearer earned it.

choices.text: Proceed to Scene 70.

M4_scholar.title: THE SCHOLAR'S GIFT
prose: *The Last Camp*

'That,' says Aldous, 'is the truest thing a new squire can know.' He looks at you for a moment. 'You will make mistakes in Vogler. Every new knight does. What matters is that you have the foundation to understand them.' He sets the old cracked book down in front of you. 'Read the third chapter tonight. Then read it again after Vogler. See how different it looks.'

boxes.text:
**✦ MAGIC ITEM GAINED: THE COMMENTARIES (INCOMPLETE)**
*Wondrous Item (Requires Attunement), Common — Grows in Power*
A copy of the secondary Measure commentaries, heavily annotated by Ser Aldous but with wide margins left blank. He intended you to fill them.
**Mechanics:** While attuned: advantage on History checks about Solamnic law. After each completed adventure in which you faced a Measure dilemma, add a note to one blank page. When all blank pages are filled (DM discretion, usually 3-5 adventures), the book becomes Aldous's Measure (Uncommon, see Keeper's gift). The item grows with you.
**Lore:** A book given to someone at the beginning of understanding, not the end. The best version of this gift is earned later. In the main campaign, any Solamnic Knight who examines it will read Aldous's marginal note on the front page: 'For the squire who had the sense to know what they did not know yet.'

choices.text: Proceed to Scene 70.

56B.title: WHAT THESSALY DOES NOT WRITE DOWN
prose: *Castle Brightholm — East Gate, Late Night*
She has been writing for three hours. You know because you checked twice and told yourself both times that you were checking the perimeter. The third time you stop pretending.

Her notebook is closed when you arrive. She was waiting. Thessaly Brightwater does not accidentally leave her notebook closed when someone she wants to talk to approaches. The pen is behind her ear. The locket is in her hand — open, for once. She closes it when she sees you looking but does not put it away.

'I wrote three versions of tonight,' she says. 'The professional one. The honest one. And the one I will not send.' She looks at you with the particular directness that means she has decided something. 'You are in the third one.'

boxes.text:
**ROMANCE MOMENT**
She is showing you the version of herself that does not appear in dispatches.

choices.text: 'Show me.'
'Keep it. Some things do not need to be read to be known.'
'I am glad I am in any version.' Leave her to write.

11_AFTERNOON.title: THE AFTERNOON
prose: The sun is overhead and the garrison shifts into its afternoon rhythm. The morning's edge — training, study, the formality of formation — gives way to something looser. Squires move between tasks with less supervision. The mess hall fills. The forge fires are banked high. Somewhere in the east wing, Thessaly is interviewing someone. Somewhere else, Mira is tending whoever needs tending. The garrison is a small world and you are learning its geography.

Tomorrow you march south. The field exercise. 'Reported bandits.' Whatever that means, it means something different than it did three weeks ago. The south road has been in the briefings every morning and every morning the language has been slightly more careful, which is how you know the situation is slightly worse.

At the mess table, between mouthfuls, a garrison rider mentions something offhand: dead draconians found at waypoints along the south road — killed cleanly, by someone who knew what they were doing. No patrol has claimed credit. 'Someone is out there hunting them alone,' he says, and shakes his head like that is either very brave or very stupid and he has not decided which. Across the table, another soldier mutters about a barn south of the ridge that refugees have been avoiding — says there is someone sheltering there, civilian clothes, moves like military. 'Could be a deserter. Could be worse.' Nobody follows up. The south road has too many stories and not enough patrols to check them all.

boxes.text: **AFTERNOON — Choose how to spend the afternoon. You may return here after each activity.**

choices.text: Go to the mess hall. Eat. Watch. Learn
Find Thessaly — she may have intelligence on the south road
Check on Mira before you march
Visit the forge. Your blade needs attention
Visit the armoury. Your gear could use an upgrade before the march
Shouts from the training yard. Something has come over the wall
The afternoon is spent. Move on to the evening
(If not yet visited) A commotion at the market — a merchant is arguing with a knight
(If not yet visited) The stockade. There is a young soldier inside who should not be there

11_ARMOURY.title: THE ARMOURY
prose: The armoury is in the east wing, past the forge. The heat hits you three steps before you reach the door. Inside, the walls are lined with racks — swords, shields, polearms, all maintained to Solamnic standard, which means they gleam even when nobody is looking. The armourer is a thin man named Garrett who moves with the precision of someone who has fitted a thousand suits of mail and knows exactly which rivet you will need replaced before you do.
He looks at your current equipment, frowns the professional frown of a man who sees room for improvement, and gestures at his stock.

Garrett watches you test the balance of whatever you have chosen. He nods once if he approves. He nods once if he does not approve, too — you cannot tell the difference. 'The south road is not kind to cheap steel,' he says. 'Whatever you carry, carry it sharp.'

boxes.text:
**SHOP — BRIGHTHOLM ARMOURY**
Browse the wares. Prices in steel pieces (stl). Check your character sheet for current gold.

**WEAPONS:**
| Item | Price | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| Longsword | 15 stl | 1d8+STR, versatile (1d10) |
| Greatsword | 20 stl | 2d6+STR, heavy, two-handed |
| Shortsword | 10 stl | 1d6+STR, finesse, light |
| Handaxe | 5 stl | 1d6+STR, thrown (20/60) |
| Dagger | 2 stl | 1d4+STR, finesse, light, thrown (20/60) |
| Javelin (3) | 3 stl | 1d6+STR, thrown (30/120) |

**ARMOUR & SHIELDS:**
| Item | Price | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| Shield | 10 stl | +2 AC |
| Studded Leather | 12 stl | AC 12+DEX (lighter than chain) |
| Chain Mail | 25 stl | AC 16 (upgrade/replacement) |
| Breastplate | 60 stl | AC 14+DEX (max 2), no stealth penalty |

**RARE STOCK ROLL (once per visit):**
Roll 1d20 to see if Garrett has anything in the back.
1–12: Standard stock only.
13–14: Uncommon — Greater Potion of Healing available (15 stl, 4d4+4 HP). 'Left behind by a patrol.'
15–16: Uncommon — Masterwork Greatsword available (25 stl, +1 damage, not magical). 'My best work this year.'
17: Uncommon — Qualinesti Chain available (75 stl, AC 13+DEX, no stealth penalty). Elven-forged, light as silk. Pre-Cataclysm trade.
18: Uncommon — Ancestral Solamnic Shield available (75 stl, +3 AC). The Rose crest glows when held by one true to the Measure.
19: Rare — Brightblade Steel Longsword available (100 stl, +1 attack/damage). Forged in the style of House Brightblade. The blade never dulls.
20: Rare — Dragonslayer Greatsword available (200 stl, +1 attack/damage, +3d6 vs dragons). A massive two-handed blade inscribed with the names of dragons slain by the Knights of old. Made for ending the winged terrors.

*Record purchases on your character sheet. Deduct steel spent.*

choices.text: Done shopping. Return to the afternoon

11_EVENING.title: THE EVENING
prose: The light changes. The garrison at dusk is a different place than the garrison at noon — quieter, longer-shadowed, the kind of quiet where conversations happen that would not happen in daylight. The wall torches are lit. The mess hall empties. The courtyard belongs to the night watch now and the people who come out when the day's work is done and the real thinking begins.

Tomorrow. The word has weight now. You can feel it in the way people move — slightly more deliberately, slightly more aware of each other. Aldric cleaned his armour twice today. Thessaly's notebook has not left her hand since lunch. Even Caeron, who is never visibly affected by anything, paused at the window during the afternoon briefing and looked south for three seconds longer than a man looks at something he is not worried about.

boxes.text: **EVENING — Choose how to spend the evening. You may return here after each activity.**

choices.text: Take a night watch shift. The wall has things to teach you
Find a quiet place. Write a letter home. You may not get another chance before the march
Seek out a companion for a quiet conversation by the fire
Turn in early. Tomorrow matters and sleep is preparation
The alarm bell. Goblins on the perimeter. Grab your weapon
Spend the evening getting to know your companions better
(If not yet visited) A commotion at the market — a merchant is arguing with a knight
(If not yet visited) The stockade. There is a young soldier inside who should not be there

11_FIRE.title: EVENING BY THE FIRE
prose: The courtyard brazier is where the garrison comes when the day is over and the barracks feel too close. Three squires are already there, talking in low voices about nothing — the food, the weather, a rumour about the south. They make room when you approach. This is how it works: the fire belongs to whoever needs it.

boxes.text: **Choose a companion to sit with. Each conversation is its own moment.**

choices.text: Aldric is here, arms crossed, watching the fire like it owes him something
Thessaly is at the edge, notebook closed for once, looking at the stars
Mira is tending a small herb pouch by firelight
Lord Caeron is standing at the edge of the light, alone
Sit alone. The fire is enough company

11_FIRE_ALDRIC.title: ALDRIC BY THE FIRE
prose: He does not look up when you sit down. This is not unfriendliness — it is Aldric's way of saying he noticed and does not need to perform noticing. You sit in silence for a while. The fire crackles. Somewhere on the wall, the night watch changes.
'Do you think about what happens after?' he says, eventually. Not looking at you. 'After the Trials. After the oath. After all of this becomes — real.'
He means: after we are not squires anymore. After the practice swords are replaced by the ones that kill people. After the south road stops being a briefing and starts being a place we go.

choices.text: 'Yes. Every night.' He looks at you. Something shifts. The fire pops. Neither of you moves to leave, but the silence has changed
'I try not to. It does not help.' He nods slowly. The fire burns lower. After a while he claps your shoulder once and stands
'I think about who I want beside me when it does.' He is very still. The fire is between you. Then he exhales and you sit together until the coals dim

11_FIRE_CAERON.title: CAERON AT THE EDGE
prose: He is standing where the firelight meets the dark — precisely at the boundary, as though he has calculated the exact distance at which he can observe without participating. When you approach he does not move but something in his posture adjusts. An acknowledgement.
'You should be resting, squire.' It is not an order. It is the observation of a man who knows what tomorrow requires and is concerned you do not.
'So should you, my lord.'

A pause. Then, very quietly: 'I do not rest well before exercises. I have found it more useful to stand here and count the things that could go wrong and make a plan for each of them. I am currently on number fourteen.'

boxes.text: **CONDITIONAL — Lord Caeron is nearby.**

choices.text: 'What is number fourteen?' He almost smiles. Almost. You stand there until he reaches number twenty-three, and then he says 'Goodnight, squire' in a voice that is almost warm
'You care more than you show, my lord.' He goes very still. A long silence. Then: 'Go and rest.' But his voice has changed, and you carry it with you back toward the firelight
Stand with him. Count your own list. Say nothing. When you finally leave, he nods once — the nod of a man who understands that some things do not need to be spoken aloud

11_FIRE_MIRA.title: MIRA BY THE FIRE
prose: She is sorting herbs by firelight with the focused efficiency of someone whose hands know the work better than her eyes do. Yarrow. Comfrey. Something you do not recognise. She does not stop when you sit down but she moves the herb pouch slightly closer to you — an invitation to watch, or to stay, or both.

'I learned this from my mother,' she says. 'She could identify forty plants by smell alone. I can do thirty-two. I keep a list of the eight I cannot.' She looks up. 'That is what a healer is, if you think about it. A person who keeps a list of what they cannot yet do and works on it.'

boxes.text: **CONDITIONAL — Mira must be in your party.**

choices.text: 'That sounds like a knight, too.' She smiles — genuinely. You sit together a while longer before the fire burns low and the night calls you both back
Ask her to teach you one. She does — carefully, patiently. By the time you can identify it by touch alone, the brazier has burned to embers
Sit quietly and watch her work. The silence is enough. When she finally ties the pouch closed, the fire has gone to coals and the courtyard is still

11_FIRE_THESSALY.title: THESSALY BY THE FIRE
prose: Her notebook is closed. This is unusual enough that it constitutes an event. She is looking at the sky with an expression you have not seen on her before — not analytical, not professional. Wistful.

'Do you know what I was before this?' she says, when you sit down. 'A clerk. In a printing house in Palanthas. I wrote advertisements for soap. I was very good at it.' A pause. 'I left because I realised I was writing about clean things from a clean room while the world was getting dirty. The pen felt dishonest for the first time.'

choices.text: 'And now?' 'Now the pen feels like a weapon. I have not decided if that is better.' She looks at the stars a moment longer. Then the notebook comes out and the moment is over, but something was exchanged
'You are doing important work here.' She looks at you with sharp gratitude. Then the mask comes back — professional, precise — but warmer at the edges
'What would you write about tonight, if you could write anything?' She opens the notebook. For a moment you read together by firelight. Then she closes it and the evening resumes

11_LETTER.title: THE LETTER HOME
prose: You sit on the edge of your bunk with a candle and a piece of parchment that cost you two coppers from the quartermaster's stores. The pen is borrowed from Thessaly, who lent it without comment and with the faintest smile that suggested she approved of what you were using it for.

You write. You cross out. You write again. The difficulty is not finding words — it is finding the right ones. You cannot tell them what you know about the south road. You cannot tell them about the doubled guard or the refugee reports or the way Caeron looks when he thinks no one is watching. What you can tell them is that you are well, that you are ready, that you have friends beside you and a purpose ahead of you. These things are true. They are not the whole truth. But they are the part of the truth that will let your mother sleep, and that is worth the two coppers.

Aldric walks past as you are sealing it. He stops. Reads your expression. 'Family?' You nod. He nods back — the nod of someone who understands exactly what that letter costs and does not need to say so. Then he is gone and the barracks is quiet and the candle burns low.

choices.text: Seal it. Set it on the quartermaster's outbound pile. The evening is not over yet
Add a postscript. Something true. Something you have not said yet. Then seal it. The candle is lower now but the evening still has time

11_MORNING.title: THE MORNING
prose: The spar with Aldric is done and the bruises are already forming. Formation is over. Lord Caeron has given the assignments. The field exercise on the south road is not today — it is tomorrow, or the day after, depending on preparations. Today is yours to use. The garrison operates on a rhythm: mornings are for training and study, afternoons for practical work and intelligence, evenings for the quieter things — the watch, the forge, the conversations that happen when the day's noise settles. How you spend this time matters. It is the last of it you will have before things change.

boxes.text: **MORNING — Choose how to spend the morning hours. You may return here after each activity.**

choices.text: Seek out Ser Aldous in the chapel library. The Measure has questions you have not answered
Check the infirmary. There was mention of refugees arriving in the night
Train harder. You want to be sharp for tomorrow
Visit the garrison quartermaster. You have steel and tomorrow is the march
The morning is spent. Move on to the afternoon
(If not yet visited) A commotion at the market — a merchant is arguing with a knight
(If not yet visited) The stockade. There is a young soldier inside who should not be there

11_NIGHT.title: THE NIGHT BEFORE
prose: The barracks settles. Boots off. Armour racked. The sounds of ten people finding sleep in the order they always find it — the heavy breather in the corner first, then the shuffler by the window, then one by one the others, until it is just you and the dark and the particular quality of silence that exists in a room full of people who are all pretending to be asleep while thinking about tomorrow.

Aldric's breathing evens out. He is either asleep or very good at pretending. You stare at the ceiling and count the beams and think about the south road and the field exercise and the word 'bandits' and what it means when the garrison doubles the guard and Lord Caeron looks south for three seconds too long.
Sleep comes eventually. It always does. The last thing you see before it takes you is the window — south-facing, dark, showing nothing. The morning bell will come. It always does.

choices.text: Sleep comes. Dawn follows. The field exercise begins
Dawn comes. Time to leave Castle Brightholm.

11_PATROL.title: THE NIGHT PATROL
prose: The alarm is a single bell, struck twice. Not the full garrison alarm — the sentry alarm, the one that means *something is here but we do not know what yet*. You are on your feet before the second strike fades. Your weapon is in your hand before you think about reaching for it. Training.

The south perimeter is a stretch of low wall between the garrison's main fortification and the old granary. The torches have been doused — deliberately, which means the sentries saw something and went dark. Aldric is beside you. He arrived at the same time you did, which means he was not sleeping either.

A garrison sentry — a man named Corwin — is crouched behind the wall. There is an arrow in his shoulder. It went through the mail at the joint, which means whoever shot it knew where to aim. 'Goblins,' he says through his teeth. 'Three, maybe four. They were probing the wall. Pulled back when I called it.'
Blood is running down his arm. The arrow is still in.

boxes.text:
**SEARCH THE FALLEN (Optional — moral choice)**
The goblins are dead. Their equipment is crude but they may carry coin or useful items.
Searching enemy dead is not forbidden by the Measure, but it is not honoured either.
Aldric looks at his boots. Corwin does not care — he is being bandaged.

**A) Search them.** (-1 ROSE or -1 CROWN, whichever is lower. Roll below.)
**B) Leave them. Report to the garrison.** (+1 ROSE or +1 CROWN. No loot.)

**LOOT ROLL (if searched):** Roll 1d20.
1-5: Nothing useful. Goblin gear is garbage.
6-10: 1d4 stl in a belt pouch. Copper mixed with steel. 1 day rations.
11-14: 2d4 stl + a mundane item (whetstone, 10ft rope, 2 days rations).
15-17: 3d4 stl + a useful item (Potion of Healing, caltrops, a signal horn). 1 day rations.
18-19: 4d4 stl + a quality item (masterwork dagger, a map of the south road). 2 days rations.
20: 5d4 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL. 3 days rations.

**GOBLIN CAMP STASH:** The goblins had a camp in the treeline. Investigation DC 11.
**SUCCESS:** Stash Loot Roll:
1-5: Empty. They ate everything.
6-10: Supplies (rations, crude bandages, 1d4 stl).
11-14: 2d6 stl + mundane item.
15-17: 3d6 stl + Potion of Healing.
18-19: 4d6 stl + quality weapon.
20: 5d6 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL.

**COMBAT MODE**
**Pen & Paper:** Use stat blocks below. Run the encounter at your table, then skip to Victory: The last goblin falls. The night patrol is clear. Corwin is alive — barely. The garrison owes you.
Fallen (0 HP): The garrison relief arrives. Aldric carries you back. 1 HP, one exhaustion.
**AFTER THE FIGHT**.
**Narrative Mode:** Follow the rounds below. Roll when prompted.
**INJURED NPC — SENTRY CORWIN**
Arrow in shoulder, bleeding. Will collapse in 4 rounds without treatment.
**Heal him:** Mira (if present, automatic), Paladin Cure Wounds (1 spell slot), Paladin Lay on Hands (5 HP from pool), Bandage (1 use, stabilize at 1 HP), Fighter Magic Initiate healing spell (if applicable).
*Record any resources spent on your character sheet.*
**If healed:** Corwin returns to the wall. +1 morale for garrison. He gives you his garrison watch token (+1 to one future Brightholm check).
**If not healed:** Corwin is carried to the infirmary. He survives but cannot fight tomorrow. The south wall is short one sentry.
**COMPANION SELECTION**
Aldric is with you automatically. Choose one additional companion (if available):
**Sera** — Scout advantage. +1 to your first attack roll (she spots them first).
**Mira** — Heals Corwin automatically. Frees your action economy.
**Lysander** — Veteran presence. +2 to your first attack roll. 'Watch the treeline.'
**ENEMY STAT BLOCKS (Pen & Paper)**
**Goblin Scout (3):** AC 13, HP 7, Speed 30ft. Scimitar +4 (1d6+2). Shortbow +4 (1d6+2, 80/320). Nimble Escape (Disengage/Hide as bonus action).
**Goblin Boss:** AC 15, HP 12, Speed 30ft. Scimitar +4 (1d6+2). Shortbow +4 (1d6+2). Redirect Attack (reaction: when missed, redirect to adjacent goblin scout — the boss sacrifices his own). Multi-attack: 2 scimitar attacks.
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 1
The treeline is forty feet south. Moonlight makes the grass silver and the shadows black. You can see shapes — low, hunched, moving with the particular scuttle of things that know they are smaller than you and have decided that numbers and darkness make up the difference.
Three goblins. They are fanned out across the approach — one left, one centre, one right. The centre one is bigger. Better armed. It is giving orders in a language that sounds like gravel being chewed.
**YOUR ATTACK — Choose a target:**
**Left goblin** / **Centre goblin (Boss)** / **Right goblin**

**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC = target's AC (Scouts: 13, Boss: 15)
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus. Meet or beat the DC = hit.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage (your weapon die + STR modifier).

**IF SERA IS PRESENT:** +1 to this roll. She spotted them from the wall — you know exactly where they are.
**IF LYSANDER IS PRESENT:** +2 to this roll. He is beside you, calm as a man watching rain. 'The big one. Always the big one first.'
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one:**
**Aldric:**
A) Flank — Move to opposite side. +1 to your next attack.
B) Shield Wall — Brace beside you. +1 AC until next round.
C) Aggressive Strike — Charges the nearest goblin. 1d8+2 damage, but Aldric takes 1d4 damage from the engagement.

**(If Sera):**
A) Scout Shot — Fires from the wall. 1d6 damage to a goblin of your choice.
B) Mark Target — Calls out movement. +2 to your next attack.
C) Disengage Cover — Covers your retreat if needed. Safe withdrawal guaranteed.

**(If Mira):**
A) Heal Corwin — Removes the arrow, stops the bleeding. Corwin stabilized. (Frees your healing resources.)
B) Bandage — Stabilizes Corwin at 1 HP. Less effective but faster.
C) Herbal Boost — Gives you a poultice. +1 to your next saving throw.

**(If Lysander):**
A) Combat Instruction — Corrects your stance mid-fight. +2 to your next attack.
B) Veteran Strike — Steps forward and cuts. 1d10+3 damage to the nearest goblin.
C) Guard — Positions himself to take the next hit meant for you.
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):**
• (Paladin) Cast Bless: +1d4 to attacks and saves for up to 3 targets, Concentration, lasts entire combat. 1 spell slot. Does not replace attack.
• (Paladin) Divine Smite: Bonus action after hitting with a melee weapon. 2d8 radiant (+1d8 vs Fiends/Undead). Once per turn. 1 spell slot (or free 1/long rest at level 2+).
• (Paladin) Lay on Hands: Heal self or Corwin up to [pool] HP. Bonus action (does not replace attack).
• (Paladin) Cure Wounds: Heal Corwin 2d8+CHA HP. 1 spell slot. Replaces your attack this round.
• (Fighter) Action Surge: Take an additional Action this round (except Magic action). 1/short or long rest.
• (Fighter) Second Wind: Heal 1d10+[level] HP. Bonus action. 2 uses/long rest, regain 1/short rest.
• (Any) Drink Potion of Healing: 2d4+2 HP. Bonus action. Consumes 1 potion.
• (Any) Use Bandage on Corwin: Stabilize at 1 HP. Replaces your attack.
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 1
The right goblin fires its shortbow. The arrow comes out of the dark with a sound like a whispered threat.
**DEFENSE ROLL:** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 14 (goblin attack +4, DC = attack bonus + 10)
**SUCCESS (14+):** You deflect or dodge. No damage.
**FAILURE (1-13):** The arrow hits. Take 1d6+2 piercing damage. Record on your character sheet.
**IF ALDRIC CHOSE SHIELD WALL:** DC reduced to 13 (+1 AC).
**IF LYSANDER CHOSE GUARD:** Lysander takes the hit instead. He grunts. 'That one was free.'
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 2
The goblins are fast. The one you did not target has circled right, looking for a flank. The boss is barking orders — sharper now, angrier. Whatever they expected to find at this wall, resistance was not it.
The night air smells of goblin — unwashed leather, rancid fat, something acrid that might be poison on the arrowheads. The grass is wet. Your boots slide. The torch on the wall behind you casts your shadow long and the goblins keep looking at it, tracking your position by the dark shape you throw.
**YOUR ATTACK — Choose a target:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC = target's AC
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus. Meet or beat the DC = hit.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage.

**PERCEPTION CHECK (free action):** Roll 1d20 + WIS modifier | DC 13
**SUCCESS:** You spot a fourth goblin — hidden in the ditch, flanking position. You can redirect your attack or warn Aldric.
**FAILURE:** You do not see the fourth goblin. It will attack in Round 3 with advantage.
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one:** (same options as Round 1)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):** (same options as Round 1)
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 2
The boss charges. It is faster than it should be, low to the ground, scimitar in each hand. It screams something that might be a war cry or might be an insult — with goblins, the distinction is academic.
**DEFENSE ROLL:** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 14
**SUCCESS (14+):** You turn the blade. The boss snarls and recoils.
**FAILURE (1-13):** The scimitar bites. Take 1d6+2 slashing damage.

**BOSS ABILITY — REDIRECT ATTACK:** If you hit the boss this round and a goblin scout is adjacent, the boss shoves the scout into the blade path. The scout takes the damage instead. The boss survives. *This happens once.*
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 3
The fight is close now. Blood on the grass — yours, theirs, or both. The goblins are not retreating. They should be retreating. Three goblins do not hold against armoured Solamnic squires unless they are desperate, fanatical, or buying time for something else. The boss is still standing and still fighting and it occurs to you, between heartbeats, that this was never a raid. This was reconnaissance. They were counting your sentries.
**IF THE 4TH GOBLIN WAS NOT SPOTTED:** It emerges from the ditch behind you. Surprise attack with advantage.
**SURPRISE DEFENSE ROLL:** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 14. The goblin rolls twice and takes the better result (advantage).
**FAILURE:** Take 1d6+2 piercing damage. The goblin is now in melee range behind you.
**IF THE 4TH GOBLIN WAS SPOTTED:** You warned Aldric. He is already turning. The goblin's ambush fails.
**YOUR ATTACK — Final round. End this.**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC = target's AC
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage. *If this kills the boss, describe it. This is your moment.*
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one:** (same options as previous rounds)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):** (same options as previous rounds)
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 3
Whatever remains fights with the desperation of things that know the mathematics have turned. If the boss is alive, it makes one final attack — not at you. At Corwin. Finishing what it started.
**IF BOSS IS ALIVE — PROTECT CORWIN:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Athletics or DEX save | DC 13
**SUCCESS:** You intercept. The boss's blade hits your shield or armour. Corwin is safe.
**FAILURE:** The boss reaches Corwin. 1d6+2 damage to an already-wounded man. If Corwin has not been healed, he drops to 0 HP and begins death saves.
AFTER THE FIGHT
The last goblin falls or flees. The night returns to its usual sounds — wind, distant water, the breathing of people who have just fought for the first time and are discovering what that does to the body. Your hands are shaking. This is normal. Aldric's hands are shaking too, and he does not try to hide it.
On the goblin boss: a crude map of the garrison perimeter. Marked in charcoal: the south wall, the granary, the sentry positions. Whoever sent these goblins knew what they were looking for.

**SEARCH THE FALLEN (Optional — moral choice)**
The goblins are dead. Their equipment is crude but they may carry coin or useful items.
Searching enemy dead is not forbidden by the Measure, but it is not honoured either.
Aldric looks at his boots. Corwin does not care — he is being bandaged.

**A) Search them.** (-1 ROSE or -1 CROWN, whichever is lower. Roll below.)
**B) Leave them. Report to the garrison.** (+1 ROSE or +1 CROWN. No loot.)

**LOOT ROLL (if searched):** Roll 1d20.
1-5: Nothing useful. Goblin gear is garbage.
6-10: 1d4 stl in a belt pouch. Copper mixed with steel. 1 day rations (smoked rat meat — edible, barely).
11-14: 2d4 stl + a mundane item (whetstone, 10ft rope, 2 days rations).
15-17: 3d4 stl + a useful item (Potion of Healing, caltrops, a signal horn). 1 day rations.
18-19: 4d4 stl + a quality item (masterwork dagger, a map of the south road). 2 days rations.
20: 5d4 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL. 3 days rations.
**RARE ITEM ROLL (only on natural 20 above):** Roll 1d20.
1–19: Quality mundane item (well-crafted goblin blade, usable as shortsword).
20: MAGIC ITEM — +1 Dagger. Elvish make. What a goblin was doing with it is a question you cannot answer tonight.

**GOBLIN CAMP STASH:** The goblins had a camp in the treeline. Investigation DC 11.
**SUCCESS:** Stash Loot Roll:
1-5: Empty. They ate everything.
6-10: Supplies (rations, crude bandages, 1d4 stl).
11-14: 2d6 stl + mundane item.
15-17: 3d6 stl + Potion of Healing.
18-19: 4d6 stl + quality weapon.
20: 5d6 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL.
**NPC GRATITUDE — SENTRY CORWIN** (if healed)
Corwin grips your arm with his good hand. His face is grey but his grip is strong. 'I owe you one, Squire. I mean that.'

**ROLL:** Persuasion (+[bonus]) | CHA | DC 10
*You are not haggling. The roll reflects how much Corwin has to give.*

**10–12 (Modest):** 'Here.' He presses 1d4 stl into your hand. Sentry pay. It is all he carries.
**13–15 (Grateful):** 2d4 stl and his garrison watch token (+1 to one future Brightholm check).
**16–18 (Deeply Grateful):** 3d4 stl, the watch token, and a Potion of Healing from the sentry kit. 'I was saving it. You need it more.'
**19+ (Indebted):** 4d4 stl, the watch token, and a family ring (5 stl value, or keep for +1 to one Persuasion check in Vogler — 'Corwin's family is from the lake region').
**FAILURE (1–9):** Corwin has nothing to give. He clasps your arm. 'I will remember your name.' .
**AFTER THE FIGHT — UPDATE YOUR SHEET**
Record current HP. Mark any used: Second Wind, Action Surge, spell slots,
Lay on Hands pool, bandages, potions consumed.
Update gold if loot was found. Record any items gained.
**Short rest available:** Recover Action Surge, regain 1 Second Wind use. HP: spend Hit Dice if desired.
Rewards: 50 XP.

choices.text: Return to the evening. The wall is secure. For now

11_QM.title: THE QUARTERMASTER
prose: The quartermaster is a broad woman named Hesta who has been supplying Solamnic garrisons since before the Cataclysm broke the supply lines. Her storeroom smells of oiled leather, lamp oil, and the particular dust of things that have been inventoried more often than they have been used. She looks at your tabard, your rank, your purse, and calculates exactly how much of your stipend you are about to leave on her counter.
'Squire,' she says, by way of greeting. 'What do you need?'
Hesta wraps your purchases in waxed cloth with garrison efficiency. 'Don't die,' she says. This is her version of good luck.

boxes.text:
**SHOP — BRIGHTHOLM QUARTERMASTER**
Browse the wares. Prices in steel pieces (stl). Check your character sheet for current gold.

| Item | Price | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| Bandage (1 use) | 1 stl | Stabilize at 1 HP |
| Potion of Healing | 5 stl | 2d4+2 HP, bonus action in combat |
| Rations (3 days) | 1 stl | Travel supplies |
| Antitoxin | 3 stl | Advantage on poison saves for 1 hour |
| Whetstone | 1 stl | +1 damage on next attack (single use) |
| Caltrops (bag) | 2 stl | 10ft area, DC 12 DEX or 1 piercing + speed 0 |
| Rope (50 ft) | 1 stl | Climbing, binding |
| Torch (5) | 1 stl | Light source |
| Longsword | 15 stl | 1d8+STR, versatile (1d10), Sap |
| Greatsword | 20 stl | 2d6+STR, heavy, two-handed, Graze |
| Battleaxe | 15 stl | 1d8+STR, versatile (1d10), Topple |
| Greataxe | 20 stl | 1d12+STR, heavy, two-handed, Cleave |
| Warhammer | 15 stl | 1d8+STR, versatile (1d10), Push |
| Morningstar | 15 stl | 1d8+STR, Sap |
| Flail | 15 stl | 1d8+STR, Sap |
| War Pick | 15 stl | 1d8+STR, versatile (1d10), Push |
| Rapier | 15 stl | 1d8+STR, finesse, Vex |
| Halberd | 20 stl | 1d10+STR, heavy, reach, two-handed, Cleave |
| Glaive | 20 stl | 1d10+STR, heavy, reach, two-handed, Graze |
| Shortsword | 10 stl | 1d6+STR, finesse, light, Vex |
| Handaxe | 5 stl | 1d6+STR, thrown (20/60), Vex |
| Dagger | 2 stl | 1d4+STR, finesse, light, thrown, Nick |
| Javelin (3) | 3 stl | 1d6+STR, thrown (30/120), Slow |
| Chain Mail | 25 stl | AC 16, STR 13 required, Stealth disadv. |
| Scale Mail | 20 stl | AC 14+DEX (max 2), Stealth disadv. |
| Studded Leather | 12 stl | AC 12+DEX |
| Leather Armor | 10 stl | AC 11+DEX |
| Shield | 10 stl | +2 AC |

**RARE STOCK ROLL (once per visit):**
Roll 1d20 to see if Hesta has anything special today.
1–12: Standard stock only.
13–14: Uncommon — Greater Potion of Healing available (15 stl, 4d4+4 HP).
15–16: Uncommon — Masterwork Longsword available (25 stl, +1 damage, not magical). ‘Solamnic make. Pre-Cataclysm.’
17: Uncommon — Thorbardin-Forged Mail available (100 stl, AC 16, crits become normal hits). Dwarven runes along the collar.
18: Uncommon — Crown Knight’s Shield available (75 stl, +3 AC). The Crown crest. Old garrison issue, blessed.
19: Rare — Kiri-Jolith’s Mail available (125 stl, AC 17). The war-god’s blessing lingers. You feel steadier wearing it.
20: Rare — Dragonbane Longsword available (150 stl, +1 attack/damage, +2d6 vs dragonkind). Pre-Cataclysm. Made for the wars before the wars.

*If the rare stock roll reveals an item, you may buy it at the listed price. Record purchases on your character sheet. Deduct steel spent.*

choices.text: Done shopping. Return to the morning

11_YARD.title: THE THING IN THE TRAINING YARD
prose: The shout comes from the yard — not a drill shout, not the bark of a training sergeant. A real shout. The kind that has fear in it. You are running before you identify the voice.

The training yard gate — the one that leads to the eastern slope, the one that is supposed to be barred at all times because the eastern slope leads to forest — is open. Not forced. Unbarred. Someone left it unlatched and the smell of the mess hall kitchen, which backs onto the yard, has drawn something in from the wild.

A dire boar. Not the farm boar you have seen in textbooks — a dire boar, chest-high, scarred, tusks the colour of old bone and the length of your forearm. It has overturned a weapons rack and is standing in the centre of the yard, sides heaving, eyes red with the particular rage of an animal that has been driven south by something worse than itself. The Dragon Army's advance is pushing wildlife north. This is what that looks like at ground level.

Two squires are pressed against the far wall. One of them is bleeding — a gash across the thigh from a tusk. The other is holding a practice sword that will not do a thing against an animal this size. They are both younger than you and they are both terrified.

**IF ALDRIC IS IN YOUR PARTY:** Aldric is already in the yard. He came from the armoury at a dead sprint and he is carrying his real sword, not a practice blade. He positions himself between the boar and the wounded squire without being told.
**IF MIRA IS IN YOUR PARTY:** You can hear her boots on the corridor stone behind you. She will be here in thirty seconds. The squire's thigh wound needs her.

boxes.text:
**COMBAT MODE**
**Pen & Paper:** Use stat blocks below. Run at your table. Skip to AFTER THE FIGHT.
**Narrative Mode:** Follow rounds below. Roll when prompted.

**ENEMY:**
**Dire Boar:** AC 12, HP 28, Speed 40 ft. Tusks +5 (2d6+3 piercing). Charge: if the boar moves 20+ ft in a straight line, tusks deal an extra 2d6 and target must make DC 13 STR save or be knocked prone. Relentless (1/day): if reduced to 0 HP by damage that would not kill outright, drops to 1 HP instead.

**YOUR STATS:** Fighter: HP 12, AC 16, Longsword +5 1d8+3. Paladin: HP 11, AC 16, Longsword +5 1d8+3.

**COMPANION ACTIONS (choose 1 per round):**
| Companion | Action A | Action B | Action C |
|-----------|----------|----------|----------|
| Aldric (if present) | Flank (+1 attack) | Shield Wall (+1 AC) | Aggressive Strike (1d8+2, takes 1d4) |
| Other squire | Distraction (boar -1 next attack) | Throw practice sword (1d4) | Flee with wounded squire (removes civilians from danger) |
**ROUND 1 — THE CHARGE**

*The boar lowers its head. The tusks catch the afternoon light. It charges — not at you, at the wounded squire. Forty feet of yard, four hundred pounds of animal, and two seconds to put yourself in the way.*

**INTERCEPT:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Athletics (+3) | STR | DC 13

**SUCCESS (13+):** You throw yourself into the boar's path. It hits you instead. Make a STR save DC 13 or be knocked prone. Either way, the squire is clear..

**FAILURE (1-12):** You are too slow. The boar reaches the squire. The squire takes 2d6+3 damage. If Aldric is present, he intercepts instead (he takes the hit, -5 HP to Aldric). If Aldric is not present, the other squire drags the wounded one aside — barely.

**YOUR ATTACK:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll (+5) | DC 12 (Boar AC)

**HIT:** Your blade bites into the boar's flank. It screams — a sound you did not know boars could make. Deal 1d8+3 damage.

**MISS:** The boar is fast. Faster than something that size should be.

**BOAR'S GORE:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Defense roll | DC 15 (Tusks +5)

**SUCCESS:** You catch the tusks on your blade and turn them aside.

**FAILURE:** Take 2d6+3 piercing damage. The tusks go through padding like it is not there.
**ROUND 2 — CORNERED ANIMAL**

*The boar is wounded and angry. It circles, head low, blood on its tusks — yours or the squire's or both. The training yard is not large enough for this. The walls make it worse. A cornered dire boar is the second most dangerous thing in the Solamnic lowlands. The first is what drove it here.*

**YOUR ATTACK:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll (+5) | DC 12

**HIT:** Deal 1d8+3 damage. The boar staggers. If this reduces it to 0 HP, **Relentless triggers**: it drops to 1 HP instead, enraged.

**MISS:** It dodges. For something this large, it moves like water.

**(Paladin) OPTIONAL — DIVINE SMITE:** If your attack hit, add 2d8 radiant damage. The light of your conviction is visible. The boar flinches. One spell slot expended.

**(Fighter) OPTIONAL — ACTION SURGE:** Take a second attack this round. One use expended.

**BOAR'S GORE (if still standing):**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Defense roll | DC 15

**SUCCESS:** Turned aside.

**FAILURE:** Take 2d6+3 piercing. If this drops you to 0 HP: **IF MIRA HAS ARRIVED** she stabilizes you. You wake up in the infirmary. The boar was finished by Aldric or the garrison watch. Proceed to AFTER THE FIGHT. **IF MIRA IS NOT PRESENT:** Aldric (if present) drags you clear. The garrison watch finishes the boar with crossbow bolts. You wake up on the yard stones.
**ROUND 3 — THE KILL (if boar still standing)**

*It is dying and it knows it and it does not care. The eyes are not red anymore — they are flat, animal, the eyes of a thing that has been running from something it cannot fight and has decided to stop running here, in this yard, against you. There is something terrible and honest in it.*

**YOUR ATTACK:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll (+5) | DC 12

**HIT:** The blade goes in clean. The boar falls. The yard is suddenly, completely quiet except for the sound of two squires breathing and the distant clatter of the garrison responding to an alarm that is already over.

**MISS:** Aldric finishes it. Or the garrison watch arrives with crossbows. Either way, it ends here.
Victory: The boar crashes to the ground. The yard is quiet. You are breathing hard and your sword arm is shaking but you are standing.
Fallen (0 HP): The boar gores you. Aldric drags you clear and the beast is brought down by the garrison. 1 HP, one exhaustion.
**AFTER THE FIGHT**

The boar is dead in the training yard. Its blood is on the flagstones and will stain. The wounded squire — his name is Pell, you learn later — has a gash that will scar but will heal. Mira, if present, treats him on the yard stones with the efficient calm of someone who has been waiting to be needed.

**IF MIRA IS IN YOUR PARTY:** She looks up from the wound. 'The gate was unlatched.' It is not an accusation. It is a diagnosis. Something drove this animal into the garrison and the garrison was not ready. She is already thinking about what else might come over the wall before morning.

**IF ALDRIC IS IN YOUR PARTY:** He is cleaning his blade. He looks at you. He does not say anything about the fight — Aldric does not comment on combat performance when the combat was real. What he does is nod. Once. The nod of a man who has been fighting beside you and has decided that fighting beside you is where he belongs.

Ser Aldous arrives. He studies the boar. He studies the gate. He studies you. 'The wildlife is moving north,' he says. 'The Dragon Army is pushing everything ahead of it. This will not be the last.' He pauses. 'You did well, Squire.'

**LOOT — BOAR TUSKS (optional):**
The dire boar's tusks are intact. They are valuable — ivory, old, dense enough to be worked into weapon grips or sold.
**A) Take the tusks.** Gain: Dire Boar Tusks (worth 3 stl, or can be worked into a weapon grip: +1 to Intimidation checks in combat).
**B) Leave them.** The garrison groundskeeper will deal with it. +1 ROSE (Pell thanks you for saving him, not for salvaging the thing that gored him).

**AFTER THE FIGHT — UPDATE YOUR SHEET**
Record current HP. Mark any used: Second Wind, Action Surge, spell slots, Lay on Hands pool.
**Short rest available:** Recover Second Wind, Action Surge.
Rewards: 65 XP.

choices.text: Return to the afternoon

1_HUB.title: THE HOURS BEFORE
prose: The sky has shifted since you woke — still dark, but the kind of dark that knows the light is coming. The castle moves around you in the small familiar sounds of a place about to begin its day. Boot-steps in the upper corridor. A door. The distant clatter of the kitchen grate being raked. You have time. Not much, but enough.

You stand in the corridor between the barracks and the courtyard and consider how to spend what remains of the morning. Seven days until the Trials. Each one a chance to learn something, or to miss something you will wish you had not.

boxes.text: **GATE: Track activities completed. The formation bell does not ring until you have spent time in at least two of Brightholm's corners this morning.**

choices.text: (If not yet visited) The training yard is calling. Aldric will be there — he is always there
(If not yet visited) The chapel library is quiet at this hour. The Measure waits
(If not yet visited) The letter from home is still folded in your pocket. You have not read it yet today
(If not yet visited) The infirmary. There is always someone who needs a steady hand
(If not yet visited) The south gate. You passed it earlier — the family may still be there
(If not yet visited) The campfire in the barracks yard. Evening stories and unfamiliar faces
(If not yet visited) The watchtower. Something on the horizon you want to see again
(If not yet visited) The evaluation hall. Ser Aldous has posted the first assessment.
(If 2+ activities completed) The bell rings. The morning you have been living in is over. Formation assembles

26_PREP.title: THE SOUTH GATE
prose: The armoury was open before the bell. Someone — you suspect Aldric — laid out your field kit on the bench with the particular arrangement of someone who has opinions about how equipment should be packed and has decided to express them through action rather than conversation. Sword oiled. Rations for three days. Waterskin. The practice blade replaced by the real one, which has a different weight — not heavier, exactly, but more serious, the weight of a thing that has a purpose beyond training.

The south gate is smaller than the main entrance — a working exit, not a ceremonial one. The stones are darker here, stained by centuries of departures that did not require an audience. Lord Caeron is already mounted, reviewing a field map with the compressed focus of a man who has been awake since before the bell and considers this normal. Aldric falls in beside you without discussion, the way he has fallen in beside you for eight months, and the familiarity of it steadies something.
**IF SERA IS IN YOUR PARTY:** She appears from a direction you did not see her arrive from, already packed, already mounted. She nods once.

**IF MIRA IS IN YOUR PARTY:** She is checking medical supplies in the saddlebag with the focus of someone cataloguing what she has against what she might need. The list in her head is longer than the one in the bag.

Caeron gives the briefing in the shadow of the gate: south road, field exercise, assess and report. The words are formal. The tone is not. He looks south for one second too long before he gives the order to move.

boxes.text:
**WAGES PAID — FIELD EXERCISE PAY**
The quartermaster hands you a leather pouch as you pass through the gate. Ten steel pieces — field exercise rate. 'Bring back receipts,' he says, deadpan. He has never once asked anyone for receipts.
**Add 10 stl to your gold.**

choices.text: Ride out. The south road is waiting

28_CARAVAN.title: THE AMBUSHED CARAVAN
28_CARAVAN.location: South Road — Half a Mile South of Camp, Night
prose: The smoke is thicker than cookfire. You smell it before you see it — wood, canvas, something chemical that makes your eyes water. The overturned cart is in the road ditch, one wheel still turning slowly. The mule is dead. The cargo is scattered across the verge: bolts of cloth, iron nails, a cask of something that has spilled into the dirt and smells of vinegar.
One body on the road. A merchant's guard — throat cut, eyes open, hand still on the hilt of a sword he never drew. He has been dead less than an hour.

The second figure is alive. Barely. He is under the cart, one leg pinned by the axle, and there is a compound fracture below the knee — bone through skin, blood soaking the road dust black. His breathing is the shallow, rapid kind that means shock. His name, you will learn, is Dorren. He is a merchant from Gateway. He was bringing supplies south because someone told him Vogler would pay double for iron and salt.
He has three rounds before the bleeding kills him.

boxes.text:
**SEARCH THE FALLEN (Optional — moral choice)**
The draconians are stone. The deserter's gear is scattered. You could search them.
Aldric is looking at the dead merchant's guard. He does not look at you.

**A) Search the bodies.** (-1 ROSE or -1 CROWN. Loot Roll below.)
**B) Leave them. The road has taken enough.** (+1 ROSE or +1 CROWN. No loot.)

**LOOT ROLL (if searched):** Roll 1d20.
1-5: Nothing useful. Draconian gear crumbles with the stone.
6-10: 1d4 stl from the deserter's belt pouch. 1 day rations.
11-14: 2d4 stl + a mundane item (whetstone, 2 days rations, a battered compass).
15-17: 3d4 stl + Potion of Healing from the deserter's kit. 2 days rations.
18-19: 4d4 stl + a quality item (the deserter's shortsword is well-made, or a map of Dragon Army patrol routes). 3 days rations.
20: 5d4 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL. 3 days rations.

**CARAVAN WRECKAGE:** Investigation DC 12. (No moral penalty — legitimate scouting.)
**SUCCESS:** Stash Loot Roll:
1-5: Spoiled supplies. Nothing salvageable.
6-10: Supplies (rations, bandages, 1d4 stl).
11-14: 2d6 stl + mundane item (iron nails, salt — trade goods).
15-17: 3d6 stl + Potion of Healing (hidden in cargo).
18-19: 4d6 stl + quality item (merchant's ledger — intel on Dragon Army supply disruption).
20: 5d6 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL.

**COMBAT MODE**
**Pen & Paper:** Use stat blocks below. Run the encounter at your table, then skip to Victory: The ambush is broken. The draconians are stone and the road is open again.
Fallen (0 HP): Aldric finishes the fight while you bleed in the road dust. 1 HP, one exhaustion.
**AFTER THE FIGHT**.
**Narrative Mode:** Follow the rounds below. Roll when prompted.
**INJURED NPC — MERCHANT DORREN**
Compound leg fracture, bleeding heavily. Dies in 3 rounds without healing.
**Heal him:** Medicine DC 13 (stabilize only), Mira (if present, automatic stabilize + 1d6+2 heal), Paladin Cure Wounds (1 spell slot, heals 2d8+CHA), Paladin Lay on Hands (from pool), Bandage (stabilize at 1 HP), Potion of Healing (2d4+2).
*Record any resources spent on your character sheet.*
**If healed:** Dorren survives. He appears in Vogler with supplies.. His shop in Vogler offers 10% discount (Scene 70C_SHOP).
**If not healed:** Dorren bleeds out. You bury him by the road. The supplies are salvageable but the man is not.
**COMPANION SELECTION**
Choose up to 2 companions (if available):
**Lysander** — +2 to your first attack. 'They are still close. I can smell the draconian.'
**Mira** — Heals Dorren automatically in Round 1. Essential if you lack healing.
**Serath** — Scouts the perimeter. Warns you of reinforcements (prevents surprise in Round 3).
**Sera** — Marks the first target. +2 to your second attack roll.
**Aldric** — Shield wall. Solid. Reliable. Angry about the dead guard.
**ENEMY STAT BLOCKS (Pen & Paper)**
**Baaz Draconian (2):** AC 13, HP 18, Speed 30ft. Scimitar +4 (1d6+2). Claw +4 (1d4+2). Death Throes: on death, turns to stone, weapon stuck (DC 11 STR to free). Disguise Self (used to look human at distance).
**Human Deserter:** AC 12, HP 11, Speed 30ft. Shortsword +3 (1d6+1). Light crossbow +3 (1d8+1, 80/320). Cowardly: flees at half HP or if both draconians fall. Surrender possible (Persuasion DC 11).
**INVESTIGATION CHECK (before or during combat):** Roll 1d20 + INT modifier | DC 12
**SUCCESS:** Hidden under the cart's false bottom — a Potion of Healing and 2d4 stl. Dorren was smuggling it. He will not mind if it saves his life.
**FAILURE:** You do not find the hidden compartment.
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 1
They are still here. That is the first wrong thing — whoever attacked the caravan should have taken what they wanted and gone. Instead, they are standing over the wreckage as if waiting. When you come around the cart, you understand why. They were not robbing the caravan. They were using it as bait.
Two draconians. You have read the reports but the reports did not prepare you for the reality: seven feet tall, scaled, the wings vestigial but the claws very real. They are wearing scavenged armour and their eyes have the flat, predatory patience of things that are confident in what they are. Between them: a human. Deserter's gear — Solamnic-issue mail with the tabard torn off. He has the look of a man who made a choice six months ago and has been living inside the consequences ever since.
The draconians see you and do not react with surprise. They react with hunger.
**YOUR ATTACK:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC = target's AC (Baaz: 13, Deserter: 12)
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage.

**IF LYSANDER IS PRESENT:** +2 to this attack. He moves beside you like a shadow with a sword.
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one:**
**Aldric:** A) Flank (+1 next attack) | B) Shield Wall (+1 AC) | C) Aggressive Strike (1d8+2, takes 1d4)
**Sera:** A) Scout Shot (1d6 dmg) | B) Mark Target (+2 next attack) | C) Disengage Cover (safe retreat)
**Mira:** A) Heal Dorren (automatic stabilize + 1d6+2) | B) Bandage Dorren (stabilize 1 HP) | C) Herbal Boost (+1 save)
**Serath:** A) Intel (+2 one attack) | B) Dagger Strike (1d4+2) | C) Feint (enemy -1 next attack)
**Lysander:** A) Combat Instruction (+2 attack) | B) Veteran Strike (1d10+3) | C) Guard (takes next hit)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):**
• (Paladin) Cast Bless: +1d4 to attacks and saves for up to 3 targets, Concentration, lasts entire combat. 1 spell slot.
• (Paladin) Divine Smite: Bonus action after hitting with a melee weapon. 2d8 radiant (+1d8 vs Fiends/Undead). Once per turn. 1 spell slot (or free 1/long rest at level 2+). *Draconians recoil from radiant damage.*
• (Paladin) Lay on Hands: Heal self or Dorren. Bonus action.
• (Paladin) Cure Wounds: Heal Dorren 2d8+CHA. 1 spell slot. Replaces attack.
• (Fighter) Action Surge: Additional Action this round (except Magic). 1/short or long rest.
• (Fighter) Second Wind: 1d10+[level] HP. Bonus action. 2 uses/long rest, regain 1/short rest.
• (Any) Drink Potion of Healing: 2d4+2 HP. Bonus action.
• (Any) Use Bandage on Dorren: Stabilize at 1 HP. Replaces attack.
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 1
The first Baaz attacks with its claws — not the scimitar. The claws. It wants you to understand what it is. The second flanks, moving with a speed that the armour should prevent but doesn't. The deserter hangs back, crossbow raised, and fires.
**DEFENSE ROLL (Baaz claw):** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 14
**SUCCESS:** Deflected. The claws scrape armour.
**FAILURE:** 1d4+2 slashing damage. The claw is filthy — describe the sensation.

**DEFENSE ROLL (Crossbow):** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 13
**SUCCESS:** The bolt goes wide.
**FAILURE:** 1d8+1 piercing damage.
*Dorren has 2 rounds remaining if not healed.*
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 2
The draconian you hit is bleeding something that is not quite blood — thicker, darker, and it steams when it hits the cold ground. It does not slow down. It gets angrier. The scimitar comes out now, held in a grip that says the claws were an introduction and this is the real conversation.
The deserter is retreating, step by step. He does not want to be here. His hands are shaking. He aimed for your head and hit the cart behind you. He is not a soldier anymore — he is a man with a crossbow and no courage left.
**PERSUASION CHECK (optional, replaces attack):** Roll 1d20 + CHA modifier | DC 11
Target the deserter. 'Drop it. Surrender. You know how this ends.'
**SUCCESS:** The deserter drops the crossbow. Falls to his knees. He is done. One less enemy. (+1 ROSE if you accept the surrender, +1 CROWN if you take him prisoner for trial.)
**FAILURE:** He fires again. Fear makes his aim worse but luck is not guaranteed.
**YOUR ATTACK:** (if not using Persuasion)
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC = target's AC
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage.
**BAAZ DEATH THROES:** If a Baaz drops to 0 HP, it turns to stone. Your weapon is stuck in the stone corpse. DC 11 STR check to free it, or draw a backup weapon.
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one:** (same options as Round 1)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):** (same options as Round 1)
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 2
The remaining Baaz presses the attack. It is stronger than it looks, and it looks very strong. The scimitar comes from the left — a feint — then the claw from the right, aimed at the gap between your breastplate and pauldron. It knows where armour fails. It has done this before.
**DEFENSE ROLL:** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 14
**SUCCESS:** You read the feint. Block the claw. Hold your ground.
**FAILURE:** 1d6+2 slashing damage. The scimitar finds the gap.

**IF DESERTER DID NOT SURRENDER:** Another bolt. DC 13 DEX save or 1d8+1 piercing.
*Dorren has 1 round remaining if not healed. His breathing is getting worse.*
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 3
The fight has the quality of something that needs to end. The road is dark and the draconian smells of copper and sulfur and its eyes have not blinked once. You are learning that the things you trained to fight are real and they do not care about your training — they care about whether you can survive the next ten seconds.
**IF SERATH IS NOT PRESENT:** Movement at the treeline. A third shape — another draconian, drawn by the noise, emerging from the south. It will arrive in 2 rounds if you do not end this now.
**IF SERATH IS PRESENT:** 'Movement south. We have two minutes before company arrives. Finish this.' Serath's warning gives you time but not much.
**YOUR ATTACK — End it.**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC = target's AC
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage. *If this kills the last Baaz, the stone death throes are your signal to move.*
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one:** (same options)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):** (same options)
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 3
The draconian makes its final play — a lunging thrust aimed at your chest, all weight behind it, the kind of attack that sacrifices defence for the chance to end you.
**DEFENSE ROLL:** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 15 (lunging attack, +1 DC)
**SUCCESS:** You sidestep. The blade buries in the cart. The draconian is overextended.
**FAILURE:** 1d6+3 slashing damage. The lunge has power behind it.
AFTER THE FIGHT
The last draconian turns to stone. The statue steams in the cold air and you will remember that sound — the hissing of hot rock meeting night dew — for the rest of your life. The deserter is on his knees or dead or fled. The road is quiet again, the way roads are quiet after violence — not peaceful, just emptied.
Dorren, if he is alive, is staring at you with the expression of a man who has just been given back the remainder of his life and has not yet decided what to do with it.

**SEARCH THE FALLEN (Optional — moral choice)**
The draconians are stone. The deserter's gear is scattered. You could search them.
Aldric is looking at the dead merchant's guard. He does not look at you.

**A) Search the bodies.** (-1 ROSE or -1 CROWN. Loot Roll below.)
**B) Leave them. The road has taken enough.** (+1 ROSE or +1 CROWN. No loot.)

**LOOT ROLL (if searched):** Roll 1d20.
1-5: Nothing useful. Draconian gear crumbles with the stone.
6-10: 1d4 stl from the deserter's belt pouch. 1 day rations (hardtack and dried meat).
11-14: 2d4 stl + a mundane item (whetstone, 2 days rations, a battered compass).
15-17: 3d4 stl + Potion of Healing from the deserter's kit. 2 days rations.
18-19: 4d4 stl + a quality item (the deserter's shortsword is well-made, or a map of Dragon Army patrol routes). 3 days rations.
20: 5d4 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL. 3 days rations.
**RARE ITEM ROLL (only on natural 20 above):** Roll 1d20.
1–19: Quality mundane item (the deserter's mail is Solamnic-issue, AC 16, good condition).
20: MAGIC ITEM — +1 Shield. The deserter took it when he left. The rose emblem has been scratched off but the enchantment remains.
**THE DEAD GUARD (Optional — NON-COMBATANT)**
The guard is still on the road. His sword is in its scabbard. He never drew it. His eyes are open. He was a man with a job and the job killed him and his employer is twenty feet away bleeding out from a compound fracture. He has a belt pouch, a water skin, and a locket around his neck.

**A) Search the guard's body.** You are searching the body of a man who was not your enemy. He was protecting a merchant on a road that should have been safe. See **Looting the Dead — Non-Combatant** penalties in Combat Reference. (-2 ROSE, -2 CROWN. 1d4 stl + 2 days rations + the locket contains a portrait of a woman and a child.)
Aldric's voice, quiet: 'He was on our side.' It is not an accusation. It is worse — it is a fact.
**B) Leave him.** (+1 ROSE. The guard deserves better than this road. You cannot give him better. You can give him the dignity of not being searched.)

**CARAVAN WRECKAGE:** Investigation DC 12. (No moral penalty — legitimate scouting.)
**SUCCESS:** Stash Loot Roll:
1-5: Spoiled supplies. Nothing salvageable.
6-10: Supplies (rations, bandages, 1d4 stl).
11-14: 2d6 stl + mundane item (iron nails, salt — trade goods).
15-17: 3d6 stl + Potion of Healing (hidden in cargo).
18-19: 4d6 stl + quality item (merchant's ledger — intel on Dragon Army supply disruption).
20: 5d6 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL.
**MERCHANT'S STRONGBOX:** Visible in the wreckage. Contains 3d6 stl. *Taking it is theft — Dorren's property, whether he lives or dies.* (-2 ROSE, -2 CROWN. Aldric will not speak to you for a scene. Caeron will hear of it.)
**NPC GRATITUDE — MERCHANT DORREN** (if healed)
Dorren cannot stand but he can grip your hand. He grips it like a man holding a rope over a cliff. 'I will be in Vogler,' he says. 'If I have anything left when I get there, it is yours.'

**ROLL:** Persuasion (+[bonus]) | CHA | DC 10

**10–12 (Modest):** 1d4 stl. 'It is all I have on me. I am sorry.'
**13–15 (Grateful):** 2d4 stl and a promise: 10% discount at his Vogler shop.
**16–18 (Deeply Grateful):** 3d4 stl, the shop discount, and a Potion of Healing from his personal stash. 'I was saving it. You are the reason I do not need it.'
**19+ (Indebted):** 4d4 stl, the discount, and a merchant's ring (10 stl value, or +1 to Persuasion checks with merchants — 'Tell them Dorren sent you').
**FAILURE (1–9):** Dorren has nothing. He is a merchant with no goods and no money on a road that almost killed him. He looks at you with wet eyes. 'I will find a way to repay you.' .
**AFTER THE FIGHT — UPDATE YOUR SHEET**
Record current HP. Mark any used: Second Wind, Action Surge, spell slots,
Lay on Hands pool, bandages, potions consumed.
Update gold if loot was found. Record any items gained.
Note if Dorren was saved (affects Scene 70C_SHOP).
**Short rest available:** Recover Action Surge, regain 1 Second Wind use. HP: spend Hit Dice.
Rewards: 50 XP.

choices.text: Return to camp. Report what you found

44C.title: THE RUINS AT NIGHT
44C.location: South Road — Old Watchtower Ruins, Night
prose: You wait. Aldric waits with you because that is what Aldric does — he does not question it, he simply crouches beside you in the dark and matches your breathing to the silence. The bootprints in the fire pit were military. Hobnailed. Heavier than refugee sandals. And they went deeper into the ruin, toward the collapsed cellar that the garrison maps do not show.
