The moon is a sliver. The wind carries the smell of old stone and something else — smoke, very faint, from below the ruin. Someone has a fire in the cellar. Someone who does not want to be seen from the road.

You hear the voice before you see the light. Not Common. Not Solamnic. A language that clicks and hisses and makes the hair on your arms stand up. Then: a child's voice. Small. Frightened. Speaking Common in fragments: 'Please. I want to go home. Please.'

boxes.text:
**DRAGON ARMY SUPPLY CACHE:** Investigation is not needed — the crates are right there. Stash Loot Roll (no moral penalty — enemy supplies):
1-5: Empty crates. They already moved the good stuff.
6-10: Supplies (rations, crude maps, 1d4 stl).
11-14: 2d6 stl + mundane item (Dragon Army patrol schedule, signal flags).
15-17: 3d6 stl + a useful item (Potion of Healing, military-grade rope, antitoxin).
18-19: 4d6 stl + quality item (Dragon Army officer's spyglass, coded orders — Caeron will want these).
20: 5d6 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL.

**SEARCH THE BODIES (Optional — moral choice):**
The soldiers and the Kapak remains. Searching Dragon Army dead is enemy intelligence, but the Kapak's acid makes it unpleasant.

**A) Search them.** (-1 CROWN. Loot Roll below.)
**B) Leave them. You have what you came for.** (+1 CROWN. No loot.)

**LOOT ROLL (if searched):** Roll 1d20.
1-5: Nothing. The Kapak acid dissolved most of it.
6-10: 1d4 stl from soldier belt pouches. 1 day rations.
11-14: 2d4 stl + mundane item (a locket with a portrait — the soldier was someone's son). 2 days rations.
15-17: 3d4 stl + Potion of Healing (the soldiers had supplies the Kapak did not share). 1 day rations.
18-19: 4d4 stl + Kapak venom vial (single-use: +1d4 poison damage on next weapon attack). 2 days rations.
20: 5d4 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL. 3 days rations.

**COMBAT MODE**
**Pen & Paper:** Use stat blocks below. Run the encounter at your table, then skip to Victory: The Kapak dissolves in its own acid. The soldiers are down. The ruins are yours.
Fallen (0 HP): Your companions hold the line while Mira stabilises you. 1 HP, one exhaustion.
**AFTER THE FIGHT**.
**Narrative Mode:** Follow the rounds below. Roll when prompted.
**INJURED NPC — REFUGEE CHILD LINNA**
Age 8. Dehydration + fever. Captured by Dragon Army foragers using the ruins as a supply cache.
**Heal her:** Mira (if present, automatic — herbs + care), Paladin Cure Wounds (1 spell slot), Paladin Lay on Hands (from pool), Bandage + water (stabilize, but fever needs herbs or magic), Potion of Healing (2d4+2 HP, cures fever).
*Record any resources spent.*
**If healed:** Linna recovers. She is returned to the refugee column or carried north. She appears in Vogler (Scene 70B) — she and her family recognize you..
**If not healed:** Linna survives the night but the fever worsens. She reaches Vogler but is bedridden. The family does not know who found her.
**COMPANION SELECTION**
Aldric is with you automatically. Choose up to 2 additional companions:
**Vael** — Reveals the Kapak's poison ability before combat. 'The one with the dark scales — its blade is coated. Do not let it cut you.'
**Thessaly** — +1 to all saving throws vs poison. She identified the antidote herbs in her research. Also +1 ROSE after the fight (she documents Linna's rescue).
**Mira** — Heals Linna automatically. Carries herbs that break the fever.
**Sera** — Stealth approach. If present, your Stealth DC is reduced by 2.
**Serath** — Knows Dragon Army patrol patterns. Warns you of timing: 'We have fifteen minutes before the next rotation.'
**ENEMY STAT BLOCKS (Pen & Paper)**
**Human Soldier (2):** AC 14 (chain shirt + shield), HP 14, Speed 30ft. Longsword +4 (1d8+2). Javelin +4 (1d6+2, 30/120). Formation: +1 AC when adjacent to another soldier.
**Kapak Draconian:** AC 14 (natural), HP 26, Speed 30ft. Poisoned Shortsword +5 (1d6+3 + DC 11 CON save or 1d6 poison and poisoned condition for 1 round). Claw +5 (1d4+3). Lick Blade (bonus action, re-poisons weapon). Death Throes: dissolves into acid pool (5ft radius, 2d4 acid damage, DC 12 DEX to avoid).
**STEALTH APPROACH:** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 14 (DC 12 if Sera present)
**SUCCESS:** Surprise round. You attack before they know you are there. One free attack with advantage.
**FAILURE:** They hear you. No surprise. The Kapak hisses and draws its blade. The soldiers form up. Linna screams.
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 1
The cellar is lit by a single low fire. Three figures: two humans in patchwork armour — Dragon Army regulars, not elite, the kind that hold supply lines and guard things that matter — and something else. The Kapak is crouched by the far wall, running its tongue along a short blade with the slow, deliberate care of a thing that is tasting its own poison. Its scales are dark, almost black, and its eyes reflect the firelight like a cat's.
Linna is tied to a support beam. She is small and filthy and her eyes are enormous. She sees you and her mouth opens but no sound comes out. She has learned that sound brings attention and attention brings pain.
**IF STEALTH SUCCEEDED:** They do not see you. The moment is yours. Take it.
**YOUR ATTACK:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC = target's AC (Soldiers: 14, Kapak: 14)
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus. (Advantage if stealth succeeded — roll twice, take higher.)
**ON HIT:** Roll damage.

**IF VAEL IS PRESENT:** 'The Kapak's poison lasts one round. If it cuts you, disengage and let it fade. Do not panic.' (You know to expect the poison — tactical advantage.)
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one:**
**Aldric:** A) Flank (+1 next attack) | B) Shield Wall — blocks the cellar entrance (+1 AC, enemies cannot flee) | C) Aggressive Strike (1d8+2, takes 1d4)
**Vael:** A) Flanking Shot (1d6 dmg) | B) Honest Assessment — reveals Kapak's remaining HP | C) Cover Fire (+1 AC)
**Thessaly:** A) Encourage (+1 next roll) | B) Document (+1 ROSE after fight) | C) Distraction — she calls out in Draconic, the Kapak hesitates (enemy skips next attack)
**Mira:** A) Rush to Linna — begins healing immediately | B) Herbal Boost (+1 save vs poison) | C) Bandage Linna (stabilize)
**Sera:** A) Scout Shot (1d6 dmg) | B) Mark Target (+2 next attack) | C) Block retreat route (enemies cannot flee)
**Serath:** A) Intel (+2 one attack) | B) Dagger Strike (1d4+2 from the shadows) | C) Feint (enemy -1 next attack)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):**
• (Paladin) Cast Bless: +1d4 to attacks and saves for up to 3 targets, Concentration, lasts entire combat. 1 spell slot.
• (Paladin) Divine Smite: Bonus action after hitting with a melee weapon. 2d8 radiant (+1d8 vs Fiends/Undead). Once per turn. 1 spell slot (or free 1/long rest at level 2+).
• (Paladin) Lay on Hands: Heal self or Linna. Bonus action.
• (Paladin) Cure Wounds: Heal Linna 2d8+CHA. 1 spell slot. Replaces attack.
• (Fighter) Action Surge: Additional Action this round (except Magic). 1/short or long rest.
• (Fighter) Second Wind: 1d10+[level] HP. Bonus action. 2 uses/long rest, regain 1/short rest.
• (Any) Drink Potion of Healing: 2d4+2 HP. Bonus action.
• (Any) Use Antitoxin: Advantage on poison saves for 1 hour. Bonus action.
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 1
The Kapak moves first. It is faster than the humans, faster than something that size should be, and it comes at you low — beneath your guard, blade leading. The poison on the shortsword glistens in the firelight, wet and dark.
**DEFENSE ROLL (Kapak):** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 15
**SUCCESS:** You evade. The blade passes close enough to smell — bitter, chemical, wrong.
**FAILURE:** 1d6+3 slashing damage. **POISON:** CON save DC 11. **Failure:** +1d6 poison damage and poisoned (disadvantage on attacks and ability checks) until end of your next turn. **Success:** Half poison damage, not poisoned.
**IF THESSALY IS PRESENT:** +1 to CON save. She told you what to expect.

**DEFENSE ROLL (Soldier):** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 14
**SUCCESS:** The longsword clangs off your armour.
**FAILURE:** 1d8+2 slashing damage.
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 2
The cellar is too small for this. The fire throws shadows that multiply everyone — you are fighting six enemies instead of three, or that is what your eyes tell you. The Kapak licks its blade again, a gesture that is both practical and theatrical, and the soldiers press you toward the wall. They are trained. Not well, but enough. Formation fighting: one attacks while the other defends.
Linna has her eyes closed. She is not watching. She has decided that if she cannot see, it is not happening.
**ATHLETICS CHECK (optional, replaces attack):** Roll 1d20 + STR modifier | DC 13
Cut Linna's bonds. She can crawl to safety behind Aldric's shield wall.
**SUCCESS:** Linna is free. She runs behind the nearest cover. If Mira is present, Mira pulls her to safety.
**FAILURE:** The bonds are thick rope. You need another round or a blade.
**YOUR ATTACK:** (if not freeing Linna)
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC = target's AC
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage.
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one:** (same options as Round 1)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):** (same options as Round 1)
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 2
The second soldier circles. He is trying to get behind you — standard pincer, textbook, and you know it is textbook because you have practised defending against it and the knowing does not make it less dangerous. The Kapak hangs back, letting the soldiers do the work, waiting for an opening. It is the smart one.
**DEFENSE ROLL (Soldier flanking):** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 15 (flanking, +1 DC)
**SUCCESS:** You turn to meet the flank. Textbook defence against textbook attack.
**FAILURE:** 1d8+2 slashing damage from behind. The formation works.

**IF ALDRIC CHOSE SHIELD WALL:** The flank fails. Aldric blocks the cellar entrance. DC remains 14.
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 3
The Kapak decides it has waited long enough. It makes a sound — not a roar, not a hiss, something between — and charges. The soldiers press from both sides. This is the moment the fight tips: either you break them here or the cellar becomes a trap with you inside it.
**YOUR ATTACK — Everything you have.**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC = target's AC
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage.

**KAPAK DEATH THROES:** If the Kapak drops to 0 HP, it dissolves into a pool of acid (5ft radius). **DEX save DC 12** or take 2d4 acid damage. *Move or be burned.*
**MEDICINE CHECK (optional):** Roll 1d20 + WIS modifier | DC 12
Treat Kapak poison on yourself or a companion. Requires herbs or antitoxin.
**SUCCESS:** Poisoned condition removed. The bitterness fades.
**FAILURE:** The poison runs its course naturally (1 round remaining).
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one:** (same options)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):** (same options)
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 3
What remains fights or breaks. The soldiers, if alive, look at each other — the look of men calculating whether dying here is worth what they are being paid. The Kapak does not calculate. It fights until it cannot.
**DEFENSE ROLL:** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 14
**SUCCESS:** You hold.
**FAILURE:** 1d6+3 damage (Kapak) or 1d8+2 damage (soldier).
AFTER THE FIGHT
The Kapak is dead — a pool of steaming acid where a living thing used to be. The soldiers are dead or gone. The cellar smells of acid, blood, and the musty dampness of old stone. The fire is still burning and by its light you can see the supply cache: crates marked with Dragon Army insignia, stacked against the far wall.
Linna is shaking. She is eight years old and she has been tied to a beam in a cellar with a Kapak draconian for what she will later say was 'a long time,' which could mean two hours or two days. She does not cry. She has gone past crying into the quiet place that children go when the world has exceeded their capacity to process it.

**DRAGON ARMY SUPPLY CACHE:** Investigation is not needed — the crates are right there. Stash Loot Roll (no moral penalty — enemy supplies):
1-5: Empty crates. They already moved the good stuff.
6-10: Supplies (rations, crude maps, 1d4 stl).
11-14: 2d6 stl + mundane item (Dragon Army patrol schedule, signal flags).
15-17: 3d6 stl + a useful item (Potion of Healing, military-grade rope, antitoxin).
18-19: 4d6 stl + quality item (Dragon Army officer's spyglass, coded orders — Caeron will want these).
20: 5d6 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL.
**RARE ITEM ROLL:** Roll 1d20.
1–19: Quality mundane item (Dragon Army officer's cloak, +1 to Stealth in wilderness — good camouflage).
20: MAGIC ITEM — +1 Chain Mail. Solamnic make, stripped from a fallen knight. The rose is still visible. Wearing it feels like a responsibility.

**SEARCH THE BODIES (Optional — moral choice):**
The soldiers and the Kapak remains. Searching Dragon Army dead is enemy intelligence, but the Kapak's acid makes it unpleasant.

**A) Search them.** (-1 CROWN. Loot Roll below.)
**B) Leave them. You have what you came for.** (+1 CROWN. No loot.)

**LOOT ROLL (if searched):** Roll 1d20.
1-5: Nothing. The Kapak acid dissolved most of it.
6-10: 1d4 stl from soldier belt pouches. 1 day rations (Dragon Army field bread, stale but edible).
11-14: 2d4 stl + mundane item (a locket with a portrait — the soldier was someone's son). 2 days rations.
15-17: 3d4 stl + Potion of Healing (the soldiers had supplies the Kapak did not share). 1 day rations.
18-19: 4d4 stl + Kapak venom vial (single-use: +1d4 poison damage on next weapon attack). 2 days rations.
20: 5d4 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL. 3 days rations.
**KAPAK VENOM VIAL** (if found): Single use. Apply to weapon as a bonus action. Next successful hit deals +1d4 poison damage. The vial is small, black, and warm to the touch. Using it is not forbidden. It is not honoured either.
**NPC GRATITUDE — LINNA**
Linna is a child. She has nothing to give you. She presses her face into your tabard and holds on and does not let go for a very long time. You can feel her heartbeat through the cloth. It is fast and small.

+1 ROSE. No roll. Some debts are not paid in steel.

*Her family finds you in Vogler (Scene 70B). They offer a meal, a blanket, and 2d4 stl — everything they have. You may refuse the steel or accept it (no penalty — they insist).*
**AFTER THE FIGHT — UPDATE YOUR SHEET**
Record current HP. Mark any used: Second Wind, Action Surge, spell slots,
Lay on Hands pool, bandages, potions, antitoxin consumed.
Update gold if loot was found. Record any items gained.
Note if Linna was healed (affects Scene 70B recognition).
**Short rest available:** Recover Action Surge, regain 1 Second Wind use. HP: spend Hit Dice.
Rewards: 80 XP.

choices.text: Carry Linna back to camp. She weighs almost nothing

44_PEDDLER.title: THE SOUTH ROAD PEDDLER
44_PEDDLER.location: South Road — Camp, Edge of Firelight
prose: He has the look of a man who has been on the road longer than the road has been safe. A mule. A cart with a canvas cover patched in three places. A smile that has sold things to people who did not know they needed them. He introduces himself as Torvin and he does not explain why a merchant is travelling south when everyone with sense is travelling north. Perhaps he is brave. Perhaps he is foolish. Perhaps the margins are better where the danger is, and Torvin has calculated the risk.
'Solamnic steel,' he says, looking at your tabard. 'You lot always need something. Let me see what I have.'
He pulls back the canvas. The cart is better stocked than it has any right to be.
'Safe travels, Squire,' Torvin says, already repacking. 'Or whatever the opposite of safe is, with you lot. Purposeful travels.'

boxes.text:
**SHOP — SOUTH ROAD PEDDLER**
Browse the wares. Prices in steel pieces (stl). Check your character sheet for current gold.

| Item | Price | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| Bandage (1 use) | 1 stl | Stabilize at 1 HP |
| Potion of Healing | 5 stl | 2d4+2 HP, bonus action in combat |
| Rations (3 days) | 1 stl | Travel supplies |
| Antitoxin | 3 stl | Advantage on poison saves for 1 hour |
| Whetstone | 1 stl | +1 damage on next attack (single use) |
| Rope (50 ft) | 1 stl | Climbing, binding |
| Healer's Kit (10 uses) | 5 stl | Stabilize without Medicine check |
| Oil Flask (3) | 1 stl | Burn, lubricate, improvise |

**RARE STOCK ROLL (once per visit):**
Roll 1d20. Torvin reaches deeper into the cart.
1–12: Standard stock only. 'That is all I have. Honest.'
13–14: Uncommon — Greater Potion of Healing available (15 stl, 4d4+4 HP). 'Found it in a ruin. Intact. Miracle.'
15–16: Uncommon — Silvanesti Elven Chain available (100 stl, AC 14+DEX max 2, Stealth OK). 'From the eastern forests. Do not ask how.'
17: Uncommon — Kapak Salvage Dagger available (80 stl, +1 attack, once/day: DC 15 CON or 2d10 poison). Taken from a Dragon Army scout. The venom is theirs.
18: Rare — Habbakuk's Javelin available (120 stl, +1 attack, once/dawn: call lightning strike 4d6). 'The Blue Phoenix blessed this. Long ago.'
19: Rare — Paladine's Light Longsword available (200 stl, +2d6 radiant on hit, sheds light). The blade burns with holy fire. Dragon Army fears this.
20: Rare — Oath-Keeper Longsword available (150 stl, +1 attack, +1d6 radiant vs evil). A knight died in Ergoth, centuries ago. His blade found its way here. It knows you.

*Record purchases on your character sheet. Deduct steel spent.*

**OPTIONAL ROLL:** Insight (+1) | WIS | DC 13

**SUCCESS (13+):** Torvin is not just a merchant. Watch his eyes — every time a soldier passes, he counts. Fingers twitch against his belt, marking tallies he will write down later. The information flows somewhere. North, you think. Whether that makes him spy or survivor depends on who is asking.

**FAILURE (1-12):** He seems like a man making a living in hard times. If there is more to him, you do not see it.
*No dice? Flip a coin: Heads = pass | Tails = fail*

choices.text: Return to the fire

44_REPORT.title: THE FIRE AND THE RECKONING
44_REPORT.location: South Road — Camp, Evening After the Refugee Column
prose: The fire is the small, efficient kind that field exercises teach — enough light to see faces, not enough to see from a distance. The refugee column is behind you now, in every sense. What remains is the weight of it: thirty-one people, three children, the man with the barrel-stave splint who stood at attention for a tabard. You are learning that some things become heavier after you stop carrying them.

Aldric sits across from you, cleaning his blade with the particular focus of a man who is processing something by giving his hands work. He has not spoken since the column. After a while: 'I keep thinking about the children.' He says this to the blade, not to you.

**IF refugee_report IS SET:** You sent the runner. Protocol satisfied, conscience less so. The garrison will make a decision — probably the right one, eventually, after the proper forms are filled. The refugees are still at the waystation. Waiting. The children do not know what they are waiting for.

**IF refugee_move IS SET:** You moved them yourself. Thirty-one people are walking north because you decided they should be. No orders. No authorisation. Just a squire who looked at three children and made a choice. Caeron will have questions. You do not have answers yet — only the certainty that waiting would have been worse.

**IF refugee_defend IS SET:** You held the line. The scout patrol found you instead of the refugees, and by the time reinforcements arrived the column was already moving north under Sera's guidance. Your sword arm aches. There is draconian blood on your tabard that will not wash out. But thirty-one people are alive because you stood between them and what was coming.

**IF refugee_caeron IS SET:** You found Caeron first. He listened — really listened, not the performative attention of officers managing subordinates. Then he said three words: 'Show me. Now.' You showed him. He made decisions. The refugees are moving north under garrison escort. Proper. Legal. Slower than you would have liked, but alive.

Lord Caeron arrives at the fire and does what officers do — takes the report, asks the right questions, writes the answers in his small precise hand. Casualties: none. Contact: confirmed Dragon Army presence. Assessment: the south road is not safe for civilian movement.

Then he closes the report book and does something you have not seen before. He sits. Not at the fire — at the edge of it, half in the dark, the way people sit when they want to be present without being the centre. He looks south. He does not say anything about what he is looking at.

boxes.text:
**OPTIONAL SCENE — SERATH'S BARN**
If you have not yet encountered Serath: there is a figure in the barn at the edge of the camp clearing. Turn to Scene 36 (Wound and Trust) now. If you have already met Serath, skip this.

choices.text: Take the full report to the command tent. The chain of command will decide what happens next.
You already acted. The column is moving north. Time to face the consequences.
You held the waystation. The refugees had time. Now report what you found.
Present your assessment to Caeron directly. He is listening.
There is a peddler camped at the edge of the firelight. He has been following the column.
Smoke on the south road. Something is burning. Caeron nods once — investigate.
Find Thessaly. She was taking notes near the old ruins.

53_BREACH.title: THE WALL BREACH
53_BREACH.location: Castle Brightholm — South Wall, Deep Night
prose: The bell is not the sentry bell. It is the wall bell — the one you were told about in the first week, the one that means the garrison itself is under attack. It rings three times and each strike pushes the night a little further away from silence and a little closer to chaos.

The south wall has a weakness. Everyone knows about it — a section where the mortar has been crumbling since before you arrived, where the stones shift when the wind is strong. The engineers were supposed to shore it up last month. Last month, there was no Dragon Army two days south. Last month, there was time.
There is no time now. The breach is eight feet wide and the draconians are already through.

You arrive at the wall and the scene is this: torchlight, dust from the broken stone, the sound of armoured things moving fast, and Ser Aldous — the old knight, the chapel keeper, the man who taught you what the Measure means when it is not just words — standing in the breach with his sword drawn and blood on his ribs. A draconian blade found the gap in his mail. He is fighting anyway.

He will not last. You can see it in the way he favours his left side, in the way his sword arm is slowing. Two rounds, maybe three. After that, the breach is open and the garrison's south flank is exposed.

boxes.text:
**COMBAT MODE**
**Pen & Paper:** Use stat blocks below. Run the encounter at your table, then skip to Victory: The breach holds. The draconians fall back into the dark. Brightholm stands.
Fallen (0 HP): Aldric pulls you behind the wall. The breach is held without you. 1 HP, one exhaustion.
**AFTER THE FIGHT**.
**Narrative Mode:** Follow the rounds below. Roll when prompted.
**INJURED NPC — SER ALDOUS**
Draconian blade across the ribs. Deep cut, bleeding badly. Collapses in 2 rounds without healing.
**Heal him:** Paladin Cure Wounds (1 spell slot, heals 2d8+CHA), Paladin Lay on Hands (from pool), Mira (if present, stabilize + 1d6+2), Bandage (stabilize at 1 HP), Potion of Healing (2d4+2).
*Record any resources spent.*
**If healed:** Aldous survives. He presides over the Trials at dawn with full authority. +1 ROSE at the ceremony. He remembers what you did.
**If not healed:** Aldous collapses. A different knight presides over the Trials — competent, but without Aldous's gravitas. The ceremony feels incomplete. Aldous recovers eventually, but slowly.
**COMPANION SELECTION — 'GATHER YOUR PARTY'**
You have seconds. Choose 2 companions. The combination changes the fight.

**Aldric + Mira:** Aldric holds the breach, Mira heals Aldous. Solid. Defensive. The safe choice.
**Aldric + Sera:** Aldric holds, Sera picks targets. Aggressive. Fast. You need to heal Aldous yourself.
**Aldric + Lysander:** Two frontline fighters and a veteran. Devastating offence. No healer unless you are one.
**Mira + Sera:** Healing and range. You are the only melee fighter. The breach depends on you alone.
**Mira + Serath:** Mira heals, Serath exploits chaos. Unconventional. Effective if you are creative.
**Sera + Serath:** Scout and shadow. No healer. No shield wall. Fast, sharp, dangerous for everyone.
**Lysander + Mira:** Veteran muscle and healing. Lysander takes point. You support.
*Any other combination of available companions: use their standard action options.*
**ENEMY STAT BLOCKS (Pen & Paper)**
**Baaz Draconian — Wave 1 (2):** AC 13, HP 18, Speed 30ft. Scimitar +4 (1d6+2). Claw +4 (1d4+2). Death Throes: turns to stone, weapon stuck (DC 11 STR to free).
**Baaz Draconian — Wave 2 (2):** Same stats. Arrive at Round 3. Climbing through the rubble. They have been waiting for the first wave to create the opening.
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 1
The breach is full of dust and noise and the particular chaos of a fight in a confined space. The two Baaz are through the wall and pressing into the courtyard — one has engaged Aldous, the other is turning toward the armoury door. If it gets past, the interior is exposed.
Torchlight catches the draconians' scales — bronze, dull, scratched from the stone they broke through. Their eyes are the worst part. Flat. Patient. The eyes of things that are doing a job and are good at it.
Aldous parries a scimitar thrust and his knees buckle. Blood is soaking through his tabard on the left side. He does not make a sound. Knights do not, apparently, even when they should.
**YOUR ATTACK — Choose a target:**
**Baaz engaging Aldous** / **Baaz heading for the armoury door**

**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC 13 (Baaz AC)
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage.
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one for each companion:**

**Aldric:** A) Flank (+1 next attack) | B) Shield Wall — blocks the armoury door (+1 AC, second Baaz cannot pass) | C) Aggressive Strike (1d8+2, takes 1d4)
**Sera:** A) Scout Shot (1d6 dmg) | B) Mark Target (+2 next attack) | C) Cover Fire (+1 AC for you)
**Mira:** A) Rush to Aldous — begins healing (stabilize + 1d6+2) | B) Bandage Aldous (stabilize 1 HP) | C) Herbal Boost (+1 save)
**Serath:** A) Intel (+2 one attack) | B) Dagger from the shadows (1d4+2, target does not see her) | C) Feint (enemy -1 next attack)
**Lysander:** A) Combat Instruction (+2 attack) | B) Veteran Strike — cleaves into the nearest Baaz (1d10+3) | C) Guard — steps in front of Aldous, takes the next hit
**Thessaly:** A) Encourage (+1 next roll) | B) Document (+1 ROSE after) | C) Distraction — shouts from the wall, one Baaz turns (skips next attack)
**Vael:** A) Flanking Shot (1d6 dmg) | B) Honest Assessment (reveals enemy HP) | C) Cover Fire (+1 AC)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):**
• (Paladin) Cast Bless: +1d4 to attacks and saves for up to 3 targets, Concentration, lasts entire combat. 1 spell slot.
• (Paladin) Divine Smite: Bonus action after hitting with a melee weapon. 2d8 radiant (+1d8 vs Fiends/Undead). Once per turn. 1 spell slot (or free 1/long rest at level 2+). *The radiant light fills the breach. Aldous sees it. He knows what you are.*
• (Paladin) Lay on Hands: Heal self or Aldous. Bonus action.
• (Paladin) Cure Wounds: Heal Aldous 2d8+CHA. 1 spell slot. Replaces attack.
• (Fighter) Action Surge: Additional Action this round (except Magic). 1/short or long rest.
• (Fighter) Second Wind: 1d10+[level] HP. Bonus action. 2 uses/long rest, regain 1/short rest.
• (Any) Drink Potion of Healing: 2d4+2 HP. Bonus action.
• (Any) Use Bandage on Aldous: Stabilize at 1 HP. Replaces attack.
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 1
The Baaz at the armoury door turns its attention to you. It is between you and the interior and it knows it — the position gives it confidence. The scimitar comes fast, a horizontal slash aimed at your midsection.
**DEFENSE ROLL:** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 14
**SUCCESS:** Parried. The blade rings off yours.
**FAILURE:** 1d6+2 slashing damage.

**ALDOUS:** If not healed this round, Aldous takes another hit from the engaged Baaz. He has 1 round remaining before he collapses.
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 2
The breach dust is settling. The fight has found its rhythm — the ugly, grinding rhythm of combat in a space too small for it. You can hear the garrison alarm spreading through the castle behind you, shouts and running boots, but the reinforcements are not here yet. The south wall is yours to hold.
Aldous is on one knee. His sword is braced against the ground and he is using it like a crutch. The Baaz facing him sees the weakness and grins — they grin, you are learning, in a way that has nothing to do with joy and everything to do with anticipation.
**ATHLETICS CHECK (optional):** Roll 1d20 + STR modifier | DC 14
Brace the fallen stone. Narrow the breach. Fewer can get through at once.
**SUCCESS:** The breach is halved. Wave 2 enters one at a time instead of together. Significant tactical advantage.
**FAILURE:** The stone shifts but does not hold. The breach remains open.
**YOUR ATTACK:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC 13
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage. *If this kills a Baaz: DEATH THROES — it turns to stone. DC 11 STR to free your weapon, or draw backup.*
**PERCEPTION CHECK (free action):** Roll 1d20 + WIS modifier | DC 13
**SUCCESS:** You hear scraping on the outer wall. More coming. Round 3. You can prepare.
**FAILURE:** Wave 2 arrives without warning.
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one for each companion:** (same options as Round 1)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):** (same options)
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 2
The surviving Baaz commit. Both attack — one high, one low. Coordinated. The draconians fight as a unit and they are better at it than the goblins were. This is not raiding. This is soldiering.
**DEFENSE ROLL (primary):** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 14
**SUCCESS:** Blocked. Your training holds.
**FAILURE:** 1d6+2 slashing damage.

**DEFENSE ROLL (secondary, only if 2 Baaz remain):** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 14
**SUCCESS:** Deflected.
**FAILURE:** 1d4+2 claw damage. The second one got through.

**ALDOUS:** If not healed by end of Round 2, Aldous collapses. He is unconscious and begins death saves (3 failures = death, 3 successes = stable at 0 HP). Healing can still save him, but the window is closing.
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 3 — WAVE 2
**IF BREACH WAS NARROWED:** One Baaz squeezes through the gap. The second is stuck on the other side, trying to find another way. You face 1 new enemy this round.
**IF BREACH IS OPEN:** Two Baaz climb through the rubble simultaneously. You face 2 new enemies.
The second wave is fresh. Unwounded. They have been waiting in the dark while you spent yourself on the first pair, and they know it. The one in front has a notched scimitar and a claw scar across its face — a veteran. It has done this before.
**INTIMIDATION CHECK (optional, free action):** Roll 1d20 + CHA modifier | DC 12
**SUCCESS:** You roar. The sound fills the breach. One of the new Baaz hesitates — skips its first attack. The breach is full of Solamnic fury and the draconian was not expecting that.
**FAILURE:** They are not impressed. They are draconians. They have faced worse than you. (Or they think they have.)
**YOUR ATTACK — Hold the line.**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC 13
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage.
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one for each companion:** (same options)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):** (same options)
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 3
The fresh Baaz attacks with the eagerness of something that has been waiting and is now, finally, unleashed. The scimitar comes from above — a chopping blow meant to drive you to your knees.
**DEFENSE ROLL:** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 15 (fresh enemy, aggressive)
**SUCCESS:** You catch the blade on yours. Your arms ache. You hold.
**FAILURE:** 1d6+3 slashing damage. The fresh Baaz hits harder.

**IF SECOND WAVE 2 BAAZ IS PRESENT:** Additional attack.
**DEFENSE ROLL:** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 14
**FAILURE:** 1d6+2 additional damage.
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 4 (FINAL)
The garrison reinforcements are coming — you can hear the boots on stone, the shouted orders, Caeron's voice cutting through the chaos with the precision of a man who has trained for exactly this. But they are not here yet. You have one more round. One more round between the draconians and the interior of Castle Brightholm.
Everything hurts. Your arms are heavy. Your breath is short. The torchlight has the quality of light seen through exhaustion — too bright, slightly unreal. But you are standing. The breach is in front of you and nothing has gotten past.
**YOUR ATTACK — Last stand.**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC 13
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage. *This is the round that matters. Describe it.*
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one for each companion:** (same options)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):** (same options)
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 4
The draconians sense the reinforcements too. They fight with desperation now — not the calculated patience of the first round but the vicious last-effort of things that know the window is closing.
**DEFENSE ROLL:** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 15
**SUCCESS:** You hold. The line holds. The breach holds.
**FAILURE:** 1d6+3 slashing damage. But you do not fall. You do not step back.
Garrison soldiers arrive. Caeron is at the front. The draconians break — not out of cowardice but out of mathematics. The breach is held. The south wall stands.
AFTER THE FIGHT
The dust settles. The stone Baaz statues steam in the night air. The breach is being filled with rubble and timber by garrison engineers who arrived thirty seconds after the fight ended, which is both impressive and infuriating.
Aldous, if he is alive, is propped against the inner wall. He looks at you. He does not say anything for a long time. Then: 'I will remember this when the Trials come.' He means: you have already passed a trial that no ceremony can replicate.
If Aldous is unconscious, the infirmary team takes him. Mira goes with them, if she is here. The corridor feels emptier.
**No body search available.** The draconians turned to stone in the breach — the engineers are already building them into the wall. There is a grim poetry to it.
**NPC GRATITUDE — SER ALDOUS** (if healed)
Aldous does not pay you. That is not what this is.
He reaches into his belt and produces a small book — leather-bound, hand-copied, the pages soft with age. *The Rose Commentary.* A treatise on the Measure of the Rose written by a knight whose name you recognize from the chapel histories. Aldous has carried it for thirty years.

'You have earned this,' he says. 'Not because you fought. Because you chose to heal before you chose to strike.'

**Item gained: The Rose Commentary.** +1 to all Measure checks..
*This is not loot. This is inheritance.*
**AFTER THE FIGHT — UPDATE YOUR SHEET**
Record current HP. Mark any used: Second Wind, Action Surge, spell slots,
Lay on Hands pool, bandages, potions consumed.
Note if Aldous was healed (affects Scene 60 Trials).
**Short rest available:** Recover Action Surge, regain 1 Second Wind use. HP: spend Hit Dice.
Rewards: 100 XP.

choices.text: The breach is held. Return to the corridors. The night is not over yet

53_HUB.title: THE LONG NIGHT
53_HUB.location: Castle Brightholm — Corridors, Night After the News
prose: The castle does not sleep. You hear it in the way it breathes — doors that do not close all the way, footsteps at hours when the corridors should be empty, the persistent amber glow of candles that should have been doused two hours ago. The news has changed the air itself. Two thousand soldiers. Three days. Maybe two.

You walk the corridors because standing still feels like consent to fear, and moving feels like something you can control. The people you care about are each handling this in their own way, in their own corners of the garrison. You have time. The night is long when no one is sleeping.

boxes.text: **GATE: Track companion visits this night. Dawn does not come until you have spoken with at least three people about what is coming.**

choices.text: (If not yet visited) Go to your companions — Aldric is in the armoury, Mira is in the infirmary, Serath watches the south road
(If not yet visited) Stay in the great hall. Someone else stayed too
(If not yet visited) Find Thessaly. She has been writing since the briefing
(If not yet visited) Find Aldric in the armoury
(If not yet visited) Something is wrong about the timing. Investigate
(If not yet visited, Tariel in party) Find Tariel
(If not yet visited, Lysander in party) Find Lysander
(If not yet visited, Mira and Serath both present) The infirmary door is ajar. You hear voices inside
(If 3+ visits) The candles have burned to nothing. The sky outside is not black any more — it is the deep grey that comes before grey becomes blue. Dawn is close
The alarm bell — south wall! Draconians at the breach!
Smoke from the grain stores. Not cook-fire smoke. Something is wrong

53_HUB_MORN.title: THE DAWN BEFORE THE TRIALS
prose: You find yourself on the east battlements without entirely deciding to walk here. The sky is the colour of steel being heated — not yet light, but no longer dark. Below, the courtyard is already moving: grooms leading horses, the armourer's apprentice carrying bundles of fletching, a cart being loaded with supplies that say *march* more clearly than any order could.
Three days. Maybe two. And between now and then — the Trials. Caeron has decided to accelerate them. Whatever comes after, you will face it as a sworn squire or not at all.

You lean on the battlement stone. It is cold under your hands. South, beyond the treeline, there is nothing visible — but you know what is there. Two thousand soldiers learning your name the way a river learns a stone: by wearing it down. You are not worn down yet. The bell will ring soon. When it does, the Trials begin.

choices.text: Go to the Great Hall. Face the Trials
Make camp at the crossroads first. There is time before the bell.

53_SABOTAGE.title: THE GRAIN STORE FIRE
53_SABOTAGE.location: Castle Brightholm — Supply Wing, Deep Night
prose: The smell hits you before the sight does — not woodsmoke, not cooking, but the sharp chemical stink of lamp oil poured on grain. Accelerant. Someone is burning the garrison's food stores on the night before a march, and that is not an accident. That is sabotage.

The supply wing corridor is dark except for the orange glow leaking under the grain store door. You can hear it — the wet crackle of oil-soaked grain catching, and under it, the sound of movement. Footsteps. Not the panicked footsteps of someone who started a fire accidentally. The measured footsteps of people with a job.
You put your hand on the door. It is warm. Not hot yet. The fire is young. Whatever is happening in there, you have minutes, not hours.

**IF SERATH IS IN YOUR PARTY:** They appear beside you. They were already coming — they smelled the oil from the corridor. 'Dragon Army sabotage team,' they say. 'Standard doctrine. Two operatives, civilian clothes, prepositioned inside the target garrison weeks ago. They light the stores the night before the assault.' Their expression is flat. 'I know this because I trained them.'

**IF MIRA IS IN YOUR PARTY:** Mira rounds the corner at a run. 'I smelled the oil from the infirmary. The grain store—' She sees the glow under the door. Her face hardens. That grain store is the one she asked Isak for in Vogler. The one she was going to use for the field hospital. The concept is transferable. 'Those supplies are the garrison's future. If they burn, the march starts hungry.'

boxes.text:
**APPROACH — Choose how to enter:**

**A) Kick the door. Surprise them. Sword drawn.** Proceed to combat immediately. You have surprise — enemies do not act in Round 1.

**B) Stealth. Open the door quietly and assess.**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Stealth (+2) | DEX | DC 13
**SUCCESS:** You see them before they see you. Two figures in civilian clothes, one pouring oil on the grain sacks, the other holding a torch. A third — larger, armoured beneath a cloak — stands guard with a hand crossbow. You have one free round of attacks before they react. Proceed to combat with surprise.
**FAILURE:** The door creaks. The guard turns. The torch drops. Proceed to combat — no surprise for either side.

**C) Sound the alarm first, then enter.**
You shout for the watch. Help will arrive in 3 rounds. The saboteurs hear you and prepare. Proceed to combat — no surprise. Garrison reinforcements arrive after Round 3.

Victory: The saboteurs are down. The grain store is saved — or lost, depending on how fast you moved.
Fallen (0 HP): The garrison arrives to finish the fight. You wake with Mira over you. 1 HP, one exhaustion.
**AFTER THE FIGHT**

**GRAIN STORE SAVED:** The supplies survived. The march starts fed.. Caeron's assessment of you moves in a direction he does not discuss but you can see in the way he includes you in the next briefing.

**GRAIN STORE LOST:** The supplies are ash. The march starts hungry. All ration costs double for Act 4. Caeron says nothing. His silence is louder than criticism.

**SABOTEURS CAPTURED ALIVE:** . Under interrogation (Caeron handles this, not you), they reveal the Dragon Army's timeline. The march begins a day earlier. This is an advantage.

**INFILTRATOR CAPTURED ALIVE:** . The infiltrator carries a coded dispatch. Serath, if present, can decode it: the Dragon Army's order of battle for the Vogler assault. This intelligence carries into Act 5.

**IF SERATH USED COMMAND CODES:** . Serath stands in the smoke-filled grain store and looks at their hands. 'I trained people to do this,' they say. 'Now I am stopping people I trained.' The complexity of this is not lost on them. Or on you.

**IF MIRA DRAGGED GRAIN SACKS:** . The sacks she saved will become bandage material in Vogler. She already knows this. She has been thinking three steps ahead since the south road.

> **SEARCH THE SABOTEURS (Optional — moral choice)**
> The saboteurs carry coin and a coded message. The infiltrator's chain shirt is quality steel.
>
> **A) Search them.** (No moral penalty — enemy combatants engaged in sabotage against your garrison.)
> **Loot Roll:** Roll 1d20.
> 1-5: 1d4 stl. Cheap daggers.
> 6-10: 2d4 stl. The coded message (intelligence value).
> 11-14: 3d4 stl. Infiltrator's chain shirt (AC 13, lighter than chain mail).
> 15-17: 4d4 stl. Chain shirt + a vial of alchemist's fire (2d6 fire, thrown 20ft).
> 18-19: 5d4 stl. Above + a set of thieves' tools (useful for locked doors in future scenes).
> 20: 6d4 stl. All above + a Dragon Army officer's signet ring. Serath recognizes it: '*That is a captain's ring. This was not a routine sabotage. Someone important ordered this.*' .
>
> **B) Leave them for the watch.** (+1 CROWN. Proper procedure. Caeron approves.)

**AFTER THE FIGHT — UPDATE YOUR SHEET**
Record current HP. Mark any used: Second Wind, Action Surge, spell slots, Lay on Hands pool, bandages, potions consumed.
**Short rest available:** Recover Second Wind, Action Surge.
**XP:** 65 XP

**COMBAT MODE**
**Pen & Paper:** Use stat blocks below. Run at your table. Skip to AFTER THE FIGHT.
**Narrative Mode:** Follow rounds below. Roll when prompted.

**ENEMIES:**
**Saboteur (2):** AC 11 (civilian clothes), HP 9, Speed 30 ft. Dagger +3 (1d4+1). Oil Flask (thrown, 10 ft): target catches fire, 1d4 fire damage per round until extinguished (action). Desperate: will try to spread fire rather than fight if possible.
**Dragon Army Infiltrator:** AC 14 (hidden chain shirt under cloak), HP 22, Speed 30 ft. Shortsword +5 (1d6+3). Hand Crossbow +5 (1d6+3, 30/120). Multi-attack: 2 shortsword attacks. Slippery: advantage on saves vs. grapple and restrain. **WILL ATTEMPT TO FLEE if both saboteurs fall.**

**ENVIRONMENT:** The grain store is 30 ft x 20 ft. Grain sacks (half cover). Oil on the floor (5 ft area: DEX DC 12 or fall prone). The fire grows: Round 1 = small (no effect). Round 2 = spreading (disadvantage on Perception from smoke). Round 3 = dangerous (1d4 fire damage per round to anyone within 10 ft of grain sacks). Round 4+ = the grain store is lost.

**OBJECTIVE:** Kill or capture the saboteurs AND extinguish the fire before Round 4.
**EXTINGUISH:** Athletics DC 13 (smother with cloaks/blankets) or create water (Paladin spell, if available). Replaces your attack that round.
**ROUND 1 — FIRE AND SHADOW**

*Orange light. Smoke already thickening. The grain sacks are stacked seven high and three of them are burning, the oil turning them into wicks. The saboteurs move with the efficiency of people who have done this before — one pouring, one ready with the torch, the infiltrator turning toward the door with the hand crossbow already rising.*

**YOUR ATTACK:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll (+5) | DC 14 (Infiltrator AC) or DC 11 (Saboteur AC)
*Choose your target: the infiltrator is the threat. The saboteurs are spreading the fire.*

**HIT:** Deal weapon damage. If targeting a saboteur: one hit drops them (9 HP, they are not fighters).

**MISS:** Smoke and shadows. The grain store is not an arena — it is a burning room full of obstacles.

**COMPANION ACTION (choose 1):**
| Companion | Action A | Action B | Action C |
|-----------|----------|----------|----------|
| Serath (if present) | Engage infiltrator (+2 attack, they know the training) | Block escape route (infiltrator cannot flee) | Shout Dragon Army command codes (saboteurs hesitate — skip their action) |
| Mira (if present) | Grab blankets — prepare to smother fire next round | Drag grain sacks from the flames (reduce fire timer by 1 round) | Treat any burn wounds (heal 1d6+2) |
| Aldric (if present) | Flank (+1 attack) | Shield Wall (+1 AC) | Aggressive Strike (1d8+2, takes 1d4) |

**ENEMY ACTIONS:**
*Saboteurs:* If not engaged, they pour more oil. Fire advances one round on the timer. If engaged, they fight with daggers (desperate, sloppy, DC 11 defense).
*Infiltrator:* Hand crossbow at closest target. **Defense roll DC 15.** On hit: 1d6+3 piercing. Then draws shortsword.
**ROUND 2 — THE FIRE SPREADS**

*Smoke is real now. Your eyes sting. The grain sacks are fully ablaze on the east wall and the heat is pushing you back. You have two rounds before the entire store is lost. The infiltrator is between you and the fire, blade drawn, expression flat — the face of a professional who does not care about the grain or the garrison or anything except completing the mission and getting out alive.*

**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll (+5) | DC 14 (Infiltrator) or DC 11 (Saboteur)

**IF YOU HAVE NOT STARTED EXTINGUISHING:** The fire advances. One more round.

**OPTIONAL — EXTINGUISH FIRE (replaces attack):**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Athletics (+3) | STR | DC 13
**SUCCESS:** You smother a section of the fire. The timer pauses. The grain store can still be saved.
**FAILURE:** The oil reignites. The fire continues to advance.

**INFILTRATOR'S ATTACK:**
Multi-attack: 2 shortsword strikes.
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Defense roll | DC 15 (first strike)
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Defense roll | DC 15 (second strike)
Each hit: 1d6+3 piercing. The infiltrator is fast, trained, and does not fight fair.
**ROUND 3 — DECISIVE MOMENT**

*The east wall is fully ablaze. The smoke is at ceiling level and dropping. Your lungs burn. If the fire is not out by the end of this round, the grain store — and the garrison's food supply for the march — is gone.*

**IF INFILTRATOR STILL STANDING:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll (+5) | DC 14
**HIT:** Deal weapon damage. If this drops the infiltrator: they fall. The fight is over.
**(Paladin) DIVINE SMITE** available: +2d8 radiant. In the firelight, the divine light is almost indistinguishable from the flames. Almost.
**(Fighter) ACTION SURGE** available: second attack.

**IF FIRE NOT YET EXTINGUISHED:**
Someone must extinguish it this round or the stores are lost.
**If you chose option C (sounded alarm):** Garrison soldiers arrive with water buckets. Fire is automatically extinguished. The stores are saved.
**Otherwise:** Athletics DC 13 (you or a companion).

**INFILTRATOR FLEES (if able):**
If both saboteurs are down and escape route is not blocked, the infiltrator runs.
**OPTIONAL PURSUIT:** Athletics DC 14 to catch them. If caught: capture alive (+1 CROWN, intelligence value). If not caught: they escape into the night. Caeron will want to know about this.
Victory: The saboteurs are down. The grain store is saved — or lost, depending on how fast you moved.
Fallen (0 HP): The garrison arrives to finish the fight. You wake with Mira over you. 1 HP, one exhaustion.

**AFTER THE FIGHT**

**GRAIN STORE SAVED:** The supplies survived. The march starts fed.. Caeron's assessment of you moves in a direction he does not discuss but you can see in the way he includes you in the next briefing.

**GRAIN STORE LOST:** The supplies are ash. The march starts hungry. All ration costs double for Act 4. Caeron says nothing. His silence is louder than criticism.

**SABOTEURS CAPTURED ALIVE:** . Under interrogation (Caeron handles this, not you), they reveal the Dragon Army's timeline. The march begins a day earlier. This is an advantage.

**INFILTRATOR CAPTURED ALIVE:** . The infiltrator carries a coded dispatch. Serath, if present, can decode it: the Dragon Army's order of battle for the Vogler assault. This intelligence carries into Act 5.

**IF SERATH USED COMMAND CODES:** . Serath stands in the smoke-filled grain store and looks at their hands. 'I trained people to do this,' they say. 'Now I am stopping people I trained.' The complexity of this is not lost on them. Or on you.

**IF MIRA DRAGGED GRAIN SACKS:** . The sacks she saved will become bandage material in Vogler. She already knows this. She has been thinking three steps ahead since the south road.

> **SEARCH THE SABOTEURS (Optional — moral choice)**
> The saboteurs carry coin and a coded message. The infiltrator's chain shirt is quality steel.
>
> **A) Search them.** (No moral penalty — enemy combatants engaged in sabotage against your garrison.)
> **Loot Roll:** Roll 1d20.
> 1-5: 1d4 stl. Cheap daggers.
> 6-10: 2d4 stl. The coded message (intelligence value).
> 11-14: 3d4 stl. Infiltrator's chain shirt (AC 13, lighter than chain mail).
> 15-17: 4d4 stl. Chain shirt + a vial of alchemist's fire (2d6 fire, thrown 20ft).
> 18-19: 5d4 stl. Above + a set of thieves' tools (useful for locked doors in future scenes).
> 20: 6d4 stl. All above + a Dragon Army officer's signet ring. Serath recognizes it: '*That is a captain's ring. This was not a routine sabotage. Someone important ordered this.*' .
>
> **B) Leave them for the watch.** (+1 CROWN. Proper procedure. Caeron approves.)

**AFTER THE FIGHT — UPDATE YOUR SHEET**
Record current HP. Mark any used: Second Wind, Action Surge, spell slots, Lay on Hands pool, bandages, potions consumed.
**Short rest available:** Recover Second Wind, Action Surge.
**XP:** 65 XP

choices.text: The grain store is secure. Return to the corridors. The night is not over yet

62E.title: THE FIRST CAMP BOND
prose: The fire is low and the stars are out — Solinari full, Lunitari a red sliver on the horizon, and the kind of sky that only exists when you are far enough from any settlement that the darkness is real. The column has settled. Somewhere in the distance an owl is working. The sound is older than anything on this road.

You find yourself at the edge of camp with the particular restlessness that comes from being tired but not sleepy, the body done but the mind still turning over the day like a stone in water. The others are nearby. You can hear them — Aldric's quiet breathing, the scratch of Thessaly's pen, the careful sounds of Mira checking her supplies in the dark.

**IF SERA IS IN YOUR PARTY:** She materialises beside you without sound. 'You did not hear me. That is fine. Aldric did not hear me either. Caeron almost heard me, which is why I respect him.' She sits. 'The road south has fresh tracks. Two days old. Civilian, heading north fast. Whatever they were running from, it is behind us now.'

**IF VAEL IS IN YOUR PARTY:** She finds you. 'I have been thinking about what Caeron said — about the towers disappearing behind the hill. He watched them too. He did not think anyone noticed.' She looks south. 'I noticed.'

choices.text: Sit with Aldric. He is sharpening something that does not need sharpening
Find Thessaly. Her pen has stopped. She is staring at nothing
Walk the perimeter with Caeron. He is already walking it
Sleep. The road will be long tomorrow. Let the fire do the thinking

64B_WOLVES.title: THE PACK
prose: You wake to the sound of horses screaming. Not the nervous whinny of animals that have smelled something wrong — the full-throated shriek of prey animals that can see the predator. You are on your feet with your weapon in hand before your brain fully registers what your body already knows: the camp is under attack.

Wolves. Not the cautious grey wolves of the northern forests that avoid campfires and armoured columns. Dire wolves — massive, gaunt, ribs showing through matted fur, eyes catching the firelight like coins. The Dragon Army's advance has pushed them north, out of their hunting grounds and into territory where the only prey large enough to sustain a pack this size is horse. Or human.

The fire is low — banked coals, not flame. The darkness beyond the fire's reach is absolute. The wolves circle at the edge of the light, and you can count them by their eyes: six pairs, twelve points of amber, moving.

**IF ALDRIC IS IN YOUR PARTY:** He is already up. Sword drawn, shield on his arm, standing between the horses and the wolves with the posture of a man who has decided that nothing is getting past him. 'Six,' he says. 'The alpha is the large one on the left. Kill the alpha and the pack breaks.'

**IF SERA IS IN YOUR PARTY:** Sera is crouched by the fire, bow strung, an arrow nocked. She has been awake — she was on watch. 'They have been circling for an hour,' she says. 'Waiting for the fire to die. I was going to feed it, but—' She nods toward the treeline. 'I wanted to see how many.'

**IF MIRA IS IN YOUR PARTY:** Mira is with the horses, calming them with hands and voice. One of the pack horses has a gash on its flank — a wolf got close enough to slash before the screaming started. 'The horse will live,' she says. 'If we live.'

boxes.text:
**COMBAT MODE**
**Pen & Paper:** Use stat blocks below. Run at your table. Skip to AFTER THE FIGHT.
**Narrative Mode:** Follow rounds below. Roll when prompted.

**ENEMIES:**
**Dire Wolf Alpha:** AC 14, HP 30, Speed 50 ft. Bite +6 (2d6+4 piercing). Pack Tactics: advantage on attack if another wolf is within 5 ft of target. Knockdown: on hit, target makes DC 13 STR save or is knocked prone. **Retreats if reduced to half HP and 3+ pack wolves are dead.**
**Dire Wolf (5):** AC 14, HP 16, Speed 50 ft. Bite +5 (2d6+3 piercing). Pack Tactics. Knockdown DC 12.

*The pack attacks in waves: 2 wolves in Round 1, 2 more in Round 2, the alpha + 1 in Round 3. Killing the alpha before Round 3 causes remaining wolves to flee.*

**YOUR STATS:** Fighter: HP per sheet, AC per sheet, weapon per sheet. Paladin: same.

**COMPANION ACTIONS (choose 1 per round):**
| Companion | Action A | Action B | Action C |
|-----------|----------|----------|----------|
| Aldric | Flank (+1 attack) | Shield Wall (+1 AC) | Guard the horses (wolves cannot reach mounts) |
| Sera | Aimed Shot (1d8+3 at any wolf) | Mark Alpha (+2 to your attacks vs alpha) | Fire Arrow (lights camp perimeter, wolves -1 attack from light) |
| Mira | Heal (1d6+2, 1/scene) | Calm horses (prevents stampede) | Throw burning brand (1d4 fire + wolf must save DC 11 WIS or flee) |
| Lysander | Veteran Strike (1d10+3) | Guard flank (wolves cannot flank you) | Hunt the Alpha (Lysander engages alpha directly: 1d10+3 to alpha, takes 1d6) |
**ROUND 1 — FROM THE DARK**

*Two wolves break from the circle. They do not snarl, do not warn. They come from the dark like arrows — low, fast, silent until the moment of impact. One goes for the horses. One goes for you.*

**YOUR ATTACK:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll (+5 or per sheet) | DC 14 (Wolf AC)

**HIT:** Your blade catches the wolf mid-lunge. Deal weapon damage. The wolf yelps — a sound that is worse than a snarl because it is the sound of a predator discovering it is prey.

**MISS:** The wolf is faster than you expected. It slides past your blade.

**WOLF'S BITE:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Defense roll | DC 15 (Wolf bite +5)

**SUCCESS:** You turn the wolf's jaws on your blade or shield.

**FAILURE:** Take 2d6+3 piercing. The bite is not a slash — it is a clamp, and the wolf shakes. STR save DC 12 or be knocked prone. If prone, the second wolf attacks with advantage next round.
**ROUND 2 — THE PACK COMMITS**

*Two more wolves from the tree line. The circle has broken — the pack is committed now, all of them moving, the fire barely enough to keep the darkness from swallowing everything. You can hear them breathing. You can smell them — wet fur, blood, the musk of animals that have not eaten in days.*

**YOUR ATTACK:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll (+5 or per sheet) | DC 14

**(Paladin) OPTIONAL — DIVINE SMITE:** On hit, +2d8 radiant damage. The light pushes the shadows back. Wolves within 10 ft must make WIS save DC 12 or disengage for 1 round.

**(Fighter) OPTIONAL — ACTION SURGE:** Two attacks this round. The wolf pack does not expect this.

**WOLF ATTACKS (x2 if both still standing):**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Defense roll | DC 15 (first wolf)
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Defense roll | DC 15 (second wolf, if alive)
Each hit: 2d6+3 piercing + knockdown save.

**OPTIONAL — ANIMAL HANDLING (replaces attack):**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Animal Handling (+1) | WIS | DC 15
**SUCCESS:** You lock eyes with a wolf and something passes between you — not dominance, but understanding. The wolf breaks from the pack and does not return. One fewer enemy..
**FAILURE:** Wolves do not negotiate.
**ROUND 3 — THE ALPHA**

*The alpha comes last. It is the largest wolf you have ever seen — shoulder height with a warhorse foal, scarred across the muzzle, one ear torn. It has survived everything the wild has thrown at it and it is not afraid of fire or steel or you. It steps into the firelight and its eyes find yours and the challenge is absolute.*

*If the alpha has been killed early (Lysander's Hunt or a lucky hit), the remaining wolves scatter into the dark. The fight is over. Skip to AFTER THE FIGHT.*

**YOUR ATTACK (vs Alpha):**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll (+5 or per sheet) | DC 14 (Alpha AC)

**HIT:** Deal weapon damage. The alpha staggers but does not fall — it takes more than one hit to drop this animal.

**ALPHA'S BITE:**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Defense roll | DC 16 (Alpha bite +6)

**SUCCESS:** You turn the alpha's jaws. It circles, recalculates.

**FAILURE:** Take 2d6+4 piercing. STR save DC 13 or knocked prone. The alpha goes for the throat when you are down.

**IF ALPHA REACHES HALF HP (15 or less) AND 3+ WOLVES ARE DEAD:**
The alpha howls — long, low, the sound of a predator making a cost-benefit analysis. The remaining wolves break and scatter. The alpha backs into the darkness, watching you, and then it is gone.

**IF ALPHA IS KILLED:**
The pack scatters instantly. The darkness reclaims them. The fire crackles. The horses stop screaming. The silence that follows is the loudest thing you have ever heard.
Victory: The alpha retreats into the dark, dragging its wounded leg. The pack scatters. Camp is safe.
Fallen (0 HP): Your companions drive the wolves off while you bleed into the grass. 1 HP, one exhaustion.
**AFTER THE FIGHT**

Dawn comes grey and cold. The wolf bodies are at the edge of the firelight — gaunt, scarred, driven to desperation by an army that does not care what it displaces. There is no glory in this fight. There is survival, and there is the understanding that the Dragon Army's destruction extends beyond the people it targets.

**IF SERA IS IN YOUR PARTY:** She examines the alpha's tracks. 'They came from the south-east. Driven out of the Lemish border forests.' She pauses. 'The Dragon Army is burning the forests to deny cover. The wolves had nowhere left to go.' She says it without judgment, but her hand is on her bow and her face is the face of a ranger who has just watched the wild die a little more.

**IF MIRA IS IN YOUR PARTY:** The pack horse's gash needs stitching. Mira works on it as the sun rises, her hands steady, her voice low. 'Everything is fleeing north,' she says. 'The animals. The refugees. The farmers. We are all running from the same thing. The difference is we are running toward it.'

**LOOT — WOLF PELTS (optional):**
Dire wolf pelts are valuable — thick, warm, large enough to serve as blankets or trade goods.
**A) Skin the wolves.** (No moral penalty — they attacked you.) Gain: 1d4 dire wolf pelts (worth 2 stl each). Alpha pelt (worth 5 stl, or can be worn as a cloak: advantage on Intimidation vs beasts).
**B) Leave them.** The road is not a place for skinning.

**AFTER THE FIGHT — UPDATE YOUR SHEET**
Record current HP. Mark any used: Second Wind, Action Surge, spell slots, Lay on Hands pool, bandages, potions consumed.
**Short rest available** after breaking camp: Recover Second Wind, Action Surge.
**XP:** 70 XP

choices.text: Break camp. The road continues. The wolves were just the prelude

64D.title: MAKING CAMP
prose: The light fails faster this far south — the treeline cuts the horizon higher and the autumn sun drops behind it like a stone into water. Caeron calls the halt at a clearing that has been used before: fire-blackened stones in a ring, a water source thirty yards east, and the particular flatness of ground that has been slept on by enough people to lose its original character.

Making camp is ritual now. You know your role without being told — fire duty, perimeter stones, the particular arrangement of gear that means sleep is available within arm's reach of everything you might need in a hurry. Aldric handles the horses. Mira, if present, claims the flattest ground for the medical kit without discussion. The efficiency of it steadies something.

The smoke on the southern horizon is closer. Not dramatically — a mile, maybe two. But the quality has changed. Yesterday it was distant and theoretical. Tonight it is a fact you can smell on the wind when it shifts.
**IF THESSALY IS IN YOUR PARTY:** She has her map out, marking the refugees' direction and the smoke's position with the methodical precision of someone building a record they expect to need.

**IF SERATH IS IN YOUR PARTY:** They are watching the smoke with the flat attention of someone who has seen what makes it. They do not share what they are thinking. The way they check their blade says enough.

boxes.text:
**RATIONS — EVENING MEAL (Day 2)**
Garrison supplies are gone. You eat from your own pack tonight.
**Consume 1 ration.** Mark it off your character sheet.

**IF YOU HAVE NO RATIONS:**
You sit by the fire with an empty stomach and try not to look at other people eating.

**IF ALDRIC IS IN YOUR PARTY:** He notices before you say anything. He always notices. He breaks his bread in half and puts the larger piece on your knee without looking at you. 'Eat it before I change my mind.' A pause. 'You know, for someone who passed the garrison supply room every day, you are remarkably bad at planning.' He means: *I am worried about you and this is the only way I know how to say it.* **Aldric covers your meal. No ration consumed. Aldric cannot cover for you again.**

**IF MIRA IS IN YOUR PARTY (and Aldric is not, or Aldric already covered):** She produces a wrapped bundle — herbs, dried fruit, a flatbread she baked at Brightholm and has been carrying since. 'I made extra. I always make extra.' She looks at you with the quiet expectation of someone who heals people for a living and considers hunger a wound. 'Next time, pack properly. I am a healer, not a quartermaster.' **Mira covers your meal. No ration consumed. Mira cannot cover for you again.**

**IF SERA IS IN YOUR PARTY (and no one above can cover):** Sera drops a strip of dried venison in your lap without breaking stride. 'Found it on a scout. Do not ask which scout.' She is already walking away. Then, over her shoulder: 'That is the only time, Squire. Learn to count your supplies or learn to go hungry. I will not save you from arithmetic.' **Sera covers your meal. No ration consumed. Sera cannot cover for you again.**

**IF NO COMPANION CAN COVER:** You go hungry. Apply hunger penalty (see Combat Reference). The fire smells like other people's food.

*Record your current ration count on your character sheet.*

choices.text: Help Aldric with the horses. The work is quiet and good
Sit with the fire. Let the day settle
Ask Caeron about tomorrow's route. He has been studying the map

64E.title: THE BURNED FARMSTEAD
64E.location: South Road — Between the Refugees and Camp, Late Afternoon
prose: You smell it before you see it. Not the distant, theoretical smoke of the southern horizon — this is close, recent, the thick black smoke of things that should not be burning. Wood. Thatch. Something organic that you do not want to identify.

The farmstead is two hundred yards off the road, set back against a tree line. The barn is fully ablaze. The farmhouse roof is burning from the east end — the fire was set there first and is working its way west. A Dragon Army patrol is in the yard: three soldiers and something else, something taller, with scales that catch the firelight. An officer. The Bozak stands apart from the soldiers, watching the burn with the detached attention of a thing that is checking a task off a list.
From inside the barn: screaming. A woman's voice. A man's voice, lower, shouting something you cannot hear over the fire.
Caeron pulls the column to a halt. He looks at the farmstead. He looks at you. 'Squire,' he says. And the word means: *you decide. This is what the Measure is for.*

boxes.text:
**SEARCH THE FALLEN (Optional — moral choice):**
The soldiers are dead. The Bozak is bone fragments. The officer's satchel survived the explosion.

**Bozak officer's satchel:** No moral penalty — enemy intelligence. Contains: patrol routes, supply line maps, a letter in Draconic that Caeron will want to read. +1d6 stl.

**Soldier bodies:**
**A) Search them.** (-1 ROSE. Loot Roll below.)
**B) Leave them. You have done enough.** (+1 ROSE. No loot.)

**LOOT ROLL (if searched):** Roll 1d20.
1-5: Nothing. Dragon Army soldiers carry nothing personal. They were not allowed to.
6-10: 1d4 stl. Standard issue pay. 1 day rations (Dragon Army field pack).
11-14: 2d4 stl + mundane item (2 days rations, a signal whistle, a bedroll).
15-17: 3d4 stl + Potion of Healing (standard issue, one per squad). 2 days rations.
18-19: 4d4 stl + quality item (an officer's compass, worth 5 stl to the right buyer). 3 days rations.
20: 5d4 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL. 3 days rations.

**FARMSTEAD CELLAR:** Investigation DC 13. (No moral penalty — searching for survivors/supplies.)
**SUCCESS:** Stash Loot Roll:
1-5: Empty. The family had nothing worth hiding.
6-10: Preserved food, 1d4 stl in a clay jar.
11-14: 2d6 stl + Potion of Healing (Hanna's grandmother's recipe, same as Vogler).
15-17: 3d6 stl + quality item (a family sword, longsword, well-maintained).
18-19: 4d6 stl + a hidden strongbox (the family's life savings — taking this is not theft if you ask, but refusing it is +1 ROSE).
20: 5d6 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL.

**COMBAT MODE**
**Pen & Paper:** Use stat blocks below. Run the encounter at your table, then skip to Victory: The Bozak detonates in death — bone fragments scatter across the farmyard. The soldiers are down. The farm is yours.
Fallen (0 HP): Your companions hold the farmyard. You wake to Mira working on your wounds. 1 HP, one exhaustion.
**AFTER THE FIGHT**.
**Narrative Mode:** Follow the rounds below. Roll when prompted.
**INJURED NPCs — FARMER HANNA AND DARRIC**
**Hanna** (inside barn): Severe burns on arms and chest. Needs magic healing or she dies. Paladin Cure Wounds (1 spell slot), Paladin Lay on Hands (10+ HP from pool), Greater Potion of Healing, or Mira + herbs (stabilize + 1d6+2, but burns need magic to fully heal). Bandages alone are not enough — they stabilize but Hanna will lose the use of her hands.
**Darric** (inside barn): Dislocated shoulder, smoke inhalation. Medicine DC 14 + bandage, or Mira (automatic), or any healing spell/potion. Less critical but in pain.
*Record any resources spent.*
**If both healed:** Hanna and Darric appear in Vogler. Hanna's hands work. Darric volunteers for the defence..
**If only Darric healed:** They reach Vogler but Hanna cannot work. Darric is bitter but grateful..
**If neither healed:** They do not make it to Vogler. The road takes them.
**COMPANION SELECTION**
Full party is available. Caeron calls the intervention — this is not optional.
**Aldric** — Takes the Bozak head-on. Brave or foolish. Both.
**Mira** — Essential for the civilians. She will run for the barn the moment it is safe.
**Sera** — Ranged fire. Picks off soldiers from the treeline.
**Serath** — Knows Dragon Army patrol timing. 'We have ten minutes before the next squad.'
**Lysander** — Veteran assault. He has done this before. His face says he wishes he had not.
**Thessaly** — Documents. Witnesses. Her pen is shaking but it is moving.
**Vael** — Warns about the Bozak's spellcasting. 'It has Burning Hands and Lightning Bolt. Do not cluster.'

Choose up to 3 companions for combat. The rest assist with the barn rescue.
**ENEMY STAT BLOCKS (Pen & Paper)**
**Human Soldier (3):** AC 14 (chain shirt + shield), HP 16, Speed 30ft. Longsword +5 (1d8+3). Javelin +5 (1d6+3, 30/120). Formation: +1 AC when adjacent to another soldier.
**Bozak Draconian (1):** AC 15 (natural + studded leather), HP 30, Speed 30ft. Greatsword +5 (2d6+3). Burning Hands (3d6 fire, 15ft cone, DC 12 DEX half, 1/encounter). Lightning Bolt (2d10 lightning, 30ft line, DC 13 DEX half, 1/encounter). Death Throes: bones explode — 1d6 thunder damage in 10ft radius, DC 13 DEX to avoid.
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 1
The soldiers see you coming. Two of them form up — shields locked, longswords ready, the mechanical discipline of men who do this for pay. The third is still at the barn door, making sure the fire does its work. The Bozak turns its head slowly, as if the interruption is a minor inconvenience. It draws a greatsword with one hand — a gesture that should require two and does not.
The barn screams are louder now. The fire is winning.
**YOUR ATTACK — Choose a target:**
**Soldier (left)** / **Soldier (right)** / **Soldier at barn door** / **Bozak officer**

**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC = target's AC (Soldiers: 14, Bozak: 15)
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage.

**IF VAEL IS PRESENT:** 'The Bozak will cast first. Spread out.' You know to expect Burning Hands.
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one for each companion:**
**Aldric:** A) Flank (+1 next attack) | B) Shield Wall (+1 AC) | C) Charge the Bozak — Aggressive Strike (1d8+2, takes 1d4, draws Bozak attention)
**Sera:** A) Scout Shot — targets soldier at barn door (1d6 dmg) | B) Mark Bozak (+2 next attack vs Bozak) | C) Cover Fire (+1 AC)
**Mira:** A) Rush to barn — begins rescue (needs barn door cleared first) | B) Herbal Boost (+1 save vs fire) | C) Bandage (stabilize nearest wounded)
**Serath:** A) Intel (+2 one attack) | B) Circle behind — Dagger Strike on barn-door soldier (1d4+2) | C) Feint Bozak (Bozak -1 next attack)
**Lysander:** A) Combat Instruction (+2 attack) | B) Veteran Strike — engages nearest soldier (1d10+3) | C) Guard — positions to take Bozak's first spell
**Thessaly:** A) Encourage (+1 next roll) | B) Document (+1 ROSE after) | C) Distraction — calls to soldiers in Common: 'Solamnic column! Surrender!' (one soldier hesitates, skips attack)
**Vael:** A) Flanking Shot (1d6 dmg) | B) Honest Assessment (reveal Bozak HP) | C) Warning Shot — forces Bozak to use Burning Hands defensively (halves cone area)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):**
• (Paladin) Cast Bless: +1d4 to attacks and saves for up to 3 targets, Concentration, lasts entire combat. 1 spell slot.
• (Paladin) Divine Smite: Bonus action after hitting with a melee weapon. 2d8 radiant (+1d8 vs Fiends/Undead). Once per turn. 1 spell slot (or free 1/long rest at level 2+).
• (Paladin) Lay on Hands: Heal self or ally. Bonus action.
• (Paladin) Cure Wounds: 2d8+CHA heal. 1 spell slot. Replaces attack.
• (Fighter) Action Surge: Additional Action this round (except Magic). 1/short or long rest.
• (Fighter) Second Wind: 1d10+[level] HP. Bonus action. 2 uses/long rest, regain 1/short rest.
• (Any) Drink Potion of Healing: 2d4+2 HP. Bonus action.
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 1
The Bozak opens with fire. Its hand comes up, palm forward, and the air between you ignites — not flame exactly, but a cone of superheated air that smells of sulfur and hate.
**BURNING HANDS — DEX save DC 12:**
**SUCCESS:** Half damage. You roll clear.
**FAILURE:** 3d6 fire damage. The heat is extraordinary. Your tabard smoulders.
**IF VAEL WARNED YOU:** Advantage on the save (roll twice, take higher).
**IF LYSANDER CHOSE GUARD:** Lysander steps in front. He takes the damage instead. His face does not change. His armour steams.

**Soldier attack:** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 14
**FAILURE:** 1d8+3 slashing damage.
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 2
The barn-door soldier is the key. As long as he is there, Mira cannot reach the barn. Hanna's screams are getting weaker, which is worse than them being loud.
The Bozak is reloading — the Burning Hands cost it something, you can see it in the way the scales along its arm are darkened. It reaches for the greatsword. The spell was its opening gambit. Now comes the steel.
**ATHLETICS CHECK (optional, replaces attack):** Roll 1d20 + STR modifier | DC 14
Break the barn door open. If the barn-door soldier is dead or engaged, this succeeds automatically.
**SUCCESS:** The door breaks. Smoke pours out. Mira can enter (or you can).
**FAILURE:** The door holds. The hinges are warped by heat. One more round.
**YOUR ATTACK:** (if not breaking the door)
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC = target's AC
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage.
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one for each companion:** (same options)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):** (same options)
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 2
The Bozak engages in melee. The greatsword comes in a wide arc — too wide for indoors, perfect for the open yard. It has reach and strength and the particular arrogance of something that considers you a minor obstacle.
**DEFENSE ROLL (Bozak greatsword):** Roll 1d20 + DEX modifier | DC 15
**SUCCESS:** You duck the arc. The sword bites dirt.
**FAILURE:** 2d6+3 slashing damage. The greatsword is not subtle. It does not need to be.

**INVESTIGATION CHECK (free, if barn is open):** Roll 1d20 + INT modifier | DC 13
**SUCCESS:** Water source — a trough by the fence. Dump it on Hanna's burns. Stabilizes her without magic.
**FAILURE:** You do not see it. Magic healing or Mira's herbs are the only options.
NARRATIVE COMBAT — ROUND 3
**IF SERATH IS NOT PRESENT:** A horn sounds from the south road. Another Dragon Army patrol has heard the fighting. They will arrive in 3 rounds. End this fast.
**IF SERATH IS PRESENT:** 'We have time. The next patrol is south of the ridge. We are clear.' No reinforcements.
The Bozak is wounded but fighting. It is the last significant threat — the soldiers are dead, fleeing, or pinned. The farmyard smells of smoke and blood and the chemical stink of draconian. Behind you, the barn fire is being fought by whoever you sent — Mira with wet blankets, or your own hands, or nobody at all.
**SURVIVAL CHECK (optional):** Roll 1d20 + WIS modifier | DC 12
Track the patrol's approach. If Serath is not present, success gives you 1 extra round before reinforcements.
**IF SERATH IS PRESENT:** Automatic success. No need to roll.
**YOUR ATTACK — Finish the Bozak.**
**ROLL REQUIRED:** Attack roll | DC 15
Roll 1d20 + your attack bonus.
**ON HIT:** Roll damage.
**BOZAK DEATH THROES:** If the Bozak drops to 0 HP, its bones EXPLODE. 1d6 thunder damage, 10ft radius. **DEX save DC 13** or take full damage. *You were warned. Move.*
**COMPANION ACTION — Choose one for each companion:** (same options)
**ABILITY / ITEM USE (choose one, optional):** (same options)
ENEMY STRIKE — ROUND 3
The Bozak knows it is losing. It channels the last of its energy — Lightning Bolt, a 30ft line of blue-white electricity that cracks the air and leaves the taste of copper in your mouth.
**LIGHTNING BOLT — DEX save DC 13:**
**SUCCESS:** Half damage. You throw yourself sideways.
**FAILURE:** 2d10 lightning damage. The world goes white. Your muscles lock. The pain is immediate and total.
**IF VAEL IS PRESENT:** Advantage on the save. You knew it was coming.
AFTER THE FIGHT
The Bozak's bones detonate and the sound is like a thunderclap in a bottle — sharp, contained, final. The soldiers are dead or fled. The farmyard is quiet except for the fire, which is dying now, and the sound of someone crying inside the barn.
Hanna is burned badly. Her arms, her chest. She was shielding Darric and the fire found her. Darric has a dislocated shoulder and the cough of a man who breathed too much smoke. They are alive. Whether they stay that way depends on what you do in the next five minutes.
**MEDICINE CHECK — DARRIC:** Roll 1d20 + WIS modifier | DC 14
Reset the shoulder, treat smoke inhalation.
**SUCCESS:** Darric stabilizes. He can walk. He is in pain but functional.
**FAILURE:** The shoulder is worse than it looked. Mira (if present) can still treat him. Without Mira, he needs a sling and time.
**HEALING HANNA — REQUIRES MAGIC OR MIRA:**
• Paladin Cure Wounds (1 spell slot): Heals burns. Hanna's hands will work.
• Paladin Lay on Hands (10+ HP): Same result.
• Mira (if present) + herbs: Stabilizes. Burns heal slowly — Hanna's hands work but with scars.
• Greater Potion of Healing: Full recovery.
• Potion of Healing: Partial — stabilizes but burns remain serious.
• Bandage only: Stabilizes at 1 HP. Hanna lives but her hands may not fully recover.
*Record resources spent.*

**SEARCH THE FALLEN (Optional — moral choice):**
The soldiers are dead. The Bozak is bone fragments. The officer's satchel survived the explosion.

**Bozak officer's satchel:** No moral penalty — enemy intelligence. Contains: patrol routes, supply line maps, a letter in Draconic that Caeron will want to read. +1d6 stl.

**Soldier bodies:**
**A) Search them.** (-1 ROSE. Loot Roll below.)
**B) Leave them. You have done enough.** (+1 ROSE. No loot.)

**LOOT ROLL (if searched):** Roll 1d20.
1-5: Nothing. Dragon Army soldiers carry nothing personal. They were not allowed to.
6-10: 1d4 stl. Standard issue pay. 1 day rations (Dragon Army field pack).
11-14: 2d4 stl + mundane item (2 days rations, a signal whistle, a bedroll).
15-17: 3d4 stl + Potion of Healing (standard issue, one per squad). 2 days rations.
18-19: 4d4 stl + quality item (an officer's compass, worth 5 stl to the right buyer). 3 days rations.
20: 5d4 stl + RARE ITEM ROLL. 3 days rations.
**RARE ITEM ROLL:** Roll 1d20.
1–19: Quality mundane item (Dragon Army chain mail, AC 16, good condition but unmarked).
20: MAGIC ITEM — +1 Longsword. Solamnic make. Taken from a fallen knight. The Bozak was carrying it as a trophy. Returning it to a Solamnic armoury grants . Keeping it is not dishonourable — it was earned.

**FARMSTEAD CELLAR:** Investigation DC 13. (No moral penalty — searching for survivors/supplies.)
