Commander Serath is reviewing maps when you enter. He looks up — and for just a moment, before the mask settles, you see something raw in his eyes.
'Close the flap,' he says quietly.
You do. He sets down his quill and stands, military precision absent for once. He looks tired. Human.
'I am not supposed to do this.' His voice is barely above a whisper. 'Officers don't form attachments. It compromises command decisions. The Measure is very clear.'
He steps closer.

'But the Measure doesn't account for you. For the way I can't stop thinking about you. For the way my heart stops every time you're in danger.' His hand rises, hovers near your cheek. 'I've lost forty-three people under my command. I cannot lose you. Not because of the mission. Because of this.'
He waits, trembling, for permission.

choices.text: Close the distance. Let him know the feeling is mutual.
'Command decisions have to come first. For both of us.'
'I respect you too much to let you compromise your position.'

FLAME_CAERON.title: BENEATH THE MASK
prose: *Camp — Dusk*
You find Caeron alone, without his armor for once. He looks smaller. Younger. Real.

'I've been insufferable,' he says without preamble. 'I know. The arrogance, the posturing, the endless need to prove I'm better than everyone.' He laughs bitterly. 'It's exhausting to be around. I can only imagine what it's like from the outside.'
He turns to face you.
'But with you... I don't want to pretend. I'm tired of pretending.' His voice cracks. 'You see through me. You always have. And somehow you didn't leave.'
He reaches out, tentative, and takes your hand.
'I'm not good at this. At being vulnerable. At needing someone. But I need you. Whatever that means. However much it costs my pride to say it.'

choices.text: Pull him close. Show him vulnerability isn't weakness.
'I see you, Caeron. The real you. And I like what I see — as a friend.'
'This isn't something I can give you. I'm sorry.'

FLAME_SERA.title: IN THE SHADOWS
prose: *Camp Edge — Third Watch*
Sera appears beside you. She does that. But tonight she doesn't speak. She just stands there, close enough that your shoulders almost touch.
'I've killed a lot of people,' she says finally. 'Told you that. What I didn't tell you is that I've never let anyone close since. Can't afford attachments in my line of work.'
She turns. Moonlight catches the scar across her collarbone.

'Then there's you. And I don't know what to do with that.' Her voice is steady but her hands are shaking. 'I've been trying to convince myself it's just proximity. War makes people desperate for connection.'
She steps closer. Her eyes are silver in the darkness.
'But it's not desperation. It's something else. Something I haven't felt in a very long time. And it scares me more than anything I've faced in the dark.'

choices.text: Take her shaking hands in yours. Step into the shadows with her.
'Whatever this is, we don't have to name it yet. I'm not going anywhere.'
'The shadows have to stay shadows, Sera. I can't follow you there.'

FLAME_VAEL.title: CROSSED BLADES
prose: *Training Ground — Dawn*
Vael challenged you to a spar. She does that. But this time when she disarms you, she doesn't step back.
She stands there, breathing hard, her sword at your throat. Then she drops it.
'I can't do this anymore.' Her voice is rough. 'The competing. The pretending I don't feel what I feel. I've been fighting you because I don't know how to fight *for* you.'
She steps closer. The signet ring on its cord catches the sunrise.

'My whole life I've had to be stronger, faster, better. Never soft. Never vulnerable.' Her hand rises to touch your face. 'But with you I want to be both. Strong *and* soft. A warrior who also knows how to love.'
Her eyes meet yours.
'Spar's over. This is me, laying down my sword.'

choices.text: Pull her into a kiss. The competition is over — you both won.
'We make better rivals than lovers. But I'll always have your back.'
'Pick up your sword, Vael. This isn't what I want.'

FLAME_TARIEL.title: THE QUESTION ANSWERED
prose: *Camp — After Dinner*
Tariel has been watching you all evening. When the others drift away, she sits beside you.
'I figured it out,' she says. 'What you fight for. I've been studying you — professionally, at first. But then it became personal.'
She smiles — rare, unguarded.

'You fight for the person next to you. Always. Not abstractions. Not causes. People.' Her green eyes hold yours. 'That's why I'm sitting here. Because I realized — I want to be the person next to you.'
She reaches out, traces the line of your jaw with one finger.
'I ask questions for a living. But this is the first time I've been afraid of the answer. So I'm just going to say it: I care about you. More than professionally. More than I should.'

choices.text: Answer her question without words. Close the distance.
'The person next to me is a friend. A good one. Can that be enough?'
'Some questions are better left unanswered, Tariel.'

FLAME_LYSANDER.title: THE DISGRACED KNIGHT'S HEART
prose: *Camp — Evening*
Lysander is sharpening his blade when you join him. He doesn't look up.
'I lost everything once,' he says. 'My rank, my family, my name. I thought I'd lost the ability to feel anything except duty and regret.'
He sets down the whetstone.

'Then you walked into my life. And something woke up. Something I thought died when they burned my tabard.' He finally meets your eyes. 'I'm not a young man. I've made mistakes that haunt me. I have nothing to offer except a battered sword and whatever's left of my heart.'
He stands, massive and weathered and somehow vulnerable.
'But that's yours, if you want it. All of it. Whatever I am, whatever I have left — it's yours.'

choices.text: Take his face in your hands. 'I want all of it.'
'What you offer is worth more than you know. But I can only offer friendship.'
'Your heart deserves someone who can give theirs fully. I can't.'

JEALOUSY_EVENT.title: CROSSED HEARTS
prose: *Camp — Tension*
You feel eyes on you. Not one pair — two.
The connection you've built with multiple companions has not gone unnoticed. By them. By the others. By yourself.
The weight of unspoken words hangs in the air. Stolen glances. Questions not asked. You cannot have all of this forever. Something will have to give.
But not tonight. Tonight, you carry the weight of hearts that trust you, and wonder if you deserve that trust.

boxes.text:
**ROMANTIC TENSION**
You are pursuing romances with multiple companions. Eventually, you will need to make a choice — or find a way to honor all of them honestly.

choices.text: This is something to face later. For now, the mission comes first.
Maybe honesty is the answer. Talk to them. Both of them.

OATH_ALDRIC.title: THE KNIGHT'S VOW
prose: *Dawn — The Edge of Camp*
Aldric finds you at first light. He's dressed in his best armor — cleaned, polished, the tabard fresh. This is deliberate.
'I've been thinking about oaths,' he says. 'The Oath of the Crown. The Oath of the Sword. All the vows I've made to the Order, to Solamnia, to the Measure.'
He kneels.

'But there's one oath I haven't made. The most important one.' He takes your hand, presses it to his heart. 'I swear — by Paladine, by Kiri-Jolith, by every star in the sky — that my sword is yours. My shield is yours. My heart is yours. Not because duty demands it. Because *I* demand it of myself.'
His eyes shine with unshed tears.
'Will you accept my oath? Will you let me stand beside you, not as companion or ally, but as... as what we both know we are?'

choices.text: Kneel beside him. 'I accept. And I make the same vow to you.'
'Rise, Aldric. We don't need oaths. What we have is enough.'
'I can't accept this. Not with what's coming. I'm sorry.'

OATH_THESSALY.title: THE SCHOLAR'S TRUTH
prose: *Starlight — Away from Camp*
Thessaly has led you to a hilltop overlooking the plains. The stars are impossibly bright.
'I've spent my whole life searching for answers,' she says. 'Who I am. Where I come from. What happened to my mother. I thought if I found enough answers, I'd finally feel complete.'
She turns to you.
'But I was wrong. The answers aren't in books or archives or the past. The answer is standing right in front of me.'
She takes both your hands.
'I love you. Not because you complete me — I complete myself. But because you make me *better*. Braver. More honest. More willing to open that locket and face what's inside.'
Her voice trembles.
'I want to build a future with you. Whatever that looks like. Wherever it takes us.'

choices.text: 'Then let's build it. Together. Starting now.'
'I love having you in my life. But I'm not ready for promises.'
'You deserve someone who can give you everything. I can't.'

OATH_MIRA.title: THE HEALER'S PROMISE
prose: *A Quiet Glade — Dusk*
Mira has found a peaceful place away from the war. Wildflowers grow here, untouched by the Dragon Armies. She picks one, holds it gently.
'I've healed so many people,' she says. 'But I've never let anyone heal me. Never let anyone close enough to see the wounds I carry.'
She tucks the flower behind your ear.
'Until you. You see me — really see me — and you don't look away. Not from the fear, not from the exhaustion, not from the times I've failed.'
She takes your hands, presses them to her heart.
'I want to spend whatever time we have left healing *together*. Not because duty demands it. Because love demands it. Because my heart demands it.'
Her dark eyes hold yours.
'Will you let me love you? Not as a healer. As... as yours?'

choices.text: 'I'm already yours. I have been for longer than you know.'
'What we have is precious. But I'm not ready to name it.'
'You deserve a peace I can't give you. I'm sorry.'

POLYAMORY_CHOICE.title: HONEST HEARTS
prose: *Camp — After Dark*
They're both here. They know. You know they know.
The silence stretches like a blade.
'We've talked,' one says. 'About you. About... this.' The other nods. 'We're not asking you to choose — not yet. But we need to know: is this something you're willing to be honest about?'
The question hangs in the air. Multiple hearts. One truth. There are no easy answers here.
'Some people can love more than one person,' one says quietly. 'Honestly. Openly. Without betrayal. Is that what this is? Or is this you not being able to choose?'
The distinction matters. To them. To you.

boxes.text:
**CROSSROADS**
This is a choice about honesty, not about romance. How you answer will shape relationships going forward.

choices.text: 'I love you both. Differently, but truly. If you can accept that...'
'I need time. This is too much to decide right now.'
'You're right. I need to choose. Give me until the war ends.'

OATH_SERATH.title: THE COMMANDER'S OATH
prose: *Command Tent — First Light*
Serath has been awake all night. Maps, reports, casualty lists. The weight of command etched into every line of his face. When he sees you enter, something shifts.
He stands. Slowly. Like a man setting down a burden he's carried too long.
'I've spent my career telling myself that duty comes first. That personal attachments are liabilities. That a good commander doesn't need... this.' He gestures between you.
He crosses the tent. Stops in front of you.

'I was wrong. I've been wrong about a lot of things. But not about you.' His voice is steady now — the voice he uses to give orders that will be obeyed. 'I love you. Not in spite of being a commander. Because of it. Because you make me want to be the kind of leader worth following.'
He takes both your hands.
'When this war ends — if we survive — I want to build something with you. A future. Whatever that looks like.'

choices.text: 'Then we build it together. Starting now.'
'I care for you. But I can't promise what comes after.'
'Duty has to come first. For both of us. I'm sorry.'

OATH_CAERON.title: THE LORD'S SURRENDER
prose: *Away from Camp — Starlight*
Caeron has led you to a hilltop. The mask is gone. All of it. He looks younger without it. Frightened. Real.
'I've spent my whole life building walls,' he says. 'Pride instead of connection. Arrogance instead of vulnerability. I told myself it was strength. It wasn't. It was cowardice.'
He turns to face you.
'You tore those walls down. I didn't even notice it happening. One day I was Lord Caeron Ashvale, untouchable and alone. The next I was just... a man who couldn't stop thinking about you.'
His voice breaks.
'I don't deserve you. I know that. But I'm asking anyway. I'm asking you to take what's left of me — the real parts, the broken parts — and let me love you with all of it.'
For the first time since you've known him, Lord Caeron Ashvale looks truly humble.

choices.text: 'I'll take all of it. The pride and the fear. The lord and the man.'
'What we have doesn't need promises. Let's see where it goes.'
'You deserve someone who can love the lord. I can only see the man.'

OATH_SERA.title: OUT OF SHADOW
prose: *Camp Edge — Moonrise*
Sera materializes beside you. She's been doing that for months. But tonight she doesn't disappear again.
'I've lived in shadows my whole life,' she says. 'It's safer there. Easier. No one can hurt you if no one can see you.'
She steps into the moonlight. Deliberately. Visibly.
'But I'm tired of hiding. Tired of watching you from the dark and pretending I don't feel what I feel.' Her silver-streaked hair catches the light. Her scar is visible. Everything is visible.
'I want to stand in the light with you. I want people to see us together and know. I want to stop being a ghost and start being... yours.'
She's trembling. This is harder for her than any fight.
'Is that something you want too?'

choices.text: Step into the moonlight with her. 'I want everyone to see.'
'I care for you. But I'm not ready to be seen. Not yet.'
'The shadows suit us both. I'm sorry, Sera.'

OATH_VAEL.title: THE RING
prose: *Training Ground — First Light*
Vael is waiting for you. Not training. Just waiting. The signet ring is in her hand.
'My mother earned this,' she says. 'Died for it. The Order that wouldn't accept her gave it to me like a consolation prize.'
She holds it out.
'But I don't want it anymore. I don't want to carry her legacy alone. I don't want to prove myself to people who already decided I'm not enough.'
She presses the ring into your palm.
'I want to build something new. With you. Not inherited honour — earned honour. Not fighting to be accepted — fighting because we choose to. Together.'
Her callused hands close around yours.
'Keep the ring. Or give it back. Either way, I'm choosing you.'

choices.text: Close your hands around hers and the ring. 'Then we carry it together.'
'Keep the ring. Your mother earned it. But I'll stand beside you.'
'You shouldn't give this up. Not for me. Not for anyone.'

OATH_TARIEL.title: THE ANSWER
prose: *A Quiet Place — Late Night*
Tariel has been asking questions since the day you met. Tonight, she has an answer.
'I know what you fight for,' she says. 'I figured that out months ago. What I didn't know — what I couldn't figure out — was what I fight for.'
She moves closer.
'I'm a spy. An analyst. A collector of secrets. I've never had anything of my own. Just borrowed truths, borrowed identities, borrowed lives.'
Her green eyes hold yours.
'But you're not borrowed. You're real. And when I'm with you, I'm real too.' Her voice softens. 'I want to stop collecting secrets and start keeping one. A real one. Ours.'
She takes your hand.
'I love you. That's my answer. What's yours?'

choices.text: 'My answer is yes. To all of it. To you.'
'I don't have an answer yet. But I want to find it with you.'
'Some questions are better left unanswered. This is one of them.'

OATH_LYSANDER.title: THE DISGRACED KNIGHT'S VOW
prose: *Camp — By the Fire*
Lysander has been sharpening his blade for an hour. He hasn't made a sound. Finally, he sets it down.
'I lost my honour once,' he says. 'Burned my own tabard rather than let them take it. Spent years thinking that was the end of my story.'
He stands.

'But honour isn't something they can take. It's something you carry. Something you choose, every day, with every action.' He crosses to you. 'And the most honourable thing I can do — the bravest, the truest — is this.'
He kneels. A knight's kneel. The one he hasn't used in years.
'I love you. Not as a disgraced knight. Not as a broken man. As whatever I am now — whatever you've made me. I'm offering you everything I have left. My sword. My heart. My oath.'
His weathered face is steady. Certain.
'Will you accept it?'

choices.text: Kneel with him. 'I accept. And I give you mine in return.'
'Rise, Lysander. You don't need to kneel. Not to me.'
'Your honour belongs to you. I can't be part of that oath.'

EPILOGUE_ROMANCE_ALDRIC.title: EPILOGUE — ALDRIC
prose: *One Year Later*
The war changed everything. The war changed nothing.
Aldric still trains at dawn. Still makes that same joke about his scar. Still looks at you like you're the only person in the room, even when the room is full of knights.
But now there's a ring on his finger. And one on yours.

You build a life together in the years that follow. Not perfect — nothing is perfect after a war. But real. He teaches young squires the forms he learned from his father. You teach them the forms you learned from him.
Sometimes, late at night, he still talks about the war. The price of doing the right thing. You listen. You always listen.
'I used to be afraid I'd become my father,' he says once. 'Gone before I was ready. Leaving you alone.'
'You're not him,' you say. 'You came home.'
He holds you tighter.
'Every time.'

choices.text: Continue.

EPILOGUE_ROMANCE_THESSALY.title: EPILOGUE — THESSALY
prose: *Three Years Later*

The locket sits on her writing desk, open now. She looks at it sometimes — her mother's face, preserved in miniature. She doesn't cry anymore. The grief has softened into something else. Memory. Gratitude.
You found her mother's village. What was left of it. There was a grave, tended by an old woman who remembered. Thessaly wept for three days. Then she wrote the story down.
She's written many stories since. The war. The march. The people who didn't come home. Her books sit in libraries across Solamnia now.
But her favorite story is the one she's still writing. The one about a copper-haired scholar who learned to open doors she'd kept locked. About a knight who stayed.
'Will you read it?' she asks, handing you pages still wet with ink.
'Always.'
Her smile is the same one she gave you the first time. Sharp. Guarded. Slowly opening.
Some lockets take years to unlock.

choices.text: Continue.

EPILOGUE_ROMANCE_MIRA.title: EPILOGUE — MIRA
prose: *Five Years Later*
The clinic in Oakheart Grove has grown. Refugees from the war, settlers building new lives, children who never knew the Dragon Armies. Mira heals them all.
But she doesn't do it alone anymore.
You learned the herbs. The poultices. The quiet words that help when medicine can't. Not a healer — never that — but a partner. Someone to hold the frightened hands while she works.
At night, in your small cottage at the edge of the grove, she rests her head on your shoulder and tells you about the ones she couldn't save. The old weight. The constant companion.
'It's lighter now,' she says once. 'Carrying it with you.'

There are children in the village who call you both by name. There are grandmothers who bless your union. There is a garden where she grows the herbs and you grow the vegetables and sometimes you just sit together and watch things bloom.
The war took so much.
This, it could not take.

choices.text: Continue.

EPILOGUE_HONOUR_HIGH.title: EPILOGUE — THE EXEMPLARY KNIGHT
prose: *The Ceremony — High Clerist's Tower*
They speak your name with reverence now.
The war is over. The Dragon Armies scattered. And when the Grand Master calls for knights whose honour never wavered, yours is the first name spoken.
You kneel in the great hall where Huma himself once knelt. The blade that touches your shoulder is ancient, blessed, heavy with the weight of every knight who came before.
'Rise, Knight of the Rose.'
The Order of the Rose. The highest calling. Wisdom, justice, sacrifice — the virtues you chose when easier paths beckoned.
Your companions are there. The ones who survived. They watched you make the hard choices. Spare the enemy. Show mercy. Put honour above expedience. They know what it cost.
But they also know what it built.
The world is watching a knight who proved the Measure still matters. Who showed that honour isn't a burden — it's a gift.
Est Sularus oth Mithas.
My honour is my life.

choices.text: Continue.

EPILOGUE_HONOUR_LOW.title: EPILOGUE — THE PRAGMATIST
prose: *The Road — Alone*
The war is over. You survived. That's more than many can say.
The Order... has opinions about your methods. The prisoner you questioned. The supplies you took. The lines you crossed when crossing them seemed necessary.
You're not expelled. Not exactly. But you're not invited to the ceremonies either. Not praised in the great halls. Not held up as an example.
'A knight should be better than the times demand,' the Grand Master said. 'You were exactly as good as they demanded. No more.'
There's truth in that. More truth than you'd like.
The road stretches ahead. New battles. New choices. Maybe this time you'll find the path you missed before. Maybe this time honour won't feel like a luxury you can't afford.
Or maybe pragmatism is its own kind of honour. The honour of surviving. The honour of winning.
The debate continues. In your head. In your heart.
The road doesn't care. The road just goes on.

choices.text: Continue.

EPILOGUE_COMPANION_DEATHS.title: EPILOGUE — THE FALLEN
prose: *The Memorial — Knight's Spur*
The names are carved into stone. Too many names.
You visit when you can. Bring flowers. Stand in silence. Remember faces that will never age now, voices that will never speak again.
The war took them. One by one. In battles you fought together, in moments you couldn't prevent. Each loss carved something out of you that will never grow back.
But you survived. You carry their stories now — the only one left who knows how they laughed, how they fought, how they loved. The last witness to lives that mattered.
'They didn't die for nothing,' someone says at the memorial. 'They died for Solamnia.'
You don't correct them. But you know the truth.
They died for you. For the person beside them. For one more dawn, one more chance, one more moment of holding the line.
Abstract causes don't bleed. People do.
You remember. You always will.

choices.text: Continue.

EPILOGUE_END.title: THE END
prose: *The story continues...*
This is not the end. It is never the end.
The Dragon Armies will regroup. New threats will rise. The darkness that Takhisis brought to Krynn will linger in shadows and hearts for generations.
But so will the light.
You have proven that one person can make a difference. That choices matter. That honour — however you define it — is worth fighting for.
The Companions of the Lance will need allies in the battles to come. Knights whose oaths mean something. Warriors who know the cost of war and pay it anyway.
Perhaps, when the call comes, you will answer again.
Until then: rest. Heal. Remember.
And keep the faith.
**THE END**
*Thank you for playing The Knight's Oath.*
